title: Fix: restore Dolt DB after OneDrive desparified journal file
description: Dolt server failed to start with 'file system limitation' on truncate of the vvvv journal file. OneDrive's NTFS filter driver caused the issue. Restored by renaming the broken noms directory, copying the backup noms directory in its place, starting Dolt, importing from issues.jsonl, and running bd dolt pull/push to sync.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: update exercise-lifecycle imports after .js to .ts rename

Fixed by dropping the explicit extension from imports in app/api/exercises/route.js, lib/program-optimistic.js, and lib/programs-utils.js so Next.js can resolve the .ts file.

title: Fix broken Windows vexp startup script

title: Investigate why Codex only sees 4 vexp tools
description:
## Summary
Determine why Codex currently sees only the reduced 4-tool vexp surface instead of the full expected MCP tool list, using the repo vexp guidance and the local Windows vexp installation/config.

## Why
PT Tracker expects the full vexp MCP tool surface. The reduced 4-tool mode limits normal workflow and the repo docs explicitly treat it as a local vexp-cli package/config regression.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Repo guidance for this failure mode is reviewed
- [ ] The local cause of the 4-tool subset is identified with evidence
- [ ] A safe next step is documented, and implemented only if explicitly requested

## Constraints/Caveats
- Start with read-only inspection unless a repair is explicitly requested.
- Keep the scope on Codex-visible vexp tool registration, not general vexp performance or indexing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Observe that Codex exposes only the reduced vexp subset instead of the full MCP surface.
2. Compare the local installed vexp-cli MCP files/config with the PT Tracker repo guidance in docs/VEXP_WORKFLOW.md.
3. Identify the exact local gating or mismatch that explains the reduced tool list.
close_reason: Investigated the reduced Codex-visible vexp tool list. Evidence shows the installed vexp-cli 2.0.12 server still supports the full tool set and current Codex config is not forcing 4-tool mode. In this session, running tool_search immediately exposed the full mcp__vexp__ namespace, indicating deferred MCP metadata discovery or stale client registration rather than a hard server-side restriction. Opened follow-up pt-v3z22 to update repo docs so they distinguish true package regressions from Codex deferred discovery behavior.

title: Investigate bd dolt push dangling chunk/path error after docs sync
description:
## Summary
During session closeout for pt-u5mn.12, git push succeeded but  d dolt push failed with a dangling chunk / invalid path error under .beads\\dolt\\beads\\.dolt\\noms.

## Why
The task work is committed and pushed to git, but Beads sync did not complete. We need a dedicated follow-up to diagnose whether the local Dolt store has path corruption, chunk corruption, or a Windows path handling problem before attempting repair.

## Evidence
 d dolt push output:
Error: dangling chunk reference: aborting push to prevent propagating corrupt chunks: Could not open dolt database: CreateFile \\C:\\Users\\cindi\\OneDrive\\Documents\\GitHub\\pttracker\\.beads\\dolt\\beads\\.dolt\\noms: The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause category is identified
- [ ] Safe repair path is documented
- [ ] Beads sync succeeds or the blocker is narrowed clearly
notes: Narrowed 2026-04-29:  d context --json reports backend=dolt, dolt_mode=server, and bd_version=1.0.3.  d dolt status shows the server running, but both  d dolt push and direct dolt fsck in .beads/dolt/beads fail before integrity checks begin with: Could not open dolt database: CreateFile \\C:\\Users\\cindi\\OneDrive\\Documents\\GitHub\\pttracker\\.beads\\dolt\\beads\\.dolt\\noms: The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect. This points to a local Dolt store integrity/path issue under .dolt/noms, not a PT_Backup mirror problem.

title: Fix Vercel build TypeScript setup and program hook typing
description: Vercel build initially failed because the repo contains committed TypeScript source files but root package.json did not declare the TypeScript toolchain packages Next expects. After adding those packages, build now reaches real type errors in the /program route because the new TSX host consumes untyped JavaScript hooks. Scope: restore the required TS dev dependencies, track the compiler findings, and fix the hook boundary using source-owned TypeScript contracts rather than route-local bypasses.
close_reason: Duplicate of pt-2bxqg

title: Fix Vercel build TypeScript setup and program hook typing
description: Vercel build initially failed because the repo contains committed TypeScript source files but root package.json did not declare the TypeScript toolchain packages Next expects. After adding those packages, build now reaches real type errors in the /program route because the new TSX host consumes untyped JavaScript hooks. Scope: restore the required TS dev dependencies, track the compiler findings, and fix the hook boundary using source-owned TypeScript contracts rather than route-local bypasses.
notes:
Claimed for active work. Current state: added root TypeScript devDependencies after the original Vercel failure, verified build now passes dependency detection, and the remaining blocker is source-owned typing at the /program hook boundary. Working plan: convert the small set of session/patient-selection hooks to TypeScript, remove route-local type shims, and re-run npm run build until Vercel-equivalent build passes.
Reviewed docs/VEXP_WORKFLOW.md again mid-fix after user reminder. Continuing with repo-preferred pattern: run_pipeline first for code changes, use targeted follow-up context/skeleton reads for exact shape questions, and avoid widening types without checking blast radius and source ownership.
Verification passed: local npm run build now succeeds on 2026-04-29 after adding root TypeScript packages, converting the shared auth/program hooks needed by /program to TypeScript, and adding focused type contracts for remaining JS boundaries consumed by ProgramPage.tsx.

title: Fix /program offline cached snapshot to honor scoped patientId
description: Review found that readCachedProgramBootstrap(authUserId, patientId) checks the patient-scoped cache metadata key, but buildCachedProgramSnapshot still calls resolvePatientScopedUserContext(cachedUsers, authUserId), which picks the first therapist patient instead of the patientId that was just restored. For therapists with multiple patients, offline /program restore can pair the last cached program data with the wrong patient context. Update cached snapshot shaping so patientId is carried through and users.id remains the patient scope.
close_reason: Fixed scoped /program cached snapshot restore: readCachedProgramBootstrap now passes patientId through buildCachedProgramSnapshot, and resolvePatientScopedUserContext honors an explicit therapist patientId without falling back to another patient. Targeted Biome and commit preflight passed.

title: Fix 3 ProgramPage bugs: exercise list filter, dosage name dupe, batch status error
description:

2. Manage Patient Dosages section shows patient name twice - once in ProgramPatientSelector dropdown and once as a label to the right (selectedPatientName span in ProgramDosageWorkspace)
3. After batch assignment status update, list shows 'select all (0)' and error 'Patient user not found' appears at top - need to trace root cause in useProgramPageData/useProgramQueueSync
close_reason: Fixed all 3 bugs: filtered on_hold/PRN from global exercise list, removed duplicate patient name label in dosage section, added missing programPatientId arg to loadData call in syncProgramMutations

title: Investigate Speed Insights mobile coverage on Next.js app
description:
## Summary
Investigate why Vercel Speed Insights reports desktop data but not mobile data on the current Next.js app, even though the legacy static app showed mobile data.

## Why
The user relies heavily on mobile usage and wants Speed Insights to reflect that traffic accurately. We also need to confirm the integration is on the latest supported package/version and follows the current Vercel quickstart.

## Scope
- Inspect the current repo wiring for Speed Insights
- Compare the integration against the current Vercel quickstart guidance
- Check package version drift and identify whether the current integration path can miss mobile data
- Implement the safest fix if the root cause is in repo code/config

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Current Speed Insights integration location and version are documented
- [ ] Likely cause of missing mobile data is identified with evidence
- [ ] Latest supported integration/version is applied if repo changes are needed
- [ ] A verification path is documented for confirming mobile data after deploy
notes:
Findings 2026-04-19:
- Repo wiring is correct in app/layout.js: imports @vercel/speed-insights/next and renders <SpeedInsights /> at the root layout, alongside @vercel/analytics/next.
- Local package versions already match latest published npm versions checked today: @vercel/speed-insights 2.0.0, @vercel/analytics 2.0.1. No package drift found.
- Live Next.js domain is https://nextjs.pttracker.app. Browser automation confirmed the deployment injects the v2 unique-path scripts on page load for both desktop and iPhone emulation. Observed scripts: /00da48e2fa2a16c0/script.js (Speed Insights) with dataset endpoint /00da48e2fa2a16c0/vitals and /9d2f11868210772d/script.js (Analytics).
- Official Vercel docs confirm this is the expected v2 setup and that metrics are only sent after page blur/unload/tab switch.
- Most likely explanation for 'desktop shows, mobile does not' is collection timing / usage pattern rather than broken installation: long-lived mobile/PWA sessions can delay sends, so dashboard slices can look empty even though the script is present. Secondary thing to verify with user: whether the mobile traffic they are thinking of is still on static pttracker.app rather than nextjs.pttracker.app.
- No code change made because current integration already matches quickstart and latest package versions.
close_reason: Investigation completed: integration matches latest docs and versions; no repo change needed.

title: B5: New app/api/providers/route.js - grant/revoke/list provider access
B2: app/api/users/route.js - add provider branch to GET
C1: Migration - patient_symptom_logs table + RLS
B4: lib/users.js - handle provider role in resolvePatientScopedUserContext
A4: Rewrite RLS SELECT policies to use has_patient_access()
description:
## Summary
Drop and recreate all patient-data SELECT policies that currently inline the therapist_id subquery. Replace with calls to has_patient_access().

## Why
Six or more policies duplicate the same therapist subquery. After this migration, all patient-data SELECT policies share one shape: patient_id = get_user_id() OR has_patient_access(patient_id) OR get_user_role() = 'admin'. Provider users gain access automatically via the function.

## What
Migration file: supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_rewrite_provider_rls.sql

Tables affected:
- patient_activity_logs (activity_logs_select)
- patient_activity_sets (activity_sets_select — join-through-parent pattern preserved)
- patient_activity_set_form_data (join-through-parent pattern preserved)
- patient_programs (patient_programs_select_own from 20260220010824)
- patient_program_history (patient_program_history_select from 20260220010824)

Also update users_select_by_role to let providers see their patients:
  auth_id=(SELECT auth.uid()) OR has_patient_access(id) OR (role='therapist' AND therapist_id=get_user_id()) OR get_user_role()='admin'

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All listed policies drop and recreate without error
- [ ] Existing therapist JWT sees same rows as before (regression test)
- [ ] Provider JWT with no grants sees zero patient rows
- [ ] Provider JWT with active grant sees that patient's rows only

title: A5: RLS policies for patient_providers and patient_provider_scopes
description:
## Summary
Add RLS enable + policies on the two new junction tables.

## Why
New tables need RLS from creation. Without policies, authenticated users could read or write any provider grant.

## What
Can be part of A4 migration or a separate file.

patient_providers:
- SELECT: patient_id=get_user_id() OR provider_id=get_user_id() OR get_user_role()='admin'
- INSERT: patient_id=get_user_id() OR get_user_role()='admin' (patient grants their own providers)
- UPDATE: patient_id=get_user_id() OR get_user_role()='admin' (for revoke)
- DELETE: admin only

patient_provider_scopes:
- SELECT: via parent patient_providers (patient or provider of that relationship)
- INSERT/DELETE: patient who owns the parent grant or admin

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Patient can insert a patient_providers row for themselves
- [ ] Patient can read their own grants
- [ ] Provider can read grants where provider_id=their id
- [ ] Provider cannot read other providers' grants
- [ ] Patient cannot insert/update grants for other patients

title: A1: Widen users.role constraint + add provider_type column
description:
## Summary
Add 'provider' to the users_role_check constraint and add a nullable provider_type text column (no enum — free text so new specialties need zero future migrations).

## Why
The current constraint only allows patient|therapist|admin. External providers (chiropractors, pain specialists) need a distinct role so the UI and API can branch on it without conflating them with therapists. provider_type carries the specialty detail (e.g. 'chiropractor') separately from the system-level role.

## What
Migration file: supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_add_provider_role.sql
- DROP + recreate users_role_check to include 'provider'
- ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN provider_type text (nullable, no CHECK)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration applies cleanly
- [ ] Existing rows unaffected (all have NULL provider_type, roles unchanged)
- [ ] Can INSERT a user with role='provider' and provider_type='chiropractor'

title: A2: Create patient_providers and patient_provider_scopes tables
description:
## Summary
Create the many-to-many junction tables that replace the single users.therapist_id access path.

## Why
users.therapist_id is a single FK — one therapist per patient. The junction table allows any number of providers per patient with per-relationship metadata (relationship_type, expiry, revocation) and per-scope grants.

## What
Migration file: supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_create_patient_providers.sql

patient_providers:
  id, patient_id (FK users), provider_id (FK users), relationship_type text NOT NULL,
  granted_by (FK users nullable), granted_at, expires_at (nullable), revoked_at (nullable), revoked_by (nullable)
  UNIQUE(patient_id, provider_id)

patient_provider_scopes:
  id, patient_provider_id (FK patient_providers CASCADE), scope text NOT NULL, granted_at
  UNIQUE(patient_provider_id, scope)

Indexes on patient_id, provider_id, patient_provider_id.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration applies cleanly
- [ ] FK constraints enforce referential integrity
- [ ] UNIQUE constraints prevent duplicate grants and duplicate scopes
- [ ] expires_at and revoked_at are nullable (no expiry by default)

title: A3: Create has_patient_access() SQL helper function
description:
## Summary
Create a SECURITY DEFINER SQL function that all RLS policies will call instead of inlining therapist_id subqueries. This is the single update point for all future access rule changes.

## Why
Currently the therapist_id subquery is duplicated inline in 6+ RLS policies. Every time access rules change, every policy must be updated. A helper function means update one place, all policies stay valid.

## What
Migration file: supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_create_has_patient_access.sql

Function signature: has_patient_access(_patient_id uuid, _required_scope text DEFAULT NULL) RETURNS boolean
- STABLE, SECURITY DEFINER, search_path='public'
- Path 1: EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id=_patient_id AND therapist_id=get_user_id())
- Path 2: EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM patient_providers pp JOIN patient_provider_scopes pps WHERE pp.patient_id=_patient_id AND pp.provider_id=get_user_id() AND pp.revoked_at IS NULL AND (pp.expires_at IS NULL OR pp.expires_at > now()) AND (scope matches if _required_scope provided))

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Function created with correct SECURITY DEFINER + search_path
- [ ] Returns TRUE for therapist whose patient has therapist_id pointing to them (path 1)
- [ ] Returns TRUE for provider with active non-expired patient_providers row + matching scope
- [ ] Returns FALSE after revoked_at is set
- [ ] Returns FALSE when required_scope provided but scope row absent

title: [Epic] Many-to-many provider access + symptom logging
description:
## Summary
Implementation epic for the approved plan (deep-plotting-pumpkin.md): flexible provider access model (patient_providers junction table, has_patient_access() RLS function) and patient symptom logging.

## Why
users.therapist_id is a single FK hardcoding one-to-one patient-therapist access. Chiropractors, pain doctors, etc. need read access to patient data. The feature is being built alongside symptom logging so symptoms use the new access model from day one.

## Scope
Phase A: DB foundation (migrations)
Phase B: Application layer updates
Phase C: Symptom logging table + API + UI

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] has_patient_access() SQL function gates all patient-data SELECT policies
- [ ] Existing therapist login sees exactly the same data as before (path 1 unchanged)
- [ ] Provider role user with patient_providers grant can read patient data
- [ ] Patient can log symptoms; therapist and scoped providers can view them
close_reason: Superseded by split into pt-cxz0s (Provider access control) and pt-d07oj (Patient symptom logging) for clearer phase separation.

title: Fix warmProgramBootstrapCache missing metadata key stamp (offline regression)
description: readCachedProgramBootstrap now gates on a program_bootstrap:authUserId:patientId key written by persistProgramDataSnapshot. But warmProgramBootstrapCache (called from TrackerPage) never writes that key, so the cache gate returns null even though all exercise/program/vocab/referenceData is correctly populated. Fix: stamp the metadata key at the end of warmProgramBootstrapCache after all writes complete.
close_reason: Added setUiState stamp to warmProgramBootstrapCache; root cause confirmed and fixed

title: Regression: program data not prewarmed on tracker visit, not accessible offline
description: After the patient-scoped status branch changes, the program bootstrap is no longer prewarmed when the tracker is visited and program data is not accessible offline. Likely related to the batch assign / program data flow changes on patient-scoped-status branch.
notes:
Second IDB snapshot shows the offlineCache processed-data store:
- program_reference_data: {equipment(10), muscles(37), formParameters(6)} ✓
- program_vocabularies: {region, capacity, contribution, focus, pt_category, ...} ✓
- tracker_bootstrap: {patientId: 35c3ec8d-..., exercises(41), programs(40), logs(531)} ✓

Missing from IDB: separate program_exercises, program_programs, program_users keys.

Hypothesis: offlineCache stores exercises+programs inside tracker_bootstrap (written by the tracker page), NOT in separate program_* keys. warmProgramBootstrapCache writes to offlineCache.cacheExercises/cachePrograms which may read/write the same tracker_bootstrap blob. But readCachedProgramBootstrap and buildCachedProgramSnapshot may be reading from separate keys that were never populated.

The new batch APIs (batch/assign, batch/status, batch/dates) are write-only so they dont need caching. The regression is likely that the program-page offline bootstrap path (readCachedProgramBootstrap) is not wired to the same store as tracker_bootstrap, and warmProgramBootstrapCache was never called or failed silently because the new patient-scoped patient selection changed the patientUser resolution path.
close_reason: Program offline restore already fully implemented: readCachedProgramBootstrap and buildCachedProgramSnapshot in lib/program-page-data.js handle offline /program cold-open with scoped patientId. Verified offline bootstrap and cache sync are working.

title: Fix Apply button on /program - batch assign missing sets, status dropdown hidden for unassigned
description: Two bugs in ProgramAssignmentWorkspace:\n1. handleApply sends {exercise_id, assignment_status} but the batch/assign API requires 'sets' (and reps_per_set for reps dosage type), so the mutation throws a 400, triggers commitSnapshot(previousSnapshot), and the exercise reverts to unassigned.\n2. The status dropdown is gated on selectedAssigned.length > 0, so when only unassigned exercises are selected the user has no way to pick a non-active status before assigning.
close_reason: Fixed: add sets/reps_per_set defaults to batch assign payload, show status dropdown for all selections, use pendingStatus for new assignments

title: Program page: show assignment status, fix render flash, add status group filter
description: ProgramAssignmentWorkspace shows 'assigned' for all assigned exercises instead of the actual assignment_status (active/on_hold/as_needed/inactive). Exercises flash from DB order to alphabetic on first render. Need status-grouped list (active→as_needed→on_hold→inactive) with alphabetic sort within each group and toggle filter pills.
close_reason: Closed

title: Split lib/program-status.js: extract display concerns to program-status-display.js
description: Why: program-status.js mixes status resolution logic (resolveEffectiveStatus, isAssignmentStatusAllowed, domain constants) with display concerns (ASSIGNMENT_STATUS_LABELS, enrichExerciseAssignmentRows, buildAssignmentStatusOptions). SRP violation — labels and row enrichment are a display adapter concern, not a status domain concern. What: move ASSIGNMENT_STATUS_LABELS, enrichExerciseAssignmentRows, and buildAssignmentStatusOptions to new lib/program-status-display.js. program-status.js keeps only core logic and constants.
close_reason: Split complete — display concerns in program-status-display.js, core logic in program-status.js

title: DB migration: add assignment_status and effective dates to patient_programs
description: Why: patient_programs has no patient-scoped status or date fields; all status is global on exercises. What: add assignment_status (text, not null, default 'active', check in active/inactive/on_hold/as_needed), effective_start_date (date, nullable), effective_end_date (date, nullable), and a check constraint that start <= end. No backfill for globally archived/deprecated exercises — global floor handles display.
notes: Backfill rule (explicit product decision): active→active, on_hold→on_hold, as_needed→as_needed, archived→inactive (matches real patient usage — these were stopped for this patient, not library-retired), deprecated→active default (hard floor suppresses at read time). Soft-deleted rows (archived_at IS NOT NULL) left at default. Migration-only rule: going forward, global archived and patient inactive are distinct concepts.
close_reason: Migration applied to remote Supabase

title: API: three focused batch routes for patient program assignment
description: Why: bulk operations need single-round-trip endpoints; a mixed dispatcher would add conditional branching better kept in separate handlers. What: POST /api/programs/batch/assign (create rows), PATCH /api/programs/batch/status (update assignment_status), PATCH /api/programs/batch/dates (update effective dates). Each validates auth (therapist/admin), verifies program_ids belong to patient_id via join, validates field values. Also update POST /api/programs and PUT /api/programs/[id] to accept assignment_status and effective dates.
close_reason: Implemented batch program routes and single-record assignment field support

title: [epic] Patient-scoped program status and batch assignment editing
description: Add patient-scoped assignment state to patient_programs (assignment_status, effective_start/end dates), a patient-switcher dropdown in /program, and a batch assignment workspace supporting multi-select create/status/date editing. Global exercise lifecycle_status unchanged. Downstream tracker and PT-view reads updated to use patient assignment status via resolveEffectiveStatus hard-floor pattern.
close_reason: All children closed: patient-scoped program status and batch assignment UI/API/DB complete.

title: Make tracker close confirm only after data is logged
description:
## Summary
Adjust tracker close behavior so closing the exercise logging panel only asks for confirmation after at least one set has been logged. If no data has been logged yet, closing should exit immediately without the finish-session warning path.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open an exercise in tracker.
2. Try to close it before logging any sets.
3. Try to close it after logging one or more sets.

## Expected Behavior
- Before any sets are logged: close exits immediately with no confirmation.
- After sets are logged: close asks for confirmation before discarding the in-progress session.

## Actual Behavior
The current Done/close path routes into finish-session behavior, which is easy to trigger unintentionally and does not match the expected close semantics.

## Scope
- inspect tracker close and finish handlers
- make close semantics distinct from finish semantics
- preserve explicit finish flow for saving logged sessions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Closing before any sets exist exits immediately with no confirmation
- [ ] Closing after sets exist asks for confirmation before discarding
- [ ] Explicit finish/save flow still works for completed sessions
close_reason: Completed: top-right tracker control is now a close cross, close/back discard immediately before any logged sets exist, and prompt for confirmation only after logged data exists.

title: Fix form-parameter option union in tracker and history edit
description:
## Summary
Make tracker form-parameter dropdowns and history-edit dropdowns show the full union of previously logged values for that exercise parameter instead of only the current exercise value plus Other.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the tracker for an exercise with a form parameter such as resistance band.
2. Open the form-parameter dropdown while logging a set.
3. Compare the shown options with the values previously logged for that parameter across the relevant history.
4. Open history edit for a logged set and inspect the same parameter dropdown.

## Expected Behavior
Tracker and history edit should show the full union of available logged values for that parameter, such as heavy, black, and blue for resistance band, plus Other when appropriate.

## Actual Behavior
Tracker appears to show only values logged for the current exercise plus Other, and history edit shows only the current logged value plus Other.

## Scope
- inspect the shared form-parameter option builders for tracker logging and history edit
- fix the option-source logic so both surfaces use the intended union of logged values for that parameter
- preserve existing Other behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tracker dropdowns show the intended union of logged values for the parameter
- [ ] History edit dropdowns show the same intended union for that parameter
- [ ] Existing logged value remains selectable and Other behavior still works
close_reason: Completed: tracker form-parameter options now come from shared global parameter history plus in-progress session values, and history edit now uses the same enriched form-context path instead of a raw exercise object.

title: Shared bugs, regressions, and issue follow-up
description:
Shared epic for active bugs, regressions, verification-found failures, and other defect follow-up that should not be scattered across unrelated coordination beads.

## Purpose
Provide one stable parent for defect work in this repo so agents can consistently file, group, and review:
- browser-verified regressions
- user-reported defects
- behavior regressions discovered during review or validation
- follow-up fixes for issue-level defects that are not new product features

## Belongs Here
- bug beads
- regression beads
- verification-discovered issue follow-up
- scoped defect investigations and fixes

## Does Not Belong Here
- new product features
- long-term roadmap ideas
- general coordination notes without a concrete defect
- structural cleanup that is not tied to an actual bug or regression

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bugs and regressions have one stable shared parent epic
- [ ] Agents can tell when to file defect work here versus under feature or coordination epics
- [ ] Defect work can be reviewed as one coherent queue without mixing in feature backlog

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Verification-found bugs have an obvious shared home
- [ ] User-reported regressions can be grouped without inventing new umbrellas each time
- [ ] Defect work stays distinct from feature backlog and coordination-only beads

## Notes
Created as the shared bug/regression umbrella for current and future defect intake.
notes: Corrected per user direction: this epic should not be claimed and should not be in_progress. Returned to unowned open state.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Remove redundant synth.cancel() in speakText causing synthesis-failed on Chrome
description: speakText() calls synth.cancel() before synth.speak(). The effects system already emits clear_speech_queue before speak_text, which calls synth.cancel() via clearSpeechQueue(). This means SELECT_SIDE (and other speech effects) trigger cancel → cancel → speak in rapid succession. Chrome's speech synthesis engine reports synthesis-failed on the new utterance. Fix: remove the cancel() from speakText — the effects system owns queue clearing.
close_reason: Removed synth.cancel() from speakText; effects system owns queue clearing via clear_speech_queue. Double-cancel before speak() was causing synthesis-failed on Chrome for all tracker speech prompts.

title: Fix context.resume() hang blocking effectQueueRef permanently
description: useBrowserAudioContext.js awaits context.resume() with no timeout. On iOS Safari, resume() called from async Promise chain (outside synchronous gesture handler) may never resolve. This permanently blocks effectQueueRef in useTimerAudio, causing all speech/audio effects to queue forever and never execute. Recovery only happens when a new window clears the ref. This is the remaining gap from pt-yirr. Fix: Promise.race with 1000ms fallback so effectQueueRef is never permanently stuck.
notes: Platform correction: issue was reproduced on Windows Chrome, not iOS Safari. context.resume() can hang in any browser when called outside a synchronous user-gesture handler — this is a Web Audio API constraint enforced by Chromium too. The Promise.race fix applies cross-platform.
close_reason: Promise.race timeout added; effectQueueRef can no longer block permanently on iOS

title: Tracker speech output fails in browser
description:
Browser verification on https://nextjs.pttracker.app found that tracker speech utterances are requested but not reliably spoken.

## Evidence
- On the tracker timer panel for Side Steps, switching from right to left updates the UI text to 'Working left side'.
- Instrumenting speechSynthesis on the page shows the app calls speak('Working left side'), but the browser then reports an utterance error of 'synthesis-failed' immediately after cancel calls.
- The operator reports hearing beeps but not the spoken side callouts.
- Additional operator evidence indicates 'pause' is also not spoken when expected, and that all tracker speech appears affected.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Determine why tracker speech utterances fail while timer audio cues still play
- Fix or route around the synthesis-failed path so tracker speech prompts are actually spoken when expected
- Re-verify in browser on nextjs.pttracker.app
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND AND FIXED (pt-28v3p): speakText() called synth.cancel() before synth.speak(). The effects system already emits clear_speech_queue before speak_text, which calls cancel() first. The double-cancel (clear_speech_queue → speakText internal cancel → speak) triggered synthesis-failed on Chrome for every tracked speech prompt: side selection ('Working left/right side'), pause, reps remaining, set complete. Fix: removed the cancel() from speakText in useBrowserSpeech.js. The effects system owns queue clearing. pt-28v3p committed in 51d68a8 and pushed.
close_reason: Root cause confirmed: double synth.cancel() before speak() — clear_speech_queue effect + speakText internal cancel — caused synthesis-failed on Chrome for all speech prompts. Fixed in pt-28v3p (commit 51d68a8).

title: Document responsibility-first file placement rule for agents
description:
## Summary
Add a durable repo doc that explains responsibility-first file placement and mixed-concern avoidance for AI agents, then point to it from AGENTS.md so agents stop reasoning from file caps or hook passing.

## Why
Agents keep drifting into line-count and hook-avoidance thinking instead of judging edits by ownership, responsibility boundaries, and independently changeable concerns. The rule needs a canonical written home that future agents can be pointed to directly.

## Scope
- Determine whether this guidance belongs in an existing structure/agent doc or a new focused doc
- Add the documented rule in the correct home
- Add a pointer from AGENTS.md
- Update docs/README.md if a new doc is added or the docs map changes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The repo has a clear canonical explanation of responsibility-first file placement for agents
- [ ] AGENTS.md points agents to the canonical doc
- [ ] The wording makes clear that file caps/hooks are coarse signals, not the target
- [ ] The doc explains the two required pre-edit questions and the expectation to create a new file when a new concern is introduced
notes:
Chose a new focused doc instead of burying this rule in the long structure binder. Added docs/RESPONSIBILITY_FIRST_PLACEMENT.md as the canonical short pre-edit ownership test, updated AGENTS.md to point directly to it, added a docs-index entry in docs/README.md, and kept docs/NEXTJS_CODE_STRUCTURE.md to a brief pointer so agents can still find the rule from the structure path without having to search a long file.
Expanded the documentation set after user review. The new responsibility-first doc now has matching pointers from AGENTS, the docs index, the structure binder, the structure-review escalation workflow, and the longer agent playbook so the rule is discoverable in both normal editing and escalation paths.
close_reason: Responsibility-first placement docs added and linked across related guidance

title: Extract ProgramVocabEditor workflow state and finalize split
description:
## Summary
Extract ProgramVocabEditor workflow state and mutation orchestration into a focused hook or other clearer owner, then finish the vocabulary editor split and verify behavior.

## Why
ProgramVocabEditor still owns selected-category state, add/edit/archive state, resets, validation, and mutation wrappers. Those are independently changeable workflow concerns and should not remain mixed with the composed workspace UI if a focused owner can carry them more clearly.

## Scope
- Decide the clearest owner for vocabulary editor workflow state consistent with repo layer rules
- Extract selected-category, add/edit/archive state, resets, validation, and mutation wrappers into that owner if it improves cohesion
- Leave ProgramVocabEditor as the named workspace owner that composes the category selector and extracted child pieces
- Run the relevant verification and update the ownership map if files change

## Acceptance Criteria
- ProgramVocabEditor is reduced to a clear workspace owner/composition role
- Workflow state lives in the clearest maintainable owner instead of remaining mixed with all UI concerns
- Verification confirms vocabulary editor behavior remains intact after the split
close_reason: Workflow-state ownership reviewed and finalized in ProgramVocabEditor.js; split verified

title: Extract ProgramVocabEditor term list and row flows
description:
## Summary
Extract the existing vocabulary term list and per-row edit/archive UI out of ProgramVocabEditor into focused child component files.

## Why
The mapped row block is the largest independently changeable UI concern in ProgramVocabEditor. Future agents should be able to change row display, inline edit, or archive-review behavior without loading the category selector and add-term form logic too.

## Scope
- Create a dedicated term-list component for the program vocabulary workspace
- Extract per-row edit and archive-review presentation into the list component or an additional row component if that boundary is clearer during implementation
- Keep archive-review copy and destructive confirmation behavior attached to the vocabulary row concern
- Keep behavior unchanged

## Acceptance Criteria
- ProgramVocabEditor no longer renders the term list / mapped row block inline
- Existing-term display, inline edit, and archive-review UI live in focused child component files
- Archive and edit flows behave the same from the user perspective
close_reason: Term list and row flows extracted into ProgramVocabTermList.js and ProgramVocabTermRow.js and verified

title: Extract ProgramVocabEditor add-term form
description:
## Summary
Extract the add-term UI and add-flow presentation out of ProgramVocabEditor into a dedicated child component.

## Why
ProgramVocabEditor currently mixes category selection, add-term flow, existing-term edit/archive flow, and shared workflow state. The add-term form is an independently changeable concern and should live in its own maintainable file.

## Scope
- Create a dedicated add-term component for the program vocabulary workspace
- Move add-term inputs, helper text, and submit button UI into that component
- Keep shared form styles shared where they are genuinely reused
- Keep behavior unchanged

## Acceptance Criteria
- ProgramVocabEditor no longer renders the add-term form markup inline
- The extracted component owns only add-term presentation concerns
- Add-term behavior and validation remain unchanged from the user perspective
close_reason: Add-term presentation extracted into ProgramVocabAddTermForm.js and verified

title: Structure review: ProgramVocabEditor.js
description:
Review the structure and ownership boundaries of the target file and determine whether any responsibilities should be extracted or divided more cleanly.

Target file:
- C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\pttracker\components\ProgramVocabEditor.js

Scope limit:
- This bead is the only task context for the reviewing subagent.
- Read only this bead for task context.
- Then read only the required repo docs named in the delegated prompt.
- Do not run bd prime.
- Do not search for other beads.
- Do not create another bead.
- Do not inspect unrelated sibling files or neighboring files unless the target file directly imports or depends on them and they must be checked to judge ownership.
- Do not widen scope beyond the target file, except to inspect directly related imports/exports needed to judge ownership.
- Write all findings and recommendations back to this bead.

Question to answer:
1. What responsibilities does the target file currently own?
2. Do any of those responsibilities belong in a different layer or separate file?
3. If so, what are the clearest extraction or division points?
4. If not, is the file structurally sound as-is?

Required output:
- responsibilities identified
- recommended extractions or divisions, if any
- what should remain in the original file
- keep-together notes, if any
- open questions, if any
- confidence for each recommendation: high / medium / low

Parent-agent note:
- Commit preflight for the /program roles fix flagged ProgramVocabEditor.js as exceeding the component cap, so this review is required before any further changes to that file or before using a structure bypass.
notes:
Responsibilities identified
- Category selection and category-option shaping for the vocabulary workspace (lines 7-14, 35-56).
- New-term draft entry and submit validation for code/definition creation (lines 43-44, 64-85, 150-191).
- Existing-term inline edit workflow for one active term at a time (lines 45-46, 87-105, 195-275).
- Archive-review workflow, including archive-review state reset and confirmation copy (lines 47, 58-62, 108-131, 212-238, 276-289).
- Shared local error handling across all three workflows (lines 48, 65, 88, 109, 148).

Recommended extractions or divisions
- Extract the current responsibility "new-term draft entry + add submit UI" into a component file such as components/ProgramVocabAddTermForm.js. Confidence: high.
  Why: the add flow (lines 43-44, 64-85, 150-191) is independently changeable from the existing-term management list. A future agent changing add-field rules, helper text, or submit affordances should not have to load the edit/archive row logic too.
- Extract the current responsibility "existing-term row management, including inline edit and archive-review UI" into a component file such as components/ProgramVocabTermList.js or components/ProgramVocabTermRow.js. Confidence: high.
  Why: the mapped row block (lines 193-294) is its own maintainable workspace with two subflows: edit and archive review. It is the largest JSX block in the file and is the clearest file-size and collateral-change pressure point.
- Extract the current responsibility "vocabulary editor state/effects and mutation wrappers" into a hook file such as hooks/useProgramVocabEditorState.js, with the presentational component left UI-only. Confidence: medium.
  Why: the selected-category guard, archive-review reset, add/edit/archive handlers, and shared error state (lines 35-131) form a small state machine rather than pure presentation. That concern is independently changeable from the JSX structure. This would align better with the repo rule that hooks own state/effects and components stay UI-focused. Confidence is medium because the final wiring needs to respect this repo's layer/import pattern when deciding whether ProgramPage or a small route-local wrapper should own the hook.

What should remain in the original file
- The file should keep owning the Program vocabulary editor surface as the named workspace for /program.
- If split, the original file should remain the thin presentational shell that composes the category picker, add-term form component, error display, and term-list component.
- The local file-only helper formatCodeInput (lines 16-21) can remain if it is still only used by the add/edit UI after the split; if reused by both extracted pieces, it becomes a small pure-helper candidate instead of staying duplicated.

Keep-together notes
- The archive-review copy and destructive confirmation affordance should stay attached to the term-row management component rather than being pulled into a generic shared destructive component. It is specific to vocabulary-term behavior, not a cross-domain destructive pattern.
- Category selection should stay close to this workspace unless another program-editor surface genuinely reuses the same selector behavior. It currently reads as vocabulary-editor context, not a repo-wide selector concern.
- No broad "one vocab editor file is always best" conclusion here: the file header remains accurate, but the body still contains independently changeable concerns inside that editor domain.

Open questions
- Should the category metadata list (lines 7-14) be treated as fixed editor behavior or as extendable domain data? This does not block the structure split, but it affects whether that mapping should remain local. Confidence: low.
- If the state/mutation logic is extracted to a hook, should the hook be owned by ProgramPage, a route-local program workspace wrapper, or a small connected component adjacent to this file? Confidence: medium.

Overall structural judgment
- The file is not structurally ideal as-is for future agent maintenance. It sits at the 300-line component hard cap and mixes add-form UI, per-row edit/archive UI, and a local workflow state machine in one place.
- Meaningful extraction is justified. The strongest extraction points are the add-term form and the existing-term list/row UI. The state/effects extraction is also directionally correct, but the exact owner should be chosen with the repo's layer rules in view.
Parent-agent planning note for implementation:
- ProgramVocabEditor is used only from ProgramPage today, inside the `Manage Vocabulary` details section.
- The extraction should mirror recent repo refactors: keep ProgramVocabEditor as the named workspace owner, then extract independently changeable concerns into focused child files.
- Preferred split shape:
  1. ProgramVocabEditor.js = workspace shell + category selector + composition
  2. ProgramVocabAddTermForm.js = add-term presentation
  3. ProgramVocabTermList.js = existing-term list and row-mode branching
  4. ProgramVocabTermRow.js = per-term display/edit/archive row UI if needed to keep list component cohesive
  5. useProgramVocabEditorState.js = selected category, add/edit/archive state, resets, validation, and mutation wrappers if extracting state materially improves ownership boundaries
- Shared destructive/archive button styling should stay in its own shared maintainable home, not in a broad shared form stylesheet.
- Shared generic form styles can remain in ExerciseForm.module.css only where they are truly shared across workspaces.
Structure review acted on 2026-04-12. Implemented the recommended extraction of add-term presentation and existing-term row flows. ProgramVocabEditor.js now composes ProgramVocabAddTermForm, ProgramVocabTermList, and ProgramVocabTermRow while keeping the vocabulary-workspace ownership boundary.\n\nImportant implementation note for future agents: the optional state-hook extraction was reviewed and intentionally not used in this pass because the repo’s layer guidance makes a direct components/ -> hook extraction the wrong default when the owner component still clearly owns the workspace workflow. The structural problem was the mixed presentation concerns, and those are now split into focused files. Verification confirmed the extracted UI still behaves correctly in /program.
close_reason: Structure review completed and recommendations implemented

title: Fix /program roles table mobile overflow and blank-gutter pan
description:
## Summary
Implement the /program mobile layout fix for the roles workspace so the page no longer widens past the viewport or allows horizontal panning into blank gutter space.

## Why
Investigation bead pt-hftm7.1 confirmed that the roles table in the roles workspace widens the document on iPhone-sized viewports, which produces a bottom horizontal scrollbar and allows the user to pan into blank black space.

## Scope
- Update the roles display in /program so existing roles stay contained within the card on narrow viewports
- Prevent the roles section from widening the document
- Preserve role removal and add-role behavior
- Verify the fix on the Next.js deployment in mobile emulation or the PWA

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open /program on https://nextjs.pttracker.app in an iPhone-sized viewport.
2. Select an exercise with existing roles in the Roles workspace.
3. Observe the bottom horizontal scrollbar and pan right into blank gutter space.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /program no longer gains page-level horizontal scroll from the roles section
- [ ] Existing roles remain readable and removable on narrow mobile widths
- [ ] The fix is verified in mobile emulation or the PWA
- [ ] No regression is introduced in the add-role form or desktop layout
notes:
Implemented the fix in the Next.js roles workspace using the older stacked roles presentation as the active layout across screen sizes, with contribution badges aligned to rehab coverage colors. Code refs: components/ProgramRolesSection.js now renders `Current Roles:` plus stacked role cards instead of the wide 5-column table; components/ExerciseForm.module.css now styles the card layout and maps HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW badges to `var(--danger-color)`, `var(--warning-color)`, and `var(--accent-color)` to match the rehab legend.

Local verification on 2026-04-12 against http://localhost:3000/program under iPhone-sized emulation after selecting Open Book in the Roles workspace: documentElement.clientWidth=320, documentElement.scrollWidth=320, maxScrollX=0. The former page-level horizontal overflow and blank-gutter pan did not reproduce. The rendered roles section showed the stacked `Current Roles:` card layout rather than the old table.
Follow-up structure cleanup completed before commit. The roles-card layout and add-role form styling were moved out of `components/ExerciseForm.module.css` into a dedicated `components/ProgramRolesSection.module.css`, so the broad shared form stylesheet no longer carries roles-only display concerns. Shared destructive/remove button styling was extracted into `components/DestructiveActionButton.module.css` and wired into both `ProgramRolesSection.js` and `ProgramVocabEditor.js`, so destructive button changes now have one maintainable home without living in the main form stylesheet.

Re-verified locally on 2026-04-12 against http://localhost:3000/program under iPhone-sized emulation after the structure cleanup: `clientWidth=320`, `scrollWidth=320`, `maxScrollX=0`, and the stacked `Current Roles:` layout still rendered for Open Book.
ProgramVocabEditor current responsibilities inventory for extraction planning:
1. Defines vocabulary category metadata for the workspace.
2. Shapes category metadata into `NativeSelect` options.
3. Owns selected-category state.
4. Owns add-term draft state for `newCode` and `newDefinition`.
5. Owns inline-edit state for `editCode` and `editDefinition`.
6. Owns archive-review state for `archiveReviewCode`.
7. Owns shared local error state across add, edit, and archive flows.
8. Resets selected category when available category options change.
9. Resets archive-review state when the currently visible term set changes.
10. Normalizes code input through `formatCodeInput(...)`.
11. Validates add-term submissions.
12. Calls `onAddTerm(...)` and clears add-term draft state on success.
13. Validates edit submissions.
14. Calls `onUpdateTerm(...)` and clears edit state on success.
15. Calls `onDeleteTerm(...)` and clears archive/edit state on success.
16. Owns archive confirmation behavior and confirmation copy.
17. Renders the category selector.
18. Renders the add-term form.
19. Renders the existing-term list.
20. Renders each term row.
21. Renders per-row inline edit UI.
22. Renders per-row archive review / confirm UI.
23. Wires shared destructive-button styling into archive actions.
24. Wires shared form/workspace CSS into the entire vocabulary surface.

Planning conclusion:
- This file currently mixes category selection, add flow, edit flow, archive flow, local workflow state, and all associated presentation.
- The split should follow independently changeable concerns, not line-count targets.
- Recent repo refactors in the last 36 hours reinforce the preferred pattern: keep the original owner thin and extract real concerns into focused files.
Vocabulary split implemented on 2026-04-12. components/ProgramVocabEditor.js now stays the named vocabulary workspace owner for /program and limits itself to category option shaping, category selection UI, workspace-level error display, local workflow state/handlers, and composition of extracted child components. Added components/ProgramVocabAddTermForm.js for add-term presentation only, components/ProgramVocabTermList.js for the existing-term list surface, and components/ProgramVocabTermRow.js for per-term display/edit/archive-review UI. Shared destructive/archive button styling now comes from components/DestructiveActionButton.module.css, so destructive button changes have one maintainable home instead of living in the broad shared form stylesheet.\n\nWorkflow-state decision: after the structure review, the state/mutation extraction was considered explicitly and intentionally not moved into a hook from this component layer. In this repo, components/ should not import hooks as a way to offload ownership by default. Keeping the vocabulary workflow state in ProgramVocabEditor.js is the clearer layer fit for now because the file remains the workspace owner and the independently changeable presentation concerns were the main structural problem.\n\nLocal verification on 2026-04-12 against http://localhost:3000/program in iPhone-sized emulation after the split: documentElement.clientWidth=320, scrollWidth=320, maxScrollX=0. Manage Vocabulary rendered the extracted category selector, add-term form, and term list in the correct workspace location. Verified behavior without committing data changes: entering edit mode showed the inline definition field and Save/Cancel controls, Cancel returned the row to display mode, Archive opened the in-row archive-review state with the expected copy and actions, Keep term returned to normal row view, empty add submission still showed Category, code, and definition are required., and code input blur still normalized  Test Code  to 	est_code. Page reloaded afterward to clear test-only input state. Commit preflight passed after the extraction.
close_reason: Roles overflow fix and related structure cleanup implemented and verified

title: Investigate /program horizontal overflow and out-of-box controls on mobile PWA
description:
## Summary
Investigate the /program page for horizontal overflow in the iOS PWA and identify all current or likely places where controls can exceed their intended containers.

## Why
The user captured a recurring horizontal scrollbar and controls that do not fit within their designated boxes on /program. This needs a whole-page investigation with concrete root-cause findings and an implementation-ready fix plan.

## Scope
- Reproduce the overflow in the live /program page
- Inspect the full page for other controls or tables that can force horizontal scrolling or escape their card bounds
- Record exact files, line numbers, affected controls, and likely root causes on the bead while investigating
- Identify the recommended code-level fix for each confirmed issue

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open /program in the iOS PWA or a narrow mobile viewport.
2. Visit the Roles and Dosages sections and scroll the full page.
3. Observe whether any control, table, or wrapper exceeds the visible card width or triggers a horizontal scrollbar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The horizontal scrollbar issue is traced to a concrete cause
- [ ] Other confirmed or likely overflow points on /program are documented with evidence
- [ ] The bead notes include sections, controls, files, and line references
- [ ] A clear solution is proposed for each finding
notes:
Started investigation. Using shared bug epic pt-hftm7 per user direction. Will inspect /program in mobile-PWA conditions and record concrete file/line/control findings plus fix recommendations.
User clarified the investigation target URL: https://nextjs.ptttracker.app. Switching browser verification to that Next.js deployment and treating it as the source of truth for reproduction.
Attempted user-provided URL https://nextjs.ptttracker.app, but DNS resolution failed in-browser. Proceeding with likely intended host https://nextjs.pttracker.app to continue the investigation and avoid blocking on a probable typo.
Confirmed the user's bottom horizontal-scrollbar report on https://nextjs.pttracker.app/program under iPhone-sized emulation after selecting Open Book in the Roles workspace. Browser measurement: documentElement.clientWidth=320, documentElement.scrollWidth=426, main.scrollWidth=426. The roles table is the active overflow source: table width ~374px inside sectionContent width ~246px. Code path: components/ProgramRolesSection.js renders a bare <table> with 5 columns and no overflow wrapper; shared table styling comes from components/ExerciseForm.module.css (rolesTable around lines 192-206, roleRemoveBtn around lines 358-368, sectionContent around lines 39-44). Root cause direction: fixed-content table inside padded flex column without a horizontal-scroll container or mobile stacking strategy; non-wrapping Remove button increases minimum width.
User clarification: when the bug occurs, horizontal panning can move far enough right that the visible viewport becomes mostly blank black space. This is consistent with the measured overflow model: a child widens the document, horizontal pan moves into the extra document width, and the content column remains anchored left instead of filling the scrolled-right area.
Confirmed the blank-gutter behavior the user described. After horizontal pan to max scroll on iPhone-sized emulation, window.scrollX ~106px (maxScrollX ~106) and the visible viewport shows a large black gutter to the right of the shifted content. Screenshot evidence captured from the far-right position. This reinforces that the bug is page-width growth from an overflowing child, not just a cosmetic scrollbar.
Whole-page mobile pass on https://nextjs.pttracker.app/program at 320px iPhone emulation: no independent overflow reproduced in the header, dosage banner/card, vocabulary rows, or exercise-form action buttons. Evidence: header scrollWidth=288 within 288px; dosage patient banner scrollWidth=245 within 245px; dosage card scrollWidth=245 within 245px; vocab rows scrollWidth=254 within 254px; exercise-form actionButtons scrollWidth=288 within 288px. Code review supports this: vocab rows are already stacked at small widths via components/ExerciseForm.module.css lines 390-395, and vocab/action rows use wrapping inlineActions around lines 299-304 plus ProgramVocabEditor.js lines 149-177 and 198-239. Current confirmed live trigger remains the roles table rendered at ProgramRolesSection.js lines 65-101 with styling from ExerciseForm.module.css lines 192-208 and the non-wrapping remove button at lines 358-368.
Recommended solution: change the existing-roles display from an unconstrained bare table to a contained mobile-safe pattern. Minimum safe fix: wrap the table from ProgramRolesSection.js lines 65-101 in a dedicated scroll container with max-width:100% and overflow-x:auto so the overflow stays inside the card instead of widening the document. Better UX fix: on narrow screens, render roles as stacked cards or key/value rows instead of a 5-column table. Supporting CSS updates should also relax minimum table pressure by allowing tighter cell wrapping and avoiding the non-wrapping remove button as the width floor where possible. Header, dosage, vocabulary, and form action buttons did not reproduce independent document-widening in this pass.
Investigation reopened to compare legacy pt_editor roles handling in the rukuba/pt-rebuild repo and the live production app, so the implementation fix can align with the prior working pattern where appropriate.
Legacy repo comparison: rukuba/pt-rebuild uses the same ProgramRolesSection table pattern and nearly identical ExerciseForm.module.css roles table styles. Relevant legacy refs: components/ProgramRolesSection.js lines 72-103 render the same bare rolesTable; components/ExerciseForm.module.css lines 192-208 style the table and lines 281-291 keep roleRemoveBtn white-space: nowrap. This means the root issue is not a newly introduced roles rendering concept; it is an unresolved mobile-fit weakness in this shared pattern, likely exposed more clearly by the current Next.js route/container behavior.
User clarified that the old pt_editor reference lives on the static branch, not the migrated pt-rebuild React surface. Switching the legacy comparison to the static branch version of pt_editor for the original roles handling pattern.
Static branch / production comparison: the static HTML still includes a rolesTable in pt_editor.html, but the live production page keeps document.scrollWidth at 320px on the same 320px mobile viewport after selecting Open Book. Production visibly renders roles through #currentRoles as stacked cards plus a separate #addRoleForm block, not as the wide table the user sees in the Next.js page. Measurement on pttracker.app/pt_editor.html: rolesTable width ~385px exists in the DOM, but document.scrollWidth stays 320px while currentRoles and addRoleForm each stay ~250px wide. Screenshot evidence shows the production roles card layout ('Current Roles' badge/text/remove button row) and narrow stacked add-role form. Practical takeaway: the old static experience avoided the mobile overflow by presenting roles in a card/list layout for the active UI, while the current Next.js page exposes the raw multi-column table directly inside the workspace card. This strengthens the recommendation to switch the current roles display to a stacked mobile-safe card/list pattern rather than preserving the bare table on narrow screens.
Exact static-source comparison anchored to the user-provided file path: C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\rukuba-static\pt-rebuild\public\pt_editor.html. In that file, the legacy editor still contains a generic table rule at lines 347-360 (`table { width: 100%; ... }`) and a raw roles table at lines 671-683 (`#rolesTable` with Region/Capacity/Focus/Contribution/Actions). But the mobile-safe roles experience the user remembers comes from the separate roles-management UI at lines 756-801: `#currentRoles` at line 764 and `#addRoleForm` at line 767. The actual stacked role rendering is in the paired legacy script `C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\rukuba-static\pt-rebuild\public\js\pt_editor.js`: `loadExerciseRoles()` around lines 1436-1457 populates `currentRoles` and reveals `addRoleForm`, and `renderCurrentRoles()` around lines 1461-1482 renders each role as a stacked card row with a badge, text summary, and Remove button. This explains the production behavior difference: the old/static flow keeps the active roles UI in card/list form on mobile, while the current Next.js `/program` page renders the wide multi-column table directly in `components/ProgramRolesSection.js` lines 65-101, which is what widens the page and creates the bottom scrollbar plus black right-side gutter.

title: Investigate /program horizontal overflow and out-of-box controls on mobile PWA
description:
## Summary
Investigate the /program page for horizontal overflow in the iOS PWA and identify all current or likely places where controls can exceed their intended containers.

## Why
The user captured a recurring horizontal scrollbar and controls that do not fit within their designated boxes on /program. This needs a whole-page investigation with concrete root-cause findings and an implementation-ready fix plan.

## Scope
- Reproduce the overflow in the live /program page
- Inspect the full page for other controls or tables that can force horizontal scrolling or escape their card bounds
- Record exact files, line numbers, affected controls, and likely root causes on the bead while investigating
- Identify the recommended code-level fix for each confirmed issue

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open /program in the iOS PWA or a narrow mobile viewport.
2. Visit the Roles and Dosages sections and scroll the full page.
3. Observe whether any control, table, or wrapper exceeds the visible card width or triggers a horizontal scrollbar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The horizontal scrollbar issue is traced to a concrete cause
- [ ] Other confirmed or likely overflow points on /program are documented with evidence
- [ ] The bead notes include sections, controls, files, and line references
- [ ] A clear solution is proposed for each finding

notes:
Structure review findings for hooks/useProgramBootstrapWarmup.js

Responsibilities identified
- Lines 12-22 own two small pure cache-presence predicates for reference data and vocabularies.
- Lines 31-148 own the full React lifecycle for shared /program bootstrap warming from a protected-route session.
- Inside that lifecycle, the hook owns:
  - online/session gating (36-37)
  - in-flight deduping keyed by auth user (39-42, 123-132)
  - IndexedDB bootstrap reads (45-59)
  - user-context resolution with cached-users fallback to fetchUsers (61-70)
  - therapist/admin eligibility gate (72-78)
  - missing-data detection plus fetch-and-cache writes for exercises, vocabularies, reference data, and programs (80-118)
  - retry trigger on browser online event (137-147)
- The file is therefore mostly one lifecycle concern, with a small embedded pure decision layer about what data counts as "already warm".

Recommended extractions or divisions
1. Extract the pure cache-presence predicates at hooks/useProgramBootstrapWarmup.js:12-22 into a pure helper in the same domain as program bootstrap/cache decisions, if that domain already has or is getting a bootstrap helper surface.
- Why: these functions are pure, independently changeable, and not inherently hook-owned. Future edits to what counts as "warm enough" for vocab/reference bootstrap should not require loading the full React lifecycle hook.
- This is the clearest low-risk split point.
- Confidence: high.

2. Keep the main async warmup lifecycle (hooks/useProgramBootstrapWarmup.js:31-148) in the original hook.
- Why: online gating, in-flight dedupe, cache reads, user-context resolution, role gating, cache writes, and online retry are one cohesive concern: maintaining /program bootstrap readiness after authenticated app use.
- Splitting that lifecycle into multiple hooks or helpers would likely force future agents to load them together anyway.
- Confidence: high.

3. Do not extract the per-resource fetch-and-cache branches (82-118) into separate hook files.
- Why: those branches are parallel parts of the same warmup transaction and are easier to reason about together than as multiple tiny helpers.
- Confidence: high.

What should remain in the original file
- The exported useProgramBootstrapWarmup hook.
- The effect lifecycle and online-event retry behavior.
- The in-flight dedupe ref and tracked promise cleanup.
- User-context resolution and therapist/admin gate.
- Coordination of fetch-and-cache writes for the /program bootstrap set.

Keep-together notes
- The hook is under the hook hard cap and reads as one route-bootstrap concern.
- The file header and README entry align well: this hook exists to warm /program bootstrap caches from protected-route usage, not to own route prefetch itself.
- The documented boundary with app/(protected)/ProtectedClientWarmers.js is clear and should remain: route prefetch there, bootstrap cache warming here.

Open questions
- The best destination for the two pure cache-presence helpers is a little unclear under the current import-layer rules, because lib files are not supposed to import other lib files. If these predicates move, they should likely live in an existing program bootstrap/cache helper surface rather than a generic utility file.
- Confidence: medium.

Overall judgment
- The file is structurally sound as-is for its main lifecycle concern. The only meaningful extraction point is the small pure "is this cache already warm?" logic.
close_reason: Structure review completed and recommendations were implemented or resolved in the offline refactor.

comments.author: codex
comments.text:
responsibilities identified
- `lib/pt-view.js` currently owns two broad categories of responsibility:
  1. pt-view route data reads and dashboard transforms for logs/programs/history presentation (`fetchLogs`, `fetchPrograms`, `groupLogsByDate`, `findNeedsAttention`, `needsAttentionUrgency`, `computeSummaryStats`, `detectKeywords`, `applyFilters`).
  2. messaging reads/writes and message counters (`fetchMessages`, `sendMessage`, `patchMessage`, `deleteMessage`, `countUnreadMessages`, `countRecentSent`).
- The file is under the `lib/` hard cap at 279 lines, so the question is cohesion, not size pressure.
- Layer placement is otherwise correct: it is a `lib/` file with no React imports, and it depends only on lower-level shared helpers.

recommended extractions or divisions
- Recommendation 1: extract the messaging read/write helpers and message-count helpers into a separate shared messaging lib, likely something like `lib/messages.js`. Confidence: high.
  Why: messaging is a shared product concern in this repo, not just a pt-view-only concern. The repo docs already describe `components/MessagesModal.js` and `hooks/useMessages.js` as shared messaging infrastructure used by index and pt-view. A future agent could reasonably need to change message send/archive/read behavior without touching pt-view history dashboard logic.
- Recommendation 2: keep the pt-view route data reads and dashboard transforms together in `lib/pt-view.js`. Confidence: high.
  Why: the logs/programs fetchers, needs-attention calculations, grouping, summary stats, keyword detection, and history filters all serve the same route-specific history-dashboard concern. Those pieces are likely to change together when pt-view dashboard behavior changes.
- Recommendation 3: if the messaging extraction happens, move both the message API calls and the message-specific counters together rather than splitting API calls from counters into separate files. Confidence: medium.
  Why: unread/recent-sent counts are part of the same messaging domain and would otherwise create unnecessary cross-file hopping for future agent work.

what should remain in the original file
- `fetchLogs` and `fetchPrograms` should remain in `lib/pt-view.js` because they are pt-view route bootstrap reads, not general-purpose shared data access.
- `groupLogsByDate`, `findNeedsAttention`, `needsAttentionUrgency`, `computeSummaryStats`, `detectKeywords`, and `applyFilters` should remain in `lib/pt-view.js` because they are all dashboard-facing transforms for the pt-view route.
- The local lifecycle helpers (`getExerciseLifecycleStatus`, `isRoutineExercise`) should remain with the needs-attention logic unless they become shared by another route.

keep-together notes
- The pt-view dashboard transforms are structurally sound as one route-domain group even though they do several different calculations. They all shape the same underlying pt-view data set for one route.
- I do not see evidence that the history-grouping, notes keyword detection, needs-attention scoring, and summary-stat calculations need to be split from each other right now. They are independently named, but they still describe one route-specific presentation domain.
- I do not recommend extracting the small lifecycle helper pair on their own. They are tiny and tightly tied to `findNeedsAttention`.

open questions
- `fetchMessages` currently lives beside pt-view route fetchers, but the repo docs point to shared messaging ownership in `useMessages` and `MessagesModal`. If there is a deliberate reason pt-view still owns a separate message data path, that intent is not visible from this file alone. Confidence: medium.
- `detectKeywords` contains an inline `CONCERNING_WORDS` list. Structurally it still fits the pt-view notes-analysis concern, but if the same keyword rules are used elsewhere, that would become a shared extraction candidate later. Confidence: low.

confidence summary
- Extract shared messaging helpers and message counters from `lib/pt-view.js`: high
- Keep pt-view dashboard reads and transforms together in `lib/pt-view.js`: high
- Keep the lifecycle helper pair local to the pt-view transform file: high
- No evidence that the remaining pt-view dashboard transforms need further subdivision right now: medium

responsibilities identified
- Owns the /pt-view route data state for logs, programs, dataError, and offlineNotice (hooks/usePtViewData.js:28-35, 57-58).
- Owns the pt-view cache-first load lifecycle: read cached logs/programs, render cached snapshot immediately, then refresh from network in the background (hooks/usePtViewData.js:80-113).
- Owns route-level fallback behavior and notice copy when live refresh fails, including offline-vs-non-offline distinction via isOfflineRequestError (hooks/usePtViewData.js:114-146).
- Owns sign-out cleanup for the pt-view logs/programs cache when token becomes null (hooks/usePtViewData.js:60-71).

  Evidence: hooks/usePtViewData.js defines getExerciseLifecycleStatus/isRoutineExercise locally (hooks/usePtViewData.js:20-26), while lib/pt-view.js already defines the same lifecycle helpers for the same pt-view domain. That is a future-agent maintainability risk because lifecycle-aware filtering rules can drift across two files.
  Likely destination if changed: lib/pt-view.js, since that file already owns pt-view fetchers and pt-view data transforms.
- No larger extraction is recommended at this time. Confidence: high.
  The main async body is one cohesive concern: cache-first /pt-view bootstrap plus refresh/fallback. Splitting the effect lifecycle into multiple files would likely create artificial fragmentation because the cache read, network refresh, cache write, fallback cache read, and route-level notice copy are edited together as one policy.

what should remain in the original file
- The hook itself should remain the owner of route-level state and the useEffect lifecycle that coordinates cache-first bootstrap, background refresh, cancellation, and route-facing notice/error state (hooks/usePtViewData.js:57-154). Confidence: high.
- The sign-out cache-clear behavior should remain here because it is specifically this hook's route-data lifecycle, not a generic cache primitive (hooks/usePtViewData.js:60-71). Confidence: high.

keep-together notes, if any
- The cached snapshot read, live refresh, cache persistence, and fallback notice handling form one complete /pt-view data-loading concern. They should stay together unless the repo introduces a broader shared route-bootstrap pattern for multiple pages. Confidence: high.
- The file is structurally below the hook hard cap in the current checkout (138 lines by local count), so there is no size-based requirement to split it right now. Confidence: high.

open questions, if any
- Should routine-program filtering for pt-view be centralized in lib/pt-view.js specifically, or is the longer-term intent to move exercise lifecycle filtering into a broader shared lifecycle helper used across tracker/program/pt-view surfaces? Confidence: low.

notes:
Structure review findings for hooks/useUserContext.js

Responsibilities identified
- Lines 31-43 own the hook's default return-state contract.
- Lines 52-80 own pure user-context derivation from the users array plus auth user id, including the messaging-facing participant labels.
- Lines 90-168 own the React lifecycle for session-driven user-context state:
  - sign-out reset and users-cache invalidation
  - cache-first restore from IndexedDB
  - live refresh from fetchUsers(...)
  - offline/effective-connectivity gating
  - fallback-to-cache error handling
  - cancellation protection
- The file is therefore a mix of one stateful hook concern plus one pure data-shaping concern.

Recommended extractions or divisions
1. Extract the pure derivation block at hooks/useUserContext.js:52-80 into an existing user-domain pure helper, preferably lib/users.js, if that file remains the canonical user-domain helper surface.
- Why: this block is pure, independently changeable, and not inherently hook-owned. Future edits to recipient/viewer name derivation or participant-role shaping should not require loading the full cache/network lifecycle hook.
- This also addresses the hook hard-cap issue from docs/NEXTJS_CODE_STRUCTURE.md more meaningfully than a cosmetic split.
- Confidence: high.

2. Keep the default-state factory at hooks/useUserContext.js:31-43 in the hook unless the team is already extracting hook-state contracts nearby.
- Why: it is small, hook-specific, and not independently valuable on its own. Moving it alone would create fragmentation without reducing reasoning load much.
- Confidence: high.

3. Do not split the main effect lifecycle (hooks/useUserContext.js:90-168) into multiple hooks or files right now.
- Why: cache restore, live refresh, offline fallback, cancellation, and sign-out clearing are one lifecycle concern and would almost always be loaded together. Splitting them would increase cross-file reasoning without a clear ownership gain.
- Confidence: high.

What should remain in the original file
- useState/useRef/useEffect orchestration.
- The session-null branch and hadSessionRef guard.
- IndexedDB cache restore and cache write behavior.
- Effective-connectivity gating and network success/failure marking.
- Final state updates and cancellation guard.

Keep-together notes
- The async load path is structurally cohesive even though it is long. It owns one concern: resolving and maintaining route-safe user context from session + cache + network.
- The sign-out cache-clear branch belongs with this hook's lifecycle because it is coupled to the hadSessionRef behavior and the timing of auth resolution.

Open questions
- The file header describes this as a messaging-context hook, while README.md describes it more broadly as the shared resolver for profile-vs-auth identity domains. That wording mismatch makes the boundary a little unclear.
- If future consumers start needing only identity primitives (profileId, patientId, role, emailEnabled) without messaging labels (viewerName, otherName, otherIsTherapist), reassess whether the messaging-facing name/participant shaping should become a separate pure selector. I do not recommend that split yet based on the current file alone.
- Confidence: medium.

Overall judgment
- The file is not structurally unsound, but it should shed the pure derivation helper because the hook is above the hook hard cap and that pure block is the clearest low-risk extraction point.
close_reason: Structure review completed and recommendations were implemented or resolved in the offline refactor.
