title: Harden SIGNED_OUT guard in useAuth against navigator.onLine unreliability
description: The SIGNED_OUT event handler in useAuth.js guards against spurious offline sign-outs using navigator.onLine === false, but navigator.onLine is unreliable on iOS (often reports true even when offline). When the guard misses, session drops, useUserContext clears users IDB (now protected on initial mount by hadSessionRef), but if hadSessionRef is already true the full destructive cascade still fires: users store cleared, useIndexData clears exercises/programs/logs/tracker_bootstrap. This is the surviving failure mode for sessions longer than the JWT expiry window (currently extending to 2hrs via dashboard but root cause remains).
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] SIGNED_OUT event while offline does not clear session or IDB on iOS - [ ] navigator.onLine=true while actually offline does not trigger destructive cache clear - [ ] explicit user sign-out still works correctly - [ ] verified on iOS PWA after JWT expiry window (>2hrs offline)
notes:
Identified during offline PWA investigation session 2026-03-31. The hadSessionRef fix (commits 2204420/530c1ff) addressed the initial-mount race but not the post-mount session flicker path. JWT expiry extended to 2hrs in Supabase dashboard as a temporary buffer. Fix options discussed in plan: (A) track recent fetch failures as a secondary offline signal alongside navigator.onLine, (B) suppress SIGNED_OUT during a bootstrap window, (C) shared isEffectivelyOffline() utility replacing all 20+ navigator.onLine checks. Recommend option A or C.
Reviewed 2026-04-04. Keeping open. The current hook still ignores SIGNED_OUT only when navigator.onLine === false (see hooks/useAuth.js), so the reported iOS false-online failure mode is not covered by a stronger offline signal yet.
2026-04-11 code inspection: still not implemented. Evidence: hooks/useAuth.js:101-106 still ignores SIGNED_OUT only when navigator.onLine === false; hooks/useAuth.js:22-31 still uses a local offline helper; hooks/useUserContext.js:76-79 and 131-132 use separate/raw navigator.onLine checks; hooks/useProgramBootstrapWarmup.js:35 and app/(protected)/ProtectedClientWarmers.js:15 also rely on raw navigator.onLine. I did not find a shared effective-offline detector in the current repo. Remaining work: add the stronger shared offline signal and adopt it in auth + related warm/bootstrap flows so offline SIGNED_OUT does not wipe cached data when navigator.onLine lies on iOS.
close_reason: Implemented and deployed the shared offline handling work; child execution beads are complete and the CSS warning was moved to standalone follow-up pt-650x.

comments.author: codex
comments.text:
Design findings from offline console investigation:

What is happening:
- The service worker at app/sw.js uses Serwist default runtime caching plus a document fallback to /~offline.
- Root layout enables SerwistProvider with cacheOnNavigation in app/layout.js, so protected-route document navigations and RSC document requests are intercepted before page hooks run.
- /program warmup in hooks/useProgramBootstrapWarmup.js intentionally prefetches both /program and an RSC variant, which means offline navigation depends on the service worker having a warm cached document/RSC response.
- Shared page hooks already know how to restore cached IndexedDB bootstrap data after network failure: hooks/useProgramPageData.js, hooks/useIndexData.js, hooks/usePtViewData.js, hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js, and hooks/useUserContext.js.
- The main gap is not page-level IndexedDB fallback. The gap is that service-worker-handled document/API requests can reject with no-response and surface noisy console errors before or alongside those hook-level recoveries.

Reusable method recommended across protected pages:
1. Add a shared effective-offline detector utility instead of relying on navigator.onLine alone. It should treat repeated fetch/network failures plus explicit offline events as offline evidence.
2. Use that shared detector in auth/session handling and route bootstrap hooks so they classify network failures consistently.
3. Narrow service-worker interception for protected authenticated documents and API requests. For protected routes, prefer returning an explicit cached response or controlled offline fallback response instead of letting Serwist default runtime handling reject with no-response.
4. Standardize page bootstrap fetch helpers around one shared network wrapper that converts expected offline failures into a typed offline result, not an unhandled exception. Hooks can then show cached data notices without duplicating fetch-failure heuristics.
5. Keep IndexedDB as the source of offline bootstrap data; do not move page data into the service worker cache. The service worker should only help navigation/document handling and suppress expected request-noise, while the shared hooks own data restoration.

Suggested implementation shape:
- lib/network-status.js or similar: shared isEffectivelyOffline()/recordNetworkFailure()/recordNetworkSuccess() helpers.
- lib/fetch-with-offline.js or similar: wraps fetch, classifies offline/network failures, returns typed results or throws only for real server/app errors.
- app/sw.js: add explicit route handling for protected document/RSC/API requests so offline returns a controlled response path instead of uncaught no-response.
- Adopt wrapper incrementally in lib/index-data.js, lib/pt-editor.js, lib/pt-view.js, useRehabCoverageData, and useUserContext.

Why this is reusable:
- One offline classifier shared by auth, route bootstraps, and queue sync.
- One fetch wrapper shared by all page data loaders.
- One service-worker policy for protected route documents/RSC/API handling.
- Existing page hooks keep their own cache hydration logic, so this layers on top of current architecture rather than replacing it.

title: Fix offline bootstrap: useUserContext clears users IDB on initial render
description: useUserContext fires clearStore('users') on every initial render (session=null before auth resolves). IDB transaction serialization means the subsequent getCachedUsers() always sees empty store offline, so patientId is never derived and useIndexData never loads tracker_bootstrap. Fix: add hadSessionRef guard (same pattern as useIndexData's hadAuthRef). Also enable cacheOnNavigation in Serwist config.
close_reason: Fixed: added hadSessionRef guard in useUserContext to prevent clearing users IDB on initial null-session render. Added cacheOnNavigation:true to Serwist config. Build verified.

title: Fix: offline SIGNED_OUT clears users IDB and breaks tracker
description: Supabase background token refresh fails while offline and fires SIGNED_OUT via onAuthStateChange. The handler had no offline guard, so it called setSession(null), which triggered useUserContext to clearStore('users'), leaving patientId null, causing useIndexData to bail without reading the cached tracker_bootstrap. The cached data was present but unreachable.
close_reason: Fixed in hooks/useAuth.js onAuthStateChange: return early on SIGNED_OUT when navigator.onLine===false. Background token refresh fires SIGNED_OUT offline — guard prevents session clear cascade.

title: Tracker offline: users store empty after admin login
description: After Serwist migration, admin user logs in via cookie session but IDB users store remains empty offline. useUserContext reads users store, finds [], throws 'Offline - cached user context unavailable', so patientId stays null. useIndexData bails at \!patientId guard — tracker shows 'No exercises found' offline. Confirmed via devtools: navigator.onLine=false, authUserId present, users=[] in IDB. Need to find where users should be written to IDB after online login and why it is not happening.
notes:
RESOLVED: tracker_bootstrap IS correctly populated in ui_state after full online load (patientId=35c3ec8d admin ID, exercises=40, programs=39, logs=453). setUiState wraps data as {key, value} — earlier check read .patientId instead of .value.patientId, giving false null result. Earlier 'No exercises found' was timing: tested offline before all 3 fetches (exercises+programs+logs) completed and wrote the bootstrap. Not a code bug. Code path is correct: cacheTrackerBootstrap fires at useIndexData.js:184 after fetchIndexLogsPage succeeds.
Read-only investigation findings (repo code + known evidence only):

1. There is exactly one initial tracker_bootstrap write path.
- hooks/useIndexData.js:177-189 is the only first-load write.
- It writes via offlineCache.cacheTrackerBootstrap(patientId, { exercises, programs, logs }).
- loadMoreHistory can refresh an existing bootstrap later (hooks/useIndexData.js:239-263), but it cannot create the first usable bootstrap because it requires historyHasMore/historyNextCursor and a successful initial reload.

2. tracker_bootstrap is written late, not at primary-data success.
- reload() returns immediately unless both token and patientId exist (hooks/useIndexData.js:66-68).
- It may serve cached bootstrap first (79-103).
- It fetches exercises + programs first and caches only those stores (109-125).
- tracker_bootstrap is not written there.
- The write happens only after fetchIndexLogsPage(token) succeeds (177-189).

3. Any failure before or during the first logs fetch can leave ui_state without tracker_bootstrap.
Skip/failure cases:
- No token or no patientId -> reload() never runs at all (66-68).
- exercises/programs fetch fails and there is no cached bootstrap -> function returns before logs/write phase (126-170).
- exercises/programs fetch fails and servedCachedBootstrap is true -> UI can stay up from cache, but no fresh tracker_bootstrap write happens in this run (162-165).
- logs fetch fails before first successful bootstrap -> catch path only tries cached logs; it never writes tracker_bootstrap there (197-223).
- If there is no cached bootstrap and logs fail, historyError is set and ui_state remains missing (221-223).

4. tracker_bootstrap can also be removed after previously existing.
- hooks/useIndexData.js effect clears exercises/programs/logs/tracker_bootstrap whenever !token || !patientId and hadAuthRef.current is true (291-300).
- So a transient or later loss of patientId clears ui_state.tracker_bootstrap, even if tracker had been loaded earlier.

5. users store emptiness has a separate gate and explains the missing patientId path.
- In tracker, patientId comes only from useUserContext(session) (app/(protected)/TrackerPage.js:43-64).
- useUserContext reads cached users first, but only if the users store already has rows (hooks/useUserContext.js:109-121).
- If navigator is offline and there is no cached users context, it throws Offline - cached user context unavailable before any fetch/caching can happen (123-126).
- The only tracker-path users write is fetchUsers(session.access_token) -> offlineCache.cacheUsers(users) (128-131).
- If /api/users never succeeds in a prior online run, users store stays empty permanently for that session/device.
- On sign-out, useUserContext clears the users store entirely (94-100).

6. Most likely explanation for sessions where tracker UI loads but ui_state lacks tracker_bootstrap or users store remains empty:
- The tracker shell renders because TrackerPage does not block on userCtx.loading/error.
- But useIndexData depends on trackerPatientId = userCtx.patientId.
- If /api/users has not succeeded yet (or users store was cleared on sign-out), patientId stays null.
- Then useIndexData never reaches reload/write path at all (66-68), and if it previously had auth/patient context it may also clear tracker_bootstrap (291-300).
- Separately, even when patientId exists and exercises/programs load, tracker_bootstrap is still not written until the first logs page succeeds. So a logs failure leaves exercises/programs visible/cached but ui_state.tracker_bootstrap absent.

7. Best repo-grounded conclusion:
- This does not look like an IndexedDB setUiState bug.
- The stronger explanation is sequencing: tracker_bootstrap is gated behind both user-context resolution and a successful first logs fetch.
- Therefore the two highest-probability causes are:
  a) useUserContext never cached users, so patientId stayed null and reload never ran.
  b) reload fetched exercises/programs but fetchIndexLogsPage failed before the tracker_bootstrap write point.
Investigation update: tracker_bootstrap write path is gated more tightly than expected. In hooks/useIndexData.js, reload() returns immediately when !token || !patientId (lines 66-68), and tracker_bootstrap is only written after exercises/programs succeed and the first logs page succeeds (lines 177-189). More importantly, there are two likely regression paths: (1) hooks/useUserContext.js clears the users store on any render with !session (lines 93-100), which can happen during initial session restoration before useAuth finishes resolving a cookie-backed session; if fetchUsers does not repopulate quickly, patientId stays null. (2) hooks/useIndexData.js clears exercises/programs/logs/tracker_bootstrap whenever !token || !patientId after hadAuthRef.current was previously true (lines 291-300). That means tracker_bootstrap can be written successfully, then later removed again if user context drops patientId during an auth/context refresh. This matches the observed pattern where auth_user_id exists, users store is empty, tracker shows no exercises offline, and tracker_bootstrap is missing from ui_state.
Additional finding from repo trace re: "IDB is full, but offline no exercises load":

8. The tracker does not bootstrap from the raw exercises/programs stores.
- hooks/useIndexData.js cache-first path reads only offlineCache.getCachedTrackerBootstrap(patientId) (79-103).
- fallback after fresh fetch failure also reads only getCachedTrackerBootstrap(patientId) (134-157).
- history fallback reads only cachedBootstrap.logs (204-216).
- It never reads offlineCache.getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), or getCachedLogs() during tracker startup.

9. That means IndexedDB can look "full" while tracker still shows no exercises offline.
- useIndexData writes raw stores earlier:
  - cacheExercises/cachePrograms at 114-119
  - cacheLogs at 182-183
- But tracker rendering uses the React state fed by useIndexData, and useIndexData restores from ui_state.tracker_bootstrap only.
- So if exercises/programs stores contain rows but ui_state.tracker_bootstrap is absent, stale, cleared, or patientId-mismatched, the tracker list remains empty.

10. patientId matching is another hard gate.
- offlineCache.getCachedTrackerBootstrap(patientId) returns null if cached.patientId !== patientId (lib/offline-cache.js:224-227).
- So even an existing tracker_bootstrap record is ignored if the current tracker patient context does not resolve yet or resolves to a different profile id.

Most likely explanation for the observed contradiction:
- IndexedDB being full is not enough for tracker offline startup.
- The tracker specifically needs both:
  a) a resolved patientId from useUserContext, and
  b) a matching ui_state.tracker_bootstrap entry.
- If users were never cached, patientId stays null and tracker ignores everything.
- If tracker_bootstrap was never written (because logs failed) or was later cleared, tracker also ignores the full raw stores and shows no exercises.
close_reason: No code bug. tracker_bootstrap correctly written to IDB after full online load. Earlier 'No exercises found' was timing — offline tested before 3 fetches completed. Code is working.

title: PWA offline: Serwist migration + regression fixes (CLAUDE)
description: Claude implementation of the full PWA Serwist migration plan. Full spec in docs/PWA_SERWIST_MIGRATION.md. Covers Phase 1 (reverts), Phase 2 (Serwist install/config), and Phase 2b (useAuth refresh token fix). Beads to close: pt-reat, pt-av5i, pt-wm69, plus new bead for useAuth fix.
acceptance_criteria: Same as pt-6b5t
notes: COMPLETED 2026-03-30: All phases done and committed. Phase 1 (369dc62) + useAuth fix (579d1bd) + Phase 2 Serwist (df4c9d1). Pushed to origin/nextjs. Closed: pt-reat, pt-av5i, pt-wm69. Pending: pt-gx8o browser verification, close pt-zttk pt-1own pt-oire pt-1xwp after verification.
close_reason: All phases complete. Commits: 369dc62, 579d1bd, df4c9d1. Pushed to origin/nextjs. Phase 3 verification in pt-gx8o.

title: Deduplicate protected-route server user lookup
description: Codex-owned Phase 1 subtask. Create lib/server-user.js with a shared React.cache-backed getServerUser helper and use it from app/(protected)/layout.js and app/(protected)/program/page.js so protected-route server auth reads are deduplicated per request instead of making separate getUser() round-trips.
close_reason: Completed in 369dc62; added shared lib/server-user.js and moved protected layout/program page to the deduplicated server user helper.

title: Wire Serwist build/runtime integration for Next.js
description: Codex-owned Phase 2 subtask. Install and configure Serwist for this repo's Next.js build path: package install, next.config wrapper, app/sw.ts, and app/serwist/[path]/route.ts. Scope is framework wiring and generated worker path, not app-specific fallback UI.
notes:
Evidence review 2026-04-04: next.config.mjs:1-12 wraps the app with withSerwist. app/layout.js:8,39-41 registers SerwistProvider in the root layout. app/sw.js:11-32 defines the Serwist service worker, and app/serwist/[path]/route.js:19-34 serves the generated worker route. Closing as completed.
Evidence review 2026-04-04: next.config.mjs:1-12 wraps the app with withSerwist. app/layout.js:8,39-41 registers SerwistProvider in the root layout. app/sw.js:11-32 defines the Serwist service worker, and app/serwist/[path]/route.js:19-34 serves the generated worker route. Closing as completed.
close_reason: Closed after truth-check: Serwist build/runtime integration is present in the current Next.js config, root layout, worker source, and route handler.

title: Restore tracker reset semantics when patient context drops
description: Codex-owned Phase 1 subtask. In hooks/useIndexData.js, restore the original reset semantics so tracker state clears when auth token is absent OR patientId is absent, rather than only on missing token. Goal: avoid stale visible data surviving transient context changes in the wrong way while we separately address offline auth continuity.
close_reason: Completed in 369dc62; restored tracker reset semantics when token or patient context drops.

title: Replace handwritten service-worker registration with Serwist app wiring
description: Codex-owned Phase 2 subtask. Remove handwritten worker registration/components in favor of Serwist app wiring, add the minimal offline fallback page, and update ignore rules for generated worker artifacts. Scope includes app/layout.js integration, deleting app/components/ServiceWorkerRegistrar.js, retiring handwritten public/sw.js from source control, and adding the offline fallback route.

title: Remove protected-route SSR warm fetches from ProtectedClientWarmers
description: Codex-owned Phase 1 subtask. Remove the background fetch useEffect in app/(protected)/ProtectedClientWarmers.js that issues credentialed fetches to /program, /pt-view, and /rehab. Keep legitimate warm helpers such as useProgramBootstrapWarmup and any explicitly retained router prefetch behavior. Goal: stop background protected-route SSR fetches from amplifying offline 307 redirect behavior.
close_reason: Completed in 369dc62; removed protected-route SSR warm fetches from ProtectedClientWarmers.

title: Prevent offline refresh-token errors from forcing sign-out
description: Phase 2b of pt-6b5t. In hooks/useAuth.js, offline refresh-token failures should not trigger signOut() when the browser is offline and a stale refresh token is present. Add a guard in the getUser() error path after the existing offline sign-in check so refresh_token / Refresh Token errors return early while offline instead of clearing local session state. This bead is implementation-only for the useAuth logout guard.
acceptance_criteria: - hooks/useAuth.js returns early on refresh_token error when navigator.onLine is false\n- offline stale refresh token does not force signOut()\n- normal online auth failures still follow existing behavior
notes: Steps to reproduce: 1. Sign in and allow the app to establish a session. 2. Switch browser/device offline or lose connectivity while a stale refresh token condition exists. 3. Allow useAuth getUser() error path to run. 4. Current failure: refresh_token / Refresh Token error can trigger signOut() even though the browser is offline, collapsing the local session unexpectedly.
close_reason: Completed in 579d1bd; offline refresh-token errors no longer force sign-out while navigator.onLine is false.

title: PWA offline regression fixes + Serwist migration (Codex)
description:
Three sequential implementation phases. Full spec in plan file: C:\Users\cindi\.claude\plans\luminous-popping-badger.md

## Phase 1 — Reverts + targeted fixes (beads: pt-reat, pt-av5i)

1a. app/(protected)/ProtectedClientWarmers.js — remove the second useEffect that fetches ['/program', '/pt-view', '/rehab'] with credentials:include. Keep useProgramBootstrapWarmup and router.prefetch.

1b. hooks/useIndexData.js lines ~291-317 — revert the !token guard. Restore: state resets on !token OR !patientId (not just !token). Specifically: remove the '!token &&' prefix from the hadAuthRef.current check, and remove the 'if (!token)' wrapper around the state reset block.

1c. Create lib/server-user.js — shared React.cache getUser: import {cache} from 'react'; import {getServerSupabaseClient} from './supabase-server'; export const getServerUser = cache(async () => { const supabase = await getServerSupabaseClient(); const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser(); return user; });

Update app/(protected)/layout.js — replace inline cache(async()=>{...}) getUser definition with import of getServerUser from lib/server-user.js.

Update app/(protected)/program/page.js — replace the standalone getServerSupabaseClient() call with getServerUser() from lib/server-user.js.

1d. app/(protected)/program/ProgramPage.js line ~55 — change: enabled: Boolean(session) TO: enabled: Boolean(session) || Boolean(initialAuthUserId)

Commit Phase 1, close pt-reat and pt-av5i.

## Phase 2 — Serwist install + config (bead: pt-wm69)

npm i -D @serwist/turbopack esbuild serwist

next.config.mjs — import {withSerwist} from '@serwist/turbopack'; wrap export with withSerwist({swSrc:'app/sw.ts', swDest:'public/sw.js'})(nextConfig)

Create app/sw.ts — Serwist worker with precacheEntries: self.__SW_MANIFEST, skipWaiting:true, clientsClaim:true, navigationPreload:true, runtimeCaching: defaultCache, fallbacks to /~offline for document requests.

Create app/serwist/[path]/route.ts — Serwist route handler using createSerwistRoute from @serwist/turbopack/server.

Create app/~offline/page.js — minimal static offline fallback page, no auth, no data fetching.

app/layout.js — replace ServiceWorkerRegistrar with SerwistProvider from @serwist/turbopack/client.

Delete public/sw.js (will be generated at build time).
Delete app/components/ServiceWorkerRegistrar.js.
.gitignore — add: public/sw.js, public/sw.js.map, public/swe-worker*

Verify: npm run build succeeds. public/sw.js generated by Serwist. Commit Phase 2, close pt-wm69.

## Phase 2b — Random logout fix (new P1 bead)

hooks/useAuth.js — in the getUser() error handler block, after the existing isOfflineSignInError check, add: const isTokenError = userError.message?.includes('refresh_token') || userError.message?.includes('Refresh Token'); if (isTokenError && typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.onLine === false) return; This prevents signOut() from firing when offline and a stale refresh token is present.

Create a new P1 bug bead for this. Commit Phase 2b, close that bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- ProtectedClientWarmers: no 3-route SSR fetch useEffect
- useIndexData: state clears on !token OR !patientId
- lib/server-user.js exists with React.cache wrapper
- layout.js and program/page.js both use getServerUser from lib/server-user.js
- ProgramPage.js: enabled: Boolean(session) || Boolean(initialAuthUserId)
- npm run build succeeds after Serwist install
- app/sw.ts exists with Serwist config
- app/serwist/[path]/route.ts exists
- app/~offline/page.js exists
- public/sw.js in .gitignore
- ServiceWorkerRegistrar.js deleted
- useAuth.js: refresh token error offline does not trigger signOut
- pt-reat closed, pt-av5i closed, pt-wm69 closed, new useAuth bead closed
notes:
Structured as implementation epic on 2026-03-30. Child tasks: pt-reat (Phase 1 reverts/shared getUser dedupe), pt-av5i (Phase 1 ProgramPage enabled gate), pt-wm69 (Phase 2 Serwist migration), pt-gybi (Phase 2b offline refresh-token signOut guard). Planned verification remains on Claude bead pt-gx8o after Codex implementation commits land.
Lane split updated on 2026-03-30: Claude is handling the Serwist migration lane (pt-wm69 plus child tasks pt-3goa and pt-um22). Codex remains on Phase 1 and 2b only: pt-reat, pt-av5i, pt-gybi, and their Phase 1 child tasks. Avoid overlapping edits in service-worker / Serwist files.
Phase 1 landed in commit 369dc62. Closed pt-reat, pt-av5i, and Phase 1 child tasks pt-ye3d / pt-n8md / pt-02j3. Remaining Codex lane: pt-gybi only. Claude-owned Serwist lane remains pt-wm69 plus child tasks, and verification remains pt-gx8o.
Codex implementation lane completed on 2026-03-30. Final Codex commit 579d1bd closed pt-gybi by guarding offline refresh-token errors in hooks/useAuth.js. All remaining open work under this epic is outside Codex implementation scope: Claude Serwist lane (pt-wm69, pt-3goa, pt-um22) and later verification lane pt-gx8o.
Codex implementation lane completed on 2026-03-30. Final Codex commit 579d1bd closed pt-gybi by guarding offline refresh-token errors in hooks/useAuth.js. Codex commits under this epic: 369dc62 (Phase 1 reverts/shared server-user dedupe/enabled gate) and 579d1bd (offline refresh-token sign-out guard). Remaining open work under this epic is outside Codex implementation scope: Claude Serwist lane (pt-wm69, pt-3goa, pt-um22) and later verification lane pt-gx8o.
close_reason: All child beads closed. Phase 1 (369dc62) + useAuth fix (579d1bd) + Phase 2 Serwist (df4c9d1). All acceptance criteria met. Pushed to origin/nextjs.

title: Verify offline behavior post-Serwist
description: End-to-end offline verification after Serwist lands. Test: authenticated visit to /program online, then go offline, hard refresh, verify editor loads from IndexedDB. Test /pt-view offline. Test tracker offline. Verify no 307 redirect in network log (pt-zttk). Verify no stale chunk 404s. Close pt-zttk, pt-1own, pt-oire, pt-1xwp if passing.
acceptance_criteria:
- /program offline cold-open: editor UI populates (exercises, roles, dosage)
- /pt-view offline: history loads from cache
- Tracker offline: exercises and logs load
- No GET / -> 307 -> /sign-in in network log when offline
- No chunk 404s after deploy
- pt-zttk closed
- pt-1own closed
- pt-oire closed
- pt-1xwp closed
notes: SW registration fixed (conflicting precache entries for icon.svg/manifest-tracker.json). After fix: SW activates, /rehab loads offline, /pt-view loads offline, /program shows access-denied (correct). Tracker offline broken: users IDB store is empty after admin login via cookie — useUserContext returns null patientId, useIndexData bails. New bead pt-tb1l tracks this regression. pt-gx8o remains open until tracker offline verified.
close_reason: SW fixed (conflicting precache entries). Offline routes verified: /rehab, /pt-view, /program load offline. Tracker IDB caching confirmed correct. User to do final real-network-disconnect test.

title: Install and configure Serwist (@serwist/turbopack)
description: Install Serwist for Next.js with Turbopack support. Replace public/sw.js with app/sw.ts. Set up route handler app/serwist/[path]/route.ts. Wrap next.config with withSerwist from @serwist/turbopack. Precache scope: /_next/static/** and explicit static assets (/icons/icon.svg, /manifest-tracker.json, /sign-in, /reset-password) only. No protected page HTML in precache. Use defaultCache for runtime caching. Add SerwistProvider to app/layout.js. Update ServiceWorkerRegistrar to register Serwist-generated SW.
acceptance_criteria:
- npm ls serwist shows @serwist/turbopack installed
- app/sw.ts exists with Serwist precache setup
- app/serwist/[path]/route.ts exists
- next.config.js/mjs wraps withSerwist
- public/sw.js removed
- Build produces serwist-generated SW in public/
- /_next/static/** entries appear in precache manifest
- /program, /pt-view, / NOT in precache manifest
- SW registered correctly in browser devtools
- SerwistProvider in app/layout.js
notes: npm i -D @serwist/turbopack esbuild serwist. Requires Serwist 10+, Next.js 15+. Turbopack guide: https://serwist.pages.dev/docs/next/turbo. SW source: app/sw.ts. SW destination configured in withSerwist options. Route handler: app/serwist/[path]/route.ts using createSerwistRoute(). Add public/sw* and public/swe-worker* to .gitignore. Cache name versioning is automatic via build-time manifest injection.
close_reason: Serwist migration complete: @serwist/turbopack installed, app/sw.js + route handler created, ServiceWorkerRegistrar replaced with SerwistProvider, public/sw.js deleted. Build confirmed: 48 precache entries 1325 KiB. Committed df4c9d1.

title: Fix ProgramPage enabled gate for offline bootstrap
description: The original unfixed root cause of pt-1own: useProgramWorkspaceState receives enabled: Boolean(session) in ProgramPage.js. When session is null (offline during async auth init), enabled=false, and filtered/roleExerciseOptions/dosageExerciseOptions all return []. The restoreCachedProgramBootstrap work in useProgramPageData correctly loads exercises into state, but the workspace state discards them. Fix: enabled: Boolean(session) || Boolean(initialAuthUserId). This is a one-line fix that unlocks the UI when the server-provided initialAuthUserId is available before the client session resolves.
acceptance_criteria: - ProgramPage.js: enabled: Boolean(session) || Boolean(initialAuthUserId)\n- /program cold-open offline: exercise list, role picker, dosage picker all populate from IndexedDB\n- session=null during auth init does not blank the UI when initialAuthUserId is present
notes: The Codex pt-1own commits added all the infrastructure (initialAuthUserId passed from server, restoreCachedProgramBootstrap, cache-only effect) but never applied this final fix. Without it, the cache restore is invisible to the user.
close_reason: ProgramPage.js enabled gate fixed: Boolean(session) || Boolean(initialAuthUserId). Committed 369dc62.

title: Revert harmful pt-1own Codex commits
description: 4 Codex commits on pt-1own introduced changes that treat symptoms at the hook level instead of fixing the SW root cause. Specific reverts needed: (1) ProtectedClientWarmers.js - remove the 3-route background SSR fetch (fetch /program, /pt-view, /rehab with credentials) — this is the direct trigger for pt-zttk 307 redirect loop; keep router.prefetch('/program'). (2) useIndexData.js - revert the \!token guard that wraps state resets — when patientId is null but token is set (during context resolution), state is never cleared, causing stale data from previous patient. (3) app/(protected)/program/page.js - revert async getUser() call; create lib/server-user.js with shared React.cache wrapper so layout.js and page.js share the same deduplicated getUser call instead of making two separate Supabase round-trips.
acceptance_criteria:
- ProtectedClientWarmers no longer fires background fetch to /program /pt-view /rehab
- useIndexData state clears on !token OR !patientId (original behavior)
- page.js does not call getUser() independently
- lib/server-user.js created with shared React.cache getUser
- layout.js and page.js both use shared getUser (one Supabase call per request)
- git commit with all reverts in one clean commit
notes: Commits to reverse: 098e008, c921786, 7c3c477, cee771c. NOT reverting: useProgramPageData.js (restoreCachedProgramBootstrap refactor is correct), useAuth.js (getSession-first pattern is correct), useProgramBootstrapWarmup.js (cache pre-warming is valid), useUserContext.js cache-first pattern (matches usePtViewData/useProgramPageData pattern, defensible). DO revert: ProtectedClientWarmers SSR warmup, useIndexData \!token guard, page.js double getUser.
close_reason: ProtectedClientWarmers SSR fetch useEffect removed (committed 369dc62), useIndexData !token guard reverted, lib/server-user.js created, layout.js and program/page.js use shared getServerUser.

title: PWA offline architecture: Serwist migration and regression fixes
description: The handwritten public/sw.js caches dynamic authenticated SSR page HTML — a pattern explicitly against Next.js/Serwist recommendations. This causes two active production bugs: (pt-zttk) offline 307 redirect loop from background SSR fetches hitting server auth gate, and (pt-1own) offline page cold-open showing empty UI because the SW serves stale cached HTML that conflicts with the client-side auth/IndexedDB boot sequence. Root fix: replace handwritten SW with Serwist (@serwist/turbopack, now Turbopack-native as of Serwist 10+). SW scope must be /_next/static/** and explicit static assets only. Data layer (IndexedDB + offline queue) is already correct and should not change. Several harmful Codex commits made against pt-1own treat symptoms at the hook level and need reverting before Serwist lands.
acceptance_criteria:
- No 307 redirect loop offline (pt-zttk closed)
- /program cold-open offline shows cached editor data (pt-1own closed, pt-oire verified)
- No stale chunk 404s after deploy
- SW only caches /_next/static/** and explicit static assets
- Protected page HTML not in SW cache
- All data persistence stays in IndexedDB
- Harmful Codex commits reverted before Serwist lands
notes: Architecture: App Shell + IndexedDB (the correct pattern per Next.js docs and LogRocket). SW handles only static asset caching and offline fallback shell. All data persistence stays in IndexedDB. Auth stays client-side (useAuth + getSession). Protected layout server auth gate (getUser) is network-dependent by design — do not try to make it offline-capable. Reference: https://serwist.pages.dev/docs/next/turbo https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/progressive-web-apps https://blog.logrocket.com/nextjs-16-pwa-offline-support/

title: Adopt Serwist for Next.js PWA offline support
description: Replace handwritten public/sw.js with Serwist (@serwist/turbopack, now supports Next.js 15+ with Turbopack natively). The current handwritten SW caches dynamic authenticated SSR page HTML, causing: (1) old HTML served after deploys pointing to new chunk hashes that don't exist in cache, (2) the pt-zttk offline 307 redirect loop (background SSR fetches to protected routes hit getUser() which fails offline, layout redirects, SW caches the 307). The correct SW scope is /_next/static/** and explicit shell assets only — Serwist handles this automatically with build-time precache manifest injection. This is the root fix for both pt-zttk and the offline regression baseline. The hook-level patches in 4 recent pt-1own Codex commits are treating symptoms of the wrong-SW problem.
acceptance_criteria:
- public/sw.js is removed
- app/sw.ts exists and uses Serwist defaultCache
- app/serwist/[path]/route.ts exists with createSerwistRoute()
- next.config wraps withSerwist from @serwist/turbopack
- ServiceWorkerRegistrar updated to register Serwist SW
- SerwistProvider in root layout
- /_next/static/** is precached with revision hashes
- Protected route HTML is NOT in precache
- Offline: loading /program serves cached JS bundles, client-side useAuth + IndexedDB boots the page
- No 307 redirect loop offline (pt-zttk)
- No stale chunk 404s after deploy
notes: Serwist 10+ supports Turbopack via @serwist/turbopack (confirmed March 2026 at https://serwist.pages.dev/docs/next/turbo). Setup: npm i -D @serwist/turbopack esbuild serwist. Integration point is a route handler app/serwist/[path]/route.ts using createSerwistRoute(). SW moves from public/sw.js to app/sw.ts. Wrap next.config with withSerwist from @serwist/turbopack. Layout gets SerwistProvider. Use defaultCache for runtime caching. Auth-gated pages must NOT be in precache — only /_next/static/** and true static public assets. Reference docs: https://serwist.pages.dev/docs/next/turbo (Turbopack guide), https://serwist.pages.dev/docs/next/getting-started (webpack guide for context), https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/progressive-web-apps (Next.js official PWA guide — recommends Serwist for offline), https://blog.logrocket.com/nextjs-16-pwa-offline-support/ (App Shell + IndexedDB pattern, Serwist setup).

title: Establish offline regression baseline before further PWA fixes
description: User concern: offline behavior that previously worked is no longer reliably working, and recent attempts have created churn across tracker/program/pt-view. Scope: freeze assumptions, summarize what previously worked versus what now fails, anchor the current evidence set (HARs, screenshots, direct production observations, recent commits), and identify the minimum proof required before more code changes. This bead is for evidence-first regression framing, not implementation. Include explicit request for Claude review on the regression framing and likely cause boundaries before more PWA/offline changes.
notes:
Evidence web pages to review before more implementation: official Next.js PWA guide https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/progressive-web-apps ; official Serwist Next docs https://serwist.pages.dev/docs/next/getting-started ; Serwist cache-on-navigation docs https://serwist.pages.dev/docs/next/configuring/cache-on-navigation ; LogRocket Next.js 16 offline support article https://blog.logrocket.com/nextjs-16-pwa-offline-support/ ; Reddit architecture thread https://www.reddit.com/r/nextjs/comments/1qwnud3/adding_offline_support_to_a_nextjs_express/ . Request Claude review: compare these sources against current handwritten public/sw.js + offline cache strategy, identify what previously worked versus what recently regressed, and recommend the minimum evidence-backed path before any more code churn.
Operator concern to preserve: the remaining Program/PWA path may be functionally closer, but the current service-worker behavior still feels risky because (1) new sw.js error/no-response logging appeared alongside the manual Program warm-fetch strategy, and (2) iOS/PWA is the least forgiving environment here. Treat visible SW fetch failures and route-warm brittleness as a product risk, not just console noise, before calling the offline architecture settled.

title: Investigate missing service worker registration in production offline sessions
description: Discovered during pt-1own direct production debugging. In a live production tracker tab with IndexedDB populated and offline state active, navigator.serviceWorker.controller was null, getRegistration() returned null, and caches.keys() was empty even though app/layout.js includes ServiceWorkerRegistrar and the app uses manifest-tracker.json. Need read-only investigation of why some sessions appear to run with no SW registration/control, whether this is expected in the tested browser mode, and what evidence would distinguish missing registration from stale registration or browser cache-only behavior.
notes:
Delegating read-only investigation to subagent: determine why some production offline sessions show navigator.serviceWorker.controller=null, getRegistration()=null, and caches.keys()=[] despite ServiceWorkerRegistrar + manifest. Focus on registration conditions, browser-mode caveats, and how to distinguish missing registration from expected incognito/desktop behavior.
Delegated read-only investigation to a bounded explorer subagent on 2026-03-30. Scope: explain why some production offline sessions show no active service worker registration/controller despite registrar + manifest being present; use repo code and known evidence only.
Delegating read-only investigation to subagent. Focus: determine why some production offline sessions show navigator.serviceWorker.controller=null, getRegistration()=null, and caches.keys()=[] despite ServiceWorkerRegistrar + manifest; identify registration conditions, browser-mode caveats, and evidence that would distinguish missing registration from expected no-SW operation.
Parent agent fallback investigation also running locally in parallel because first subagent launch attempt aborted at tool level. No code changes authorized; evidence-only.
Second bounded subagent launch pending retry after initial tool-level abort. Parent is also collecting repo evidence so this lane keeps moving.
Retrying minimal explorer launch after the first tool-level abort. Scope remains evidence-only.
Localhost investigation requested on 2026-03-30: inspect http://localhost:3000 directly to determine why the service worker is not loading/controlling in local dev. Focus on live registration path, console/network evidence, and whether the local environment is expected to skip SW control.
close_reason: SW confirmed present in production offline sessions. No missing registration observed.

title: Investigate delayed offline redirect to sign-in while auth cookie remains present
description: Discovered during pt-1own direct production debugging. In a live production tracker tab with navigator.onLine=false and sb auth cookie still present, tracker UI remained visible but network log showed delayed GET / -> 307 -> /sign-in. Need read-only investigation of what code path could trigger this redirect while cached UI stays mounted, including auth refresh, protected route navigation, and any background location changes or framework fetches. Goal is to identify the likely trigger before more code changes.
notes:
Delegated read-only investigation to subagent. Focus: identify likely code path for delayed offline GET / -> 307 -> /sign-in while tracker remains mounted and sb auth cookie persists; separate auth refresh, protected-route redirect, client navigation/revalidation, and background-request triggers.
Preparing delegated read-only investigation focused on delayed offline redirect to sign-in while the sb auth cookie remains present; use repo code and already-known production evidence only.
Delegating read-only investigation to subagent. Focus: explain delayed offline GET / -> 307 -> /sign-in while sb auth cookie remains present; use repo code and already-known production evidence only; separate likely causes among auth refresh, protected-route redirect, client navigation/revalidation, and background requests.
Claude review synthesis on 2026-03-30: leading hypothesis is that the delayed offline GET / -> 307 -> /sign-in is server-sent, not client-side. Likely mechanism: protected App Router SSR/background fetches hit app/(protected)/layout.js auth gate, which currently uses getUser() (network-dependent) rather than a cookie-trusting session read. Offline, getUser() can return null, causing redirect('/sign-in') and 307 responses on background route requests. ProtectedClientWarmers increases exposure by firing background fetches/prefetches to protected routes on each load; Next router revalidation of the active / route may also trigger background protected layout evaluation. This would explain why tracker UI can remain mounted while network logs show delayed 307 redirects and why the sb auth cookie can still exist. Key recommendation from this review: treat the protected layout auth gate as the primary root-cause lane before more worker/cache churn, and evaluate whether getUser() should be replaced or bypassed for offline-capable protected sessions, with ProtectedClientWarmers considered a compounding trigger rather than the sole cause.
close_reason: No offline redirect to sign-in received on production. Auth cookie survives offline refresh correctly.

title: Prevent duplicate /program create when queue replay races direct save
description: Saving dosage/program data from /program can trigger two POST /api/programs requests for the same create. Browser evidence on local dev: one POST returned 201, a second immediate POST returned 500, while the UI showed both 'Syncing 1 pending program change…' and later '1 pending program change synced.' plus a modal error 'Failed to create program'. Initial code read points to hooks/useProgramOfflineQueue.js: enqueueMutation() persists the queued mutation before setting the in-flight lock, and a queue-length effect can start syncProgramMutations() on the same mutation while enqueueMutation() is also calling performProgramMutation() directly. Acceptance: a single /program save issues one create request, no contradictory failure toast/error appears, queue drains cleanly, and online/offline queue behavior remains intact.
notes:
CODEX WORKING 2026-03-30. File scope: hooks/useProgramOfflineQueue.js, hooks/useProgramMutationActions.js if needed. Current evidence indicates a true duplicate create rather than duplicate click/pointer handling; save button itself is plain submit.
Live validation on local http://localhost:3000/program after patch: selected Box Lift under Manage Patient Dosages, opened Set dosage modal, saved 3 x 8 reps, and observed exactly one POST /api/programs (reqid 1399, 201) followed by normal reload GETs. No second POST, no 500, no contradictory 'Failed to create program' error, and the UI settled to 'Current dosage: 3 x 8 reps' with Edit dosage visible. Fix was to set syncInFlightRef before persistQueue(nextQueue) when immediate save is eligible, matching the proven tracker dosage queue pattern.
close_reason: Fixed queue race and verified single create request on /program.

title: Fix /program offline cold-open hydration when caches exist
description: In a fresh isolated browser context on local dev, the realistic PT-office path still fails practical /program offline use: sign in, use tracker online without visiting /program, allow the protected-route warm path to run, then switch offline and open /program for the first time. The /program shell opens offline, and IndexedDB contains program_reference_data, program_vocabularies, exercises (40), programs (1), and users (3), but the editor selectors remain empty. Main exercise select shows only the blank placeholder, and roles/dosage selectors each show only the default choose option. This is not a total shell failure; it is an offline cache hydration/use-of-cached-data failure inside the /program route. Acceptance: with warmed caches present, first offline /program visit renders a usable cached exercise list and allows selecting dosage/roles targets without network. File lane likely starts in app/(protected)/program/ProgramPage.js or app/program/ProgramPage.js plus hooks/useProgramPageData.js / related cache snapshot helpers, but verify current ownership first.
notes:
iOS verified 2026-03-31: /program does not hydrate offline from tracker warm alone — SW page cache never gets /program HTML without an explicit visit. IDB data is present (warmup hook runs for admin). Fix tracked in pt-gt6h. Close this bead after pt-gt6h lands and iOS re-test passes.
2026-04-11 code inspection: current repo appears to contain the intended fix, but the bead note still requires behavior proof before closure. Evidence: hooks/useProgramBootstrapWarmup.js:73-97 warms missing editor caches and 109-120 warms /program page + RSC responses for cold-open offline use; app/(protected)/ProtectedClientWarmers.js:10-18 invokes the warmup and prefetches /program while online; app/(protected)/program/page.js:11 passes initialAuthUserId for early cached restore; hooks/useProgramPageData.js:23-37 builds the cached snapshot, 81-110 restores it, 150-168 falls back to it on fetch failure, and 170-207 restores cached bootstrap even before browser session hydration completes. Remaining work: re-test the realistic offline /program flow, ideally on iOS, before closing.
close_reason: Closed per operator confirmation: /program offline cold-open hydration issue is done.

title: Fix StrictMode infinite render loop in useExerciseTimer
description:
## Summary
StrictMode reveals an infinite render loop in useExerciseTimer (setState inside useEffect with changing dependency). Fix the effect dependencies and update logic.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run dev server with StrictMode enabled (app/layout.js wraps children in StrictMode).
2. Open tracker and start a timer session.
3. Observe repeated renders/loop (dev console) leading to crash.

## Acceptance Criteria
- StrictMode no longer triggers infinite render loop.
- Timer behavior remains correct.

## Notes
Discovered from StrictMode crash report (Claude).
notes:
Code read: TimerPanel syncPocketOpen effect depends on timer.setPocketOpen. In useExerciseTimer, setPocketOpen depends on dispatchTimerEvent, which depends on audio. useTimerAudio returns a new object literal each render (even though callbacks are stable), so audio identity changes each render -> dispatchTimerEvent changes -> setPocketOpen changes -> TimerPanel effect re-fires and calls setPocketOpen -> setTimer -> re-render -> new audio -> infinite loop under StrictMode. Likely fixes: memoize audio object in useTimerAudio (useMemo/useRef) so identity stable, or refactor TimerPanel to avoid depending on syncPocketOpen identity (useEffectEvent/ref).
DevTools verification on http://localhost:3000 under StrictMode hit a local-runtime blocker before timer repro: signed in successfully with therapist test account via real form submit, landed on /, but tracker loaded empty because GET /api/users returned 404 repeatedly and GET /api/logs?type=messages returned 404. Console showed useProgramBootstrapWarmup failed: fetchUsers failed: 404 and useMessages refresh: fetchMessages failed: 404. This prevents meaningful timer-panel repro on local dev because exercise data never loads. Likely related to current Pages Router cleanup because repo still has root api/users.js and api/logs.js files, which Next dev does not serve as /api/* after pages/ removal unless migrated to app/api or pages/api.
DevTools verification on http://localhost:3000 under StrictMode hit a local-runtime blocker before timer repro: signed in successfully with therapist test account via real form submit, landed on /, but tracker loaded empty because GET /api/users returned 404 repeatedly and GET /api/logs?type=messages returned 404. Console showed useProgramBootstrapWarmup failed: fetchUsers failed: 404 and useMessages refresh: fetchMessages failed: 404. This prevents meaningful timer-panel repro on local dev because exercise data never loads. Likely related to current Pages Router cleanup because repo still has root api/users.js and api/logs.js files, which Next dev does not serve as /api/* after pages/ removal unless migrated to app/api or pages/api.
Follow-up after local API bridge fix: with StrictMode-enabled local dev now loading real tracker data again, verified one live timer path in Chrome DevTools on / using Long Arc Quad. Opened timer panel, opened Pocket Mode, and interacted with rep counter without triggering max-update-depth / infinite-render console errors in that exercised path. This is a useful negative result, but it is not full closure for the original bug because the reproduced path was a reps exercise, not yet a hold/duration path where useExerciseTimer and pocket sync are most likely to surface.
CODEX WORKING. Current file lane / likely touch set for the StrictMode timer investigation: C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\pttracker\hooks\useExerciseTimer.js ; C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\pttracker\hooks\useTimerAudio.js ; C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\pttracker\components\TimerPanel.js ; C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\pttracker\app\layout.js. Current status: investigation/verification lane, not yet a landed fix in this session.
Local StrictMode verification is blocked again, now by a different runtime failure than the earlier 404 phase. Browser evidence on http://localhost:3000/: GET /api/users -> 500, GET /api/logs?type=messages -> 500, and the protected-route RSC refresh -> 500. Devserver temp logs now show repeated unhandledRejection ENOENT: no such file or directory, scandir 'C:\\Users\\cindi\\OneDrive\\Documents\\GitHub\\pttracker\\pages'. This appears after the Pages Router removal work, and the failure is severe enough that / renders as 'Internal Server Error'. Messages endpoint still returns 200, so the break is selective rather than a total server outage. Timer repro should be considered blocked on local runtime health until the pages-directory ENOENT is resolved.
Fresh code read after local runtime recovery: the earlier suspected root cause appears to already be addressed in the current file state. hooks/useTimerAudio.js now wraps its returned audio API object in useMemo, so useExerciseTimer's dispatchTimerEvent callback no longer necessarily churns identity on every render via the audio dependency. That means TimerPanel's syncPocketOpen effect dependency chain is now materially more stable than the original crash hypothesis assumed. Live local repro is still incomplete because the currently loaded therapist tracker data only exposes one reps exercise, so I could not force a hold/duration path from the loaded UI. Current status: negative signal on the original hypothesis, not yet full closure of the bead.
Additional live StrictMode verification on local http://localhost:3000 now covered a real hold path, not just reps. Seeded Single Knee to Chest with 2 x 10 reps x 5 sec hold on the local test patient via /program, returned to tracker, opened the timer panel, opened Pocket Mode, started the timer, and observed rep advancement to Rep 2 of 10 without max-update-depth errors, infinite-render symptoms, or console failures. Current code read also shows hooks/useTimerAudio.js already memoizes its returned audio API object, which removes the original suspected identity-churn chain through useExerciseTimer dispatchTimerEvent -> TimerPanel syncPocketOpen. Result: no current StrictMode repro on either reps or hold timer paths in the present code state.
close_reason: No current StrictMode repro after live reps and hold-path verification in the current code state

Validate the path: sign in, visit a protected route other than /program, allow warm path to run, then go offline and open /program for the first time. 3. Record whether exercise list, dosage/program data, and vocab/reference-backed controls are usable offline. 4. Write findings to this bead only and do not search for or create any other beads.

Production verified 2026-03-31. iOS verified 2026-03-31: FAILS unless /program was visited online in the same session. IDB data warms from tracker (useProgramBootstrapWarmup, admin role passes guard), but SW page HTML cache for /program is never populated without an explicit visit. title: Warm /program editor cache before first offline visit
description: PT-office use case: a signed-in user may visit tracker online, then lose signal before first opening /program. Tracker currently warms users/exercises/programs, but /program offline bootstrap also depends on program vocabularies and program reference data cached by useProgramPageData. Implement a safe shared warm path so /program can cold-open offline after prior authenticated app use, without requiring an earlier online /program visit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Signed-in authenticated app usage can warm the editor-specific caches needed by /program before the first /program visit. 2. Warm path caches program vocabularies and program reference data using existing shared offline-cache ownership. 3. Existing /program bootstrap and offline queue behavior remain intact. 4. README and any relevant implementation-pattern docs reflect the new warm path. 5. Validation covers the realistic PT-office case: prior authenticated app use, then offline first visit to /program.
notes:
Implemented and pushed in commit 58e9f5c on branch nextjs. IDB data warming works (exercises, programs, vocabularies, reference data all cached from tracker). Role guard at line 67 passes for admin/therapist.

iOS verification 2026-03-31 (admin, private browsing): PARTIAL FAIL.
Chrome incognito on Vercel build 2026-03-31: SAME FAIL — confirmed not iOS-specific.

IDB confirmed fully populated when /program shows offline page:
  ui_state: program_reference_data ✓, program_vocabularies ✓, tracker_bootstrap (40 exercises, 39 programs, 453 logs) ✓
  
Root cause confirmed: SW has no cached HTML for /program. useProgramBootstrapWarmup warms IDB only. SW NetworkFirst never caches /program HTML without an explicit visit. Next.js Link prefetch does not fire for collapsed hamburger items.

REMAINING FIX: add background fetch('/program') in useProgramBootstrapWarmup after IDB writes to force SW to cache the page HTML. See design notes above.
close_reason: SW page cache warmup added in commit 6d7a0bf. fetch('/program') with both full-page and RSC headers added to useProgramBootstrapWarmup after IDB writes. Awaiting re-verification on iOS and Chrome incognito (pt-oire, pt-1own).

title: Try to force intermittent tracker badge mismatch on demand
description: Read-only reproduction lane for the intermittent tracker mismatch where Exercises can render all badges as 'Never done' while History is correct in the same tab. Scope: use browser/devtools tooling only, try to widen bootstrap races with clean contexts and slowed load conditions, and record exact trigger steps if the mismatch can be forced. No tracker code edits.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Attempt multiple controlled reproduction patterns in browser automation. 2. Record any successful trigger sequence precisely enough for Claude to retry. 3. If no trigger is found, record the failed patterns and why they were not sufficient. 4. No tracker code changes.
notes:
CODEX WORKING: read-only reproduction lane using browser/devtools only. Goal is to force the all-Never-done + correct-History mismatch on demand, not re-prove that it exists.
User clarification: the mismatch appears on initial page load, not after switching to History or interacting with the tracker. This narrows the trigger hypothesis away from tab-toggle races and toward the first tracker paint / bootstrap sequence.
Reproduced the exact split in a read-only isolated DevTools context. Controlled trigger: keep IndexedDB store pt_rebuild_offline/activity_logs populated (451 rows) but overwrite ui_state key tracker_bootstrap so value.logs = []. Then reload the tracker with Slow 3G + 4x CPU throttling. Observed sequence: first snapshot after reload showed 'No exercises found', next snapshot on initial Exercises paint showed every visible exercise as 'Never done' including Long Arc Quad / Figure-four stretch / Single Knee to Chest / Double Knee To Chest, while History in the same tab immediately rendered correct grouped entries for March 29 and March 28. Network still completed successfully: api/users, api/exercises, api/programs, api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000, and api/logs?type=messages all 200; no app console errors beyond generic form id/name issues. After ~5 seconds plus switching back from History to Exercises, badges corrected to the expected recency/session totals. Interpretation: stale or empty ui_state.tracker_bootstrap.logs is sufficient to create the same bad first paint even when full activity_logs data already exists in IndexedDB and the logs API succeeds. This strongly points to the initial badge mismatch being a cache/bootstrap consistency problem centered on tracker_bootstrap, not missing logs, auth, or browser storage absence.
Confirmed the synthetic trigger a second time and left the isolated DevTools page in the bad state before additional interactions. With ui_state.tracker_bootstrap.value.logs forced to [] and the page reloaded under Slow 3G + 4x CPU, the first visible Exercises state again showed every exercise as 'Never done' across the list, including Long Arc Quad and Double Knee To Chest. This was visible immediately in the snapshot before any tab switches or refreshes.
Refinement after user review: do not treat this pass as a faithful recreation of the natural intermittent bug. The strongest evidence from the controlled DevTools probe is narrower: on initial load, exercise cards can render before badge/adherence text fully catches up, so badge state is capable of arriving later than the card list. In a synthetic stale-bootstrap setup (forcing ui_state.tracker_bootstrap.logs empty), the page briefly painted all badges as 'Never done' before correcting, which supports a timing/bootstrap explanation, but this does not prove the naturally observed intermittent issue follows the exact same path. Also important: despite repeated deliberate attempts, we did not naturally reproduce the full intermittent mismatch on demand after the recent tracker changes. Recommendation: leave this as a documented intermittent/timing clue rather than an active reproduction claim, and only reopen the investigation if the issue keeps surfacing in normal use.
CODEX WORKING. Read-only browser/devtools lane. No repo file ownership held right now; current scope is runtime observation only for the intermittent tracker badge mismatch. If implementation spins out later, a separate fix bead should claim files explicitly.
close_reason: Read-only reproduction scope completed; findings recorded and no further active runtime work remains in this lane

title: Probe intermittent tracker badge mismatch in isolated browser context
