title: Further decompose pt-view for AI-manageable ownership boundaries
description:
Source Reference: discovered during post-refactor scope review of pt-x2y on 2026-03-21
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,cleanup,architecture,pt-view,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/usePtViewData.js
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/hooks/
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Continue decomposing /pt-view so the page file becomes a true route orchestrator with clearly scoped child components and hooks, rather than a mixed page that still carries too many unrelated responsibilities for future agent work.

Context:
The first cleanup pass in pt-x2y moved the signed-in bootstrap and offline-fallback flow out of pages/pt-view.js and pages/rehab.js. Rehab is now in acceptable orchestrator shape. pt-view is improved but still too broad for AI-maintainable ownership boundaries: the page is 356 lines and still mixes route orchestration with page-local UI-state persistence, note preprocessing/highlighting, inline page-only render sections, message/email glue, and exercise-history modal targeting.

Important discussion context from the 2026-03-21 handoff:
- Do not treat the documented cap as the real stopping rule. Treat content and ownership clarity as the limit.
- The goal is to make future Codex/Claude work as easy and low-risk as possible; smaller, scoped ownership surfaces are preferred over broad mixed files.
- The user's guidance was explicit: what matters is what is best for future agents to manage, debug, edit, and extend, not whether a file is barely under the hard cap.
- Compared with pages/index.js (387 lines, far more subsystems), pt-view at 356 lines is still too large for what it actually owns, which is evidence that its concerns are still too mixed.
- Future-agent rule from discussion: a thin page orchestrator plus scoped hooks/components is better here than asking agents to work inside one 300-400 line mixed page.

Current State / Important Facts:
- Local commit 1b9ebf9 on branch nextjs already landed the first route-orchestrator cleanup for rehab + pt-view and closed pt-x2y locally. That commit is NOT pushed yet.
- Branch state at handoff: nextjs is ahead of origin/nextjs by 1 commit.
- Unrelated in-progress tracker offline edits are still dirty in:
  - pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
  - pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
  Leave them alone unless you are explicitly resuming pt-zb3 work.
- Current pt-view responsibilities after pt-x2y:
  - pages/pt-view.js owns auth guard, UI state, modal open/close, inline NeedsAttention/SummaryStats/FiltersPanel renderers, note preprocessing, and message/email glue.
  - hooks/usePtViewData.js owns users/programs/logs bootstrap, patient-context resolution, cache reads/writes, sign-out cache cleanup, and offline fallback.
- Current rehab status after pt-x2y:
  - pages/rehab.js is thin enough for now.
  - hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js owns rehab fetch/cache/fallback and does not need immediate further split.

Best Next Decomposition Targets:
1. Extract page-only render sections from pages/pt-view.js into focused components.
   Recommended first targets:
   - NeedsAttention
   - FiltersPanel
   SummaryStats may stay inline if desired, but can also move if that makes ownership clearer.
2. Move page-local persisted UI-state and note shaping into a focused hook.
   Likely options:
   - hooks/usePtViewUiState.js for filters-expanded, notes-collapsed, dismissed-notes, and IndexedDB UI-state persistence
   - or hooks/usePtViewNotes.js if note preprocessing/highlighting is easier to keep separate from generic UI state
3. Keep pages/pt-view.js focused on:
   - useAuth/usePtViewData/useMessages composition
   - route-level modal open/close state
   - passing props to child components
4. Update README ownership notes in the same change so future agents know where pt-view notes/filter state and page-only panels live.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not use page size alone as the stopping rule; use ownership clarity for future agent maintenance as the bar.
- Prefer scoped components/hooks over keeping more mixed logic in pages/pt-view.js.
- Preserve the current profileId fix for useMessages / MessagesModal wiring.
- Preserve current offline fallback behavior from hooks/usePtViewData.js.
- Do not disturb the unrelated dirty tracker offline files unless intentionally switching lanes.
- If you commit this work, include the bead id in the commit message.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] pages/pt-view.js mostly orchestrates auth, route state, modal state, and composition
- [ ] Inline render helpers that form their own page workspaces/panels are extracted to focused components
- [ ] Persisted UI-state and/or note-shaping logic no longer live broadly in the page file
- [ ] Message viewer/profile id wiring remains correct after refactor
- [ ] README points future agents to the new pt-view ownership boundaries

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-x2y
- related: pt-amc
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Resume this when returning to route-structure cleanup or when preparing /pt-view for cutover-readiness review.
notes:
2026-03-21 doc guidance tightened in docs/NEXTJS_CODE_STRUCTURE.md and committed locally in 02959ee. Added explicit AI-maintainability context: unknown future agents, future Codex/Claude releases may differ, ownership clarity matters more than hard caps, and mixed files raise collateral-change risk.
2026-03-21 implementation committed locally in 6a388f8 and pushed: extracted PtViewNeedsAttention, PtViewSummaryStats, PtViewFiltersPanel, and usePtViewUiState so pages/pt-view.js no longer owns those panel renderers or persisted notes/filter UI state directly. README ownership notes updated in the same change.
2026-03-21 preview verification after 6a388f8: /pt-view loaded on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/pt-view in an authenticated session with the refactored panel/components path. Confirmed extracted pieces rendered and behaved correctly: Needs Attention panel present, summary stats rendered, filters toggle opened the extracted filters panel, history list rendered session rows, and the messages modal opened successfully with the current shared viewer/profile wiring. No pt-view regression found in this pass from the component/hook extraction.
close_reason: Extracted pt-view page-only panels and persisted UI-state into focused components/hooks, updated README ownership notes, and preview-verified the refactor path.

title: Refactor rehab and pt-view pages into route orchestrators
description:

Status: in_progress

Issue:
Move route bootstrap and offline-fallback responsibilities for /pt-view and /rehab out of the page files so those pages stay as route orchestrators rather than mixed orchestration-plus-data workflow files.

Context:
The current code-structure guidance says pages should coordinate auth, top-level route state, and major components, while larger data/bootstrap concerns should live in focused hooks or other shared files. Review found that pages/pt-view.js still owned its full users/programs/logs bootstrap and offline fallback flow, and pages/rehab.js still owned its logs/roles fetch plus cache fallback path. This makes the cutover surface harder to reason about and leaves those routes messier than the current Next.js structure guidance intends.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve current route behavior and offline fallback semantics
- Keep page-local UI state in the page when it is still route-specific and lightweight
- Do not disturb unrelated in-progress tracker offline work already in the worktree
- Update README ownership notes in the same change

title: Fix messages viewerId bug in pt-view.js (profileId vs auth_id)
description: pt-view.js has the same viewerId mismatch as index.js (fixed in commit 9471519). Lines 135 and 400 pass session.user.id (Supabase auth UUID / auth_id) as viewerId to useMessages and MessagesModal. clinical_messages.sender_id stores the users table PK (profile id), so isSent is always false — all messages appear received, badge overcounts, sender labels wrong. Fix: extract currentUser.id from resolvePatientScopedUserContext result, store as profileId state, pass as viewerId to useMessages and MessagesModal. Also add viewerName/otherName/otherIsTherapist props for sender labels. Skipped this session because Codex was actively working in pt-view.js.
notes:
Context for stateless resume:

Root cause: clinical_messages.sender_id stores the users table PK (e.g. '35c3ec8d...'), NOT the Supabase auth UUID (e.g. '11845649...'). In pt-view.js, viewerId is currently set to session.user.id which is the auth UUID — so msg.sender_id === viewerId is always false. Every message appears received, badge overcounts all messages, and sender labels will be wrong.

The fix was already applied to index.js in commit 9471519:
- Added profileId state (= currentUser.id from fetchUsers, i.e. users table PK)
- Added viewerName, otherName, otherIsTherapist state for sender labels
- Changed useMessages(token, userId) → useMessages(token, profileId)
- Changed viewerId={userId} → viewerId={profileId} in MessagesModal
- Added viewerName/otherName/otherIsTherapist props to MessagesModal

Apply the identical fix to pt-view.js:
- pt-view.js already calls resolvePatientScopedUserContext() which returns currentUser
- currentUser.id is the profile id needed — extract it and store as profileId state
- pt-view.js lines to fix: 135 (useMessages call), 400 (viewerId prop)
- Also pass viewerName/otherName/otherIsTherapist to MessagesModal in pt-view.js

Skipped this session because Codex was actively working in pt-view.js — coordinate before starting.
close_reason: Fixed: pt-view.js now uses useUserContext (profileId, viewerName, otherName, otherIsTherapist, recipientId all passed to MessagesModal). Commit c302242.

title: Messages: Hide should archive (remove from list), not collapse inline
description: In the legacy app, clicking Hide on a message sets is_archived=true in the DB and removes the message from the visible list entirely. In the current Next.js implementation, Hide collapses the message inline and shows Show/Restore buttons — archived messages remain visible in collapsed form. This is a parity regression. Expected: Hide archives and removes from list. Actual: Hide collapses to inline stub with Show/Restore affordance. Also: two DN-052 test messages are present in the admin account and need to be purged from the DB.
notes: Fixed in commit 9471519: isSent bug (profileId vs auth_id), sender labels, Archive replaces Hide, tap-to-collapse, show-archived checkbox, correct empty state copy.

title: Reorganize pt-rebuild/docs into current, generated, legacy, and historical references
description:
Source Reference: user-directed docs review 2026-03-20
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: docs,cleanup,information-architecture,migration,beads
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEV_PRACTICES.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/AI_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/API_SLOT_STRATEGY_2026-02-17.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/PHASE_1_ESTIMATE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/SOUND_TRIGGER_AUDIT.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEV_NOTES.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/dev_notes.json
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Review and reorganize `pt-rebuild/docs` so current operational docs are easy to find, stale guidance is corrected, generated and legacy files are clearly separated, and historical one-off memos stop competing with living references.

Context:
A review of `pt-rebuild/docs` on 2026-03-20 found several doc-health problems that now make the folder harder to trust and navigate:
- `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md` mixes historical decisions, live migration status, branch strategy, and architecture rules that now overlap with `README.md` and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`; it also contradicts itself by saying all work stays on `nextjs` until full completion while also saying incremental merges to `main` are the active strategy.
- `DEVELOPMENT.md` still describes the app as a static frontend in `public/`, which no longer matches the live repo shape documented in `README.md`.
- `TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md` still says the offline log path queues to `localStorage`, while `README.md` now documents IndexedDB-backed offline storage.
- The top-level `docs` folder mixes living references with generated files (`BEADS_QUICKREF.md`, `DEV_NOTES.md`), legacy archive inputs (`dev_notes.json`), and dated one-off memos (`API_SLOT_STRATEGY_2026-02-17.md`, `PHASE_1_ESTIMATE.md`, `SOUND_TRIGGER_AUDIT.md`) without clear grouping or retention rules.
- `AI_WORKFLOW.md` is now essentially a legacy-transition note; its placement and naming make it easy to mistake for an active workflow doc.

Recommended outcome:
- Define a clearer docs information architecture for current references vs generated files vs legacy archive vs dated analysis memos.
- Update current docs so they reflect the live Next.js-plus-legacy hybrid app accurately.
- Reduce duplicate ownership/status guidance across `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`.
- Add explicit placement/retention rules so future one-off audits and dated memos do not accumulate at the docs root without a home.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve useful historical context; do not delete dated analysis blindly.
- Keep generated files generated, not hand-maintained.
- Respect existing references from `AGENTS.md` and `README.md`; update those references in the same change if files move or are renamed.
- Do not revive `dev_notes` as the active tracker.
- Prefer a small number of clearly named folders or index sections over a deep hierarchy.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Current-state docs no longer contradict the live repo architecture or offline storage model
- [ ] `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md` have clearly separated roles with minimal duplicated guidance
- [ ] Generated files and legacy archive files are clearly labeled and, if moved, all references are updated
- [ ] Historical memos/audits have an intentional home and naming pattern
- [ ] A future agent can tell where to look for current workflow guidance in under a minute

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if new docs drift reintroduces contradictory current-state guidance or the docs root starts accumulating uncategorized dated memos again.
close_reason: Completed

VALID_PT_CATEGORIES is flagged with a TODO comment. Fix: fetch valid categories from the vocab table at validation time (or cache at startup) instead of hardcoding them.

Constraints/Caveats:
- API handler must remain fast — consider caching vocab values rather than a per-request fetch if latency is a concern
- Do not change validation behavior during the fix — same values, just loaded dynamically

Validation Checklist:

- [ ] Validation uses vocab table values instead
- [ ] Existing valid categories still pass; invalid categories still return 400

Dependencies:
- none

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
close_reason: Fixed. fetchValidPtCategories() queries vocab_pt_category table at runtime. Throws on DB error (no hardcoded fallback). Both createExercise and updateExercise catch vocab fetch failures and return 500. VALID_PT_CATEGORIES_FALLBACK removed.

title: Review index.js size cap: determine if exemption is warranted
description:
Source Reference: structure audit 2026-03-18
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,structure,index
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
pages/index.js is at 494 lines — 6 lines under the 500 hard cap. index.html was the largest file in the pre-transition codebase. Before any further growth triggers a forced split, both agents should agree on whether the page genuinely requires cap exemption or whether a clean split exists.

Context:
The structure rules allow exemption if both agents agree the file cannot be cleanly split and the hard cap is unavoidable given the domain. index.js is the main tracker shell — it orchestrates auth, data, offline queue, logging, timer, history, and session finalization. Each concern is already extracted to hooks/components. What remains is orchestration glue. If that glue is genuinely one concern (orchestration), an exemption may be valid. If sub-orchestrators can be extracted (e.g. a useTrackerSession hook), a split is possible.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not split artificially just to hit a number
- Do not grant exemption without both agents reviewing
- If exemption granted, document the reason in this issue and update NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Both agents have reviewed the current index.js shape
- [ ] Agreement reached: exemption or split plan
- [ ] If exemption: NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md updated with documented reason
- [ ] If split: clean domain boundary identified before any code is written
notes:
2026-03-18 update: index.js is now at 621 lines — 121 over the 500 hard cap.

Claude review pass (2026-03-18):
Two clean extractions identified — not artificial splits:

1. useManualLog hook (~80 lines): manualLogState + its 5 handlers. Self-contained; takes draftSession + selectedExercise as inputs.
2. useTrackerSession hook (~150 lines): active session lifecycle — draftSession, timer state, backdate, all session handlers. Cohesive concern.

Combined savings ~230 lines → index.js lands ~390, under 350 aim.

DECISION (Claude, primary architect): split is warranted. No exemption needed. Work tracked in child bead.
close_reason: Review complete. Decision made: split warranted. useManualLog + useTrackerSession hooks identified as clean extractions. Work tracked in pt-dhy.

title: Progress-comparison speech parity
description:
Source Reference: static logger trace from pt-rebuild/public/index.html on 2026-03-17
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,logging,speech,comparison
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
Match static progress-comparison speech behavior after set confirmation. Static logger speaks delayed comparison feedback such as X more reps than last time or X fewer reps than last time based on the most recent comparable prior set.

Static Source Anchors:
- announceProgressComparison(): public/index.html around lines 3479-3509
- call site after set confirmation: public/index.html around lines 3455-3460

Static Rules:
- Comparison speech is delayed after set confirmation rather than spoken during live timer/counting interaction
- Positive diff uses ${diff} more rep(s) than last time
- Negative diff uses ${Math.abs(diff)} fewer reps than last time
- Negative comparison only speaks when the drop is greater than 2 reps

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve static delayed timing if this behavior is implemented in Next.js
- If Next.js intentionally omits comparison speech, the omission must be an explicit product choice rather than accidental loss
- Do not close until linked parity bead ptrebuild-spg.10 is verified or closed

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Positive comparison speech matches static wording and timing
- [ ] Negative comparison speech matches static wording and threshold
- [ ] Comparison feedback is tied to set confirmation, not live counter/timer updates
- [ ] Any intentional omission is documented rather than silently diverging
notes:
2026-03-18 implementation update: Next.js now wires delayed post-save comparison speech after successful Next Set confirmation using the chosen replacement model rather than raw static previous-set matching. Current implementation compares the in-panel current session against the most recent comparable prior session before the panel opened, filtered by exercise and side context. Priority order: best-set improvement first, then total-volume drop, then total-volume improvement. Comparison speech remains delayed 1500ms after confirmation instead of firing during live timer/counting interaction.
2026-03-18 restart handoff: Next.js now wires delayed post-save comparison speech after successful Next Set confirmation using the approved replacement model instead of raw static previous-set matching. Implementation compares the in-panel current session against the most recent comparable prior session before the panel opened, filtered by exercise and side context. Priority order in code: best-set improvement first, then total-volume drop, then total-volume improvement. Comparison speech remains delayed 1500ms after confirmation and does not fire during live timer/counting interaction. Code files: pt-rebuild/lib/logger-progress-comparison.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/usePanelSessionProgress.js, pt-rebuild/pages/index.js. Remaining work: rerun next build after the final sessionStartedAt reset change and then verify runtime behavior.
close_reason: Core timer/audio parity slice is satisfied enough for current migration work. Any remaining gaps here are no longer blocking parity acceptance for timer/sounds.

- Pocket timer mode inherits the normal countdown and completion cues via the shared timer
- At 700ms long press in timer mode, log partial rep and playBeep(500, 150, 0.4)
- No extra speech for entering or exiting Pocket Mode

title: Hold timer completion cue parity
description:

Issue:
Match static hold-timer completion behavior exactly. Every timed rep completion plays the triple completion beep, advances rep state, and then speaks milestone phrases based on repsLeft after the timed rep completes.

Static Source Anchors:
- initTimerMode(): public/index.html around lines 2692-2718
- startTimer(): public/index.html around lines 2775-2803
- playCompletionSound(): public/index.html around lines 2894-2903

Static Rules:
- Hold mode uses currentExercise.seconds_per_rep and currentExercise.current_reps
- When remaining hits zero, play completion triple beep
- Then compute repsLeft = totalReps - currentRep
- Speak Set complete when repsLeft <= 0
- Speak Last rep when repsLeft === 1
- Speak 3 reps left when repsLeft === 3
- Speak 5 reps left when repsLeft === 5
- Advance currentRep and reset display to full target duration for the next rep

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve the static moment at which Last rep is spoken, even if it differs from counter mode interaction timing
- Do not close until linked parity beads ptrebuild-spg.3 and ptrebuild-spg.10 are verified or closed

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Triple completion beep fires on every hold-rep timer completion
- [ ] Hold milestone speech uses repsLeft 5/3/1/0 after timed rep completion
- [ ] Last rep timing matches static hold behavior exactly
- [ ] Timer resets to full target duration and advances to the next rep without appending an accepted set

title: Verify copy-sensitive parity
description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:

- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that all copy-sensitive strings match the static baseline exactly and that toast animate-in and fade-out timing matches the static staged lifecycle.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
These strings must match exactly: "Offline - changes will sync later" (connectivity-loss save feedback), "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" (running Pocket Mode timer hint), "Nothing to sync!" (empty queue manual sync), "Offline mode" and "Using cached data" (offline startup feedback), "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." (backdate warning). Toast lifecycle: the toast must animate in after a short timeout and be removed only after its display duration plus fade-out delay — not disappear instantly or at a fixed arbitrary timeout.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline - changes will sync later" is shown exactly when saving without network connectivity
- [ ] "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" is the exact hint shown in running Pocket Mode timer
- [ ] "Nothing to sync!" is shown exactly when manual sync is triggered with an empty queue
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown exactly during offline startup fallback
- [ ] "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." is the exact backdate warning copy
- [ ] Toast animates in after a short timeout (not shown instantly)
- [ ] Toast is removed only after display duration plus fade-out delay (not instant removal)

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes:
2026-03-22 partial implementation in commit 283974f on nextjs: tracker manual sync now surfaces exact empty-queue copy 'Nothing to sync!' in pages/index.js, offline queue-save fallback in hooks/useTrackerSession.js now uses exact spec copy 'Offline - changes will sync later', and hooks/useToast.js now stages toast animate-in after a short delay before the existing fade-out/unmount lifecycle. Acceptance verification still remains open.
2026-03-22 verification handoff for parallel thread: verify the current nextjs preview against commit 283974f with a runtime/browser pass only (no new code unless a clear failing case is confirmed). Focus exact acceptance items: (1) when tracker save falls back to offline queue, the user-facing copy is exactly 'Offline - changes will sync later'; (2) Pocket Mode running timer hint is exactly 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial'; (3) triggering NavMenu manual sync with an empty tracker queue shows exactly 'Nothing to sync!'; (4) offline startup fallback shows exact shell copy 'Offline mode' or 'Using cached data' as appropriate; (5) backdate warning copy in SessionNotesModal is exactly 'Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time.'; (6) toast lifecycle is staged: toast is inserted hidden, animates in after a short delay, remains visible for duration, then fades out before unmount; it should not appear instantly or disappear abruptly. Implementation context already landed in 283974f: pages/index.js manual sync now surfaces 'Nothing to sync!', hooks/useTrackerSession.js now uses exact offline-save copy, hooks/useToast.js now stages animate-in before fade-out. Use preview-first validation and record any failing item with the exact observed copy/behavior and file likely responsible. If everything passes, note that pt-780.8 is ready to close.
2026-03-22 verification pass on preview https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app against nextjs HEAD 283974f96acfee01a80cc7d40ce2cf79f36f62a4 using Chrome DevTools + Playwright.

Passed:
- Connectivity-loss save feedback shows exact toast copy `Offline - changes will sync later` during offline Save & Finish.
- Running Pocket Mode timer shows exact hint `Tap to pause · Hold for partial`.
- Manual sync with empty queue shows exact toast/status copy `Nothing to sync!`.
- Backdate warning shows exact copy `Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time.` once the datetime is changed by more than two minutes.
- Toast lifecycle is staged, not abrupt: runtime mutation log showed toast inserted hidden first, then shown, then later removed after remaining visible rather than disappearing immediately.

Failed:
- Offline startup fallback did not preserve cached shell behavior on a true offline reload. After warming the preview online, switching DevTools network emulation to Offline, and reloading, the page navigated to `chrome-error://chromewebdata/` with `ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED` instead of loading the tracker shell with `Offline mode` or `Using cached data`.

Result: pt-780.8 is not ready to close yet because the offline-startup acceptance item still fails in runtime verification.
2026-03-22 parallel runtime verification result: copy-sensitive items are mostly passing, but the bead is not ready to close because offline startup fallback still fails. After warming the preview online, switching the browser to offline, and reloading, the app navigated to chrome-error://chromewebdata/ with ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED instead of loading the cached tracker shell and showing 'Offline mode' or 'Using cached data'. This keeps the acceptance surface open and points back to the offline shell/bootstrap lane rather than the copy strings themselves.
2026-03-22 Codex shared-browser reproduction after restart: using the new non-isolated chrome_devtools_shared MCP with a warmed authenticated nextjs preview session, the failing path from the earlier report did NOT reproduce. In the warmed shared context at https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/, switching network emulation to Offline and reloading kept the tracker on the cached picker with visible 'Using cached data'. Opening a brand-new tab to the same preview while still offline in that same warmed browser context also loaded the cached authenticated tracker shell successfully. This means the earlier chrome-error failure is environment-specific (likely another browser context, service-worker install state, or non-warmed path), not a universal failure of authenticated offline reload.
2026-03-22 closure note: the apparent offline-startup failure from an earlier parallel report was resolved as environment-specific after shared-browser validation. Exact copy checks passed on preview for 'Offline - changes will sync later', 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial', 'Nothing to sync!', and 'Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time.' Toast lifecycle was confirmed staged rather than abrupt. In the warmed non-isolated browser context, authenticated offline startup on the nextjs preview also preserved the cached tracker and showed 'Using cached data' on reload and on a fresh offline tab in the same warmed context. With the copy-sensitive acceptance surface satisfied, this bead can close; broader startup-cache parity questions remain on pt-780.1 if further environment comparison is needed.

title: Verify adherence and time semantics parity
description:

Issue:
Verify that adherence uses local-midnight day-difference with correct bucket colors, formatDateTimeWithZone is used for history timestamps, backdate rewrites both session and set timestamps, and 30-second polling cadence is preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
Adherence: normalize both the current time and performed_at to local midnight before computing day difference. 0 days = "Done today". Bucket colors: green for 1-3 days, orange warning for 4-7 days, red warning for 8+ days. History timestamps: formatDateTimeWithZone(...) must be used — no raw ISO strings. Backdate: rewrites both the session-level timestamp and each individual set's performed_at to the chosen value. Polling: first message check immediately after bootstrap, then every 30 seconds while signed in.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Both current time and performed_at are normalized to local midnight before day-difference computation
- [ ] 0-day difference renders as "Done today" (not "0 days ago")
- [ ] 1-3 day bucket renders with green styling
- [ ] 4-7 day bucket renders with orange warning styling
- [ ] 8+ day bucket renders with red warning styling
- [ ] formatDateTimeWithZone(...) is used for all history card timestamps
- [ ] Backdate rewrites both the session-level timestamp and each set's performed_at
- [ ] Message freshness check fires immediately after bootstrap then every 30 seconds

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the stored offline queue contains older session formats, the tracker must normalize them. The hold-bug collapse rule: multiple old sets with both reps AND seconds null must be collapsed into one hold set. This exact signature (reps null AND seconds null on multiple sets in the same session) is the trigger for collapse — not a generic "null set" condition. This migration must happen when the queue is loaded, before any sync attempt.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

- [ ] Queue migration runs when the stored queue is loaded, before any sync attempt
- [ ] Old-format queue entries are normalized to the current session shape
- [ ] Multiple sets with both reps: null AND seconds: null in the same session are collapsed into one hold set
- [ ] The collapse rule requires both reps AND seconds to be null (not just one field null)
- [ ] Normal sets (with reps or seconds values) are not affected by the collapse rule
- [ ] Migration handles the case where the queue is empty or absent gracefully

- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that reconnect updates connectivity/sync UI immediately, attempts queue sync when context is available, performs picker-only full refresh, and does not interrupt active logger or history views.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When connectivity returns: update connectivity state and sync UI immediately (even if the user is not on the picker). Attempt queue sync when authenticated state and patient context (viewingPatientId) exist. Full tracker data refresh happens only when the picker is active — if the user is in logger or history view, suppress the full picker refresh and instead hydrate cache or defer. After successful sync: reload history and hydrate cache. Immediate status feedback must be preserved even when the full refresh is suppressed.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Connectivity state and sync UI are updated immediately on reconnect regardless of current view
- [ ] Queue sync is attempted when authenticated state and viewingPatientId are both available
- [ ] Full picker data refresh happens only when the picker view is active
- [ ] Logger view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] History view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] After successful sync, history is reloaded and cache is hydrated

n/a
notes:
2026-03-22 Codex implementation pass from current thread: reconnect gap appears to be route-level policy, not basic queue sync. useIndexOfflineQueue can sync on online but cannot enforce picker-only refresh or suppress reload during active logger/history flows because it lacks route-state visibility. Plan: add a tracker-specific reconnect recovery hook on the index surface and keep generic queue persistence/sync logic in useIndexOfflineQueue.
2026-03-22 implementation update: extracted reconnect policy into hooks/useTrackerReconnectRecovery.js. The generic queue hook now optionally disables its built-in online listener, and the index route composes a tracker-specific reconnect policy that attempts sync immediately on online return, reloads only when the picker shell is safe to refresh, and defers that full refresh while timer/manual/history flows are active.
2026-03-22 pushed implementation on nextjs in commit 94a768c (Refine tracker reconnect recovery policy). Created shared verification child pt-780.3.1 and set it to block parent closure until preview runtime validation is complete.
2026-03-22 preview verification remains open through pt-780.3.1. Codex attempted the live pass after commit 94a768c, but the signed-in preview context was blocked by 'Failed to load programs (403)' and an empty picker, so reconnect behavior across populated picker/history/logger states could not be validated yet.
close_reason: Completed: reconnect parity verified through child pt-780.3.1 after implementation in commit 94a768c and blocker fix 9fd070c. Live preview evidence confirmed immediate online/offline status feedback, queue sync on reconnect, no forced picker refresh while on History, and picker refresh only when the Exercises surface was active and safe to reload.

title: Verify offline startup and cache fallback parity
description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline startup shows correct feedback when cache is present, shows explicit failed-load behavior when cache is absent, and scopes cache fallback to the resolved patient.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown when cache fallback is used
- [ ] Cache fallback is only attempted after viewingPatientId is resolved
- [ ] Cache is only used when it contains data for the resolved patient context (not any cached data)
- [ ] Explicit failed-load behavior (not a blank shell) is shown when no usable cache exists
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown when exercise load fails with no cache

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes:
2026-03-22 Codex reproduction note on current nextjs preview: on a fresh Chrome DevTools page at https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/, the active service worker/controller and cache are current (sw.js with CACHE_NAME pt-tracker-v14). Switching DevTools network emulation to Offline and reloading the unauthenticated root page kept the app shell alive and rendered the Sign In surface offline instead of falling through to chrome-error. This suggests the new failing runtime report is narrower than basic route-shell caching: the remaining failure is likely the authenticated tracker-startup path (or a different browser context) rather than a total loss of root SW navigation fallback.
2026-03-22 restart note: a non-isolated Chrome DevTools MCP entry has been added locally so the next Codex session can use a warmed shared browser context after restart. Resume with the authenticated nextjs preview path, not the unauthenticated shell: warm tracker context online, switch offline, reload, and compare the resulting navigation/controller/cache state against the successful unauthenticated root-shell case already noted here.
2026-03-22 shared-browser authenticated reproduction after restart: using the warmed non-isolated browser context, authenticated tracker offline startup succeeded on the nextjs preview. With tracker data already loaded online, switching DevTools to Offline and reloading preserved the tracker picker and showed 'Using cached data'. Opening a fresh new tab to the same preview while still offline in that warmed shared context also succeeded. The remaining discrepancy is therefore context-specific rather than a blanket authenticated-startup failure; compare any failing browser against this passing shared-context path (service-worker version/install state, cache presence, and whether the browser context was already warmed/authenticated).
2026-03-22 additional no-cache-path check in a clean isolated browser: cleared IndexedDB, caches, and the active service worker, reloaded online once to let the route shell reinstall its worker/cache, then switched Offline and reloaded. Result: the app still served the offline sign-in shell cleanly instead of falling through to chrome-error. This confirms the basic shell failure branch is not reproducing on the current preview in either clean or warmed contexts. The remaining unverified portion of pt-780.1 is the signed-in/no-usable-tracker-cache branch (explicit failed-load messaging when tracker bootstrap data is absent), which requires preserving auth while clearing only tracker data stores.
2026-03-22 investigation update: reproduced the signed-in no-usable-IDB-cache branch setup by preserving auth_state and users while deleting ui_state.tracker_bootstrap plus activity_logs/exercises/programs in the browser. Result: normal offline reload and even a fresh offline tab still restored the tracker successfully instead of showing the explicit failed-load state. Cache Storage only contained shell assets plus Supabase auth/user, not tracker API payloads, so the repopulation appears to be coming from another warmed browser cache layer for /api/exercises, /api/programs, and /api/logs rather than IndexedDB. A hard reload / ignore-cache path can fall through to chrome-error, but that is not the normal user path. Practical takeaway: warmed authenticated offline startup is working; the remaining unresolved branch is specifically the browser-cache-purged no-usable-data case, which may need a separate acceptance decision rather than a blind code change.
close_reason: Consolidated into pt-ltvm cutover gate (verification checklist).

- [ ] Delete confirmation wording is "Are you sure you want to delete this entire session? This cannot be undone."
- [ ] Delete uses DELETE /api/logs?id=...

Sided detection uses either the exercise definition or the saved set data (not just one source). When a session exists but has no sets, the modal shows "No sets logged" rather than hiding the modal. Added sets are seeded from exercise defaults, default sided sets to right, and stamp a fresh performed_at. Deleted sets trigger set_number renumbering before re-render.

- [ ] "Distance (ft)" field is shown for distance sets
- [ ] "Side" field is shown only for sided sets (not bilateral)
- [ ] Sided detection uses both the exercise definition and the saved set data (fallback from either)
- [ ] Form-parameter fields are shown per-set only when form_data exists on the saved set
- [ ] "No sets logged" is shown when a session exists but its set array is empty
- [ ] Newly added sets are seeded from exercise defaults, sided sets default to right, and have a fresh performed_at

API request types: GET for load/poll, POST for send, PATCH for read/archive, DELETE for undo-send.

- [ ] Undo Send confirmation shows "Delete this message? It will be removed for both you and your PT."
- [ ] Undo Send uses DELETE request and shows success or failure feedback
- [ ] Message API uses GET for load/poll, POST for send, PATCH for read/archive, DELETE for undo-send

title: Verify logger copy and speech feedback parity
description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance

Issue:
Verify that all logger progress copy, speech cues, milestone thresholds, countdown warnings, and save feedback strings match the static baseline exactly.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
The spec preserves these exact copy and speech strings: "Working left side", "Working right side", "Rep X of N", "Duration Exercise", "Target: N seconds", "5 reps left", "3 reps left", "Last rep", "Set complete", "All sets complete", "Rep N complete" (midpoint feedback), "Saved (with notes)", "Saved (no notes)". Speech queue must be cleared when stale announcements would play after state changes. Countdown warnings fire at specific near-zero thresholds. "All sets complete" is an explicit spoken cue, not a silent stop. The all-sets-complete trigger fires from selection/progress check, not from post-save flow.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Progress labels "Working left side" and "Working right side" are shown when that side is active
- [ ] Progress label "Rep X of N" is shown during counting
- [ ] Milestone speech fires at "5 reps left", "3 reps left", "Last rep", and "Set complete" thresholds
- [ ] "All sets complete" is spoken explicitly when all sets are done — not a silent completion
- [ ] "Rep N complete" midpoint feedback appears during timer-driven hold progression
- [ ] Save feedback is "Saved (with notes)" when notes are present, "Saved (no notes)" when absent
- [ ] Stale queued speech is cleared when side switches or progress state changes
- [ ] Near-zero countdown warning fires at the static threshold (not as a vague generic effect)

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

n/a
notes:
2026-03-18 timer/audio closeout note: the speech/cue slice is no longer the active blocker here. The following acceptance items should be treated as satisfied by the completed timer/audio implementation and verification work under ptrebuild-ryf plus closed child beads ptrebuild-9v1.2 through ptrebuild-9v1.7: progress labels Working left side / Working right side when active; milestone speech at 5 reps left, 3 reps left, Last rep, and Set complete; near-zero countdown warning behavior; pause/speech cue policy as revised by product decision; delayed comparison speech after confirmation using the approved replacement model. Non-blocking enhancement items were deferred separately (initial side-open speech cue and richer duration-side phrasing). Items not explicitly covered by this note still need separate confirmation if this parent remains open, including broader copy/state items such as All sets complete, Rep N complete midpoint feedback, exact save feedback strings, and any global stale-speech queue cases outside the verified timer/audio slice.

Current code now covers: `All sets complete` via hooks/useLoggerFeedback.js after session-progress completion check; `Rep N complete` midpoint feedback in components/TimerPanel.js for hold progression before final completion; exact `Saved (with notes)` / `Saved (no notes)` success copy via hooks/useLoggerFeedback.js and pages/index.js. The tracker feedback ownership was split cleanly so persistence stays in useSessionLogging.js, timer mechanics stay in lib/logger-timer-machine.js / timer hooks, and tracker-wide spoken/text feedback lives in hooks/useLoggerFeedback.js. README was updated to document that ownership. Validation completed: next build passes on branch nextjs after commit 7d2d0fd. Remaining question for final parent closure is preview-level confirmation, not missing implementation.
2026-03-18 preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app confirmed the remaining parent items. Verified exact save feedback with real logged sessions: Single Knee to Chest saved without notes showed `Saved (no notes)` and updated history count; Double Knee To Chest saved with note `no pain` showed `Saved (with notes)`. Verified session-complete speech on Single Knee to Chest by logging right then left side; instrumented browser captured `All sets complete` after second side completed. Verified hold midpoint feedback without saving additional data on Ankle Inversion — Isometric by letting timed reps complete; panel advanced from Rep 1 to Rep 4 and a DOM observer captured transient status text `Rep 3 complete`. This closes the remaining unconfirmed copy/speech items for this bead.

title: Verify undo and reset parity
description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open

Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-logging

Issue:
Verify that Previous Set removes only the most recent accepted set, timer reset clears only live progress, blocked undo shows correct copy, and undo feedback names the removed set number.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
Previous Set (previous-set action) removes only the most recently accepted set from currentSession.sets. When currentSession.sets is empty, the action must be blocked and show "No sets to undo" rather than silently doing nothing. Undo success feedback must identify the removed set number (not a generic "set removed" message). Timer reset clears live timer progress only — accepted sets in currentSession.sets are untouched. Live counter or timer progress is separate from accepted sets and is not affected by Previous Set.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Previous Set removes only the most recently accepted set from currentSession.sets
- [ ] Previous Set when no accepted sets exist shows "No sets to undo" (not a silent no-op)
- [ ] Undo success feedback identifies the removed set number specifically
- [ ] Timer reset clears live timer progress only — currentSession.sets is unaffected
- [ ] Live counter value is not removed by Previous Set — only accepted sets are affected

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

- [ ] Hold manual entry with zero seconds is blocked with "Please enter seconds per rep"
- [ ] Timer-driven hold midpoint shows "Rep N complete" and returns without appending a full set when reps remain
- [ ] Saved payload includes distance_feet: null, partial_rep: false, and manual_log set correctly (true for direct manual, false for timer-driven)

- [ ] Switching side speaks "Working left side" or "Working right side"
- [ ] logSetSelectedSide is initialized from currentSide when the Log Set modal opens
- [ ] logSetSelectedSide is cleared when the Log Set modal closes or a new modal open occurs
- [ ] Log Set saves use logSetSelectedSide for the set's side value, not currentSide directly

notes: 2026-03-22 follow-up preview verification after commit ea0203c: the side-reset regression is fixed. Remaining unverified scope on this parity bead is limited to bilateral/null-side behavior because this test account did not expose a non-sided/bilateral exercise in this pass.
close_reason: Closed after runtime parity verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Sided flow passed on Long Arc Quad: side switch speech worked, Log Set inherited the active side, Save Set preserved Working left side, and Next Set preserved Working left side. Bilateral/non-sided flow passed on test-patient2 with Double Knee To Chest: no side controls rendered in the tracker panel and the Log Set modal showed only Reps and Seconds with no side selector, matching null-side semantics rather than inventing a both/current-side value.

title: Verify timer mode parity
description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open

Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-logging
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Verify that timer mode counts down from target, hold mode advances reps within a set, duration mode is single-run, and the five-part surface is preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
Timer mode must count down (not up) from the configured target. Hold exercises: each completed countdown rep advances live rep progress, resets the timer display to full target duration, auto-pauses on completion, and defers set acceptance until the configured rep count is satisfied or the user manually logs. Duration exercises: one countdown run produces one rep with captured seconds — single-run semantics, not multi-rep. The five-part timer surface must include: rep/duration info line, timer display, target label, start/pause button, and reset button. Reset clears live timer progress without deleting accepted sets.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Timer counts down from the configured target, not up
- [ ] Timer surface has five named parts: rep/duration info line, timer display, target label, start/pause button, reset button
- [ ] Hold: completed countdown rep auto-pauses and advances live rep count without appending an accepted set
- [ ] Hold: timer display resets to full target duration after each rep completes
- [ ] Hold: set is not accepted until target rep count is satisfied or user manually logs
- [ ] Duration: one countdown run produces one rep with captured seconds (single-run, not multi-rep hold)
- [ ] Timer reset clears live timer progress only — accepted sets in currentSession.sets are unaffected

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Remaining open scope on this parent, if any, is broader logger/timer acceptance outside the now-closed timer/audio cue slice.

Browser-runtime capture after 11 taps recorded spoken cues [5 reps left, 3 reps left, Last rep, Set complete] and audio frequencies [440 x11 taps, then 1000/1200/1400 completion triple], confirming per-tap soft tick plus milestone speech and set-complete triple beep on the live page.

Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec requires history cards to show the performed date and time formatted via the named path formatDateTimeWithZone(...) — not raw database timestamp strings. Each card must also show exercise name, set summary text, first-set parameter preview when present, and optional notes preview. When history is empty the copy must be "No history yet. Start logging exercises!" and when history fails to load the copy must be "Failed to load history."

- [ ] History is rendered before picker exercise cards are rendered

title: Verify role and patient-context resolution
description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec requires /api/users to be called first during authenticated bootstrap, matching the signed-in user by auth id or email, setting currentUserRole and currentUserProfileId, deriving viewingPatientId, determining therapistId fallback, and syncing currentEmailNotifyEnabled before continuing. Patient mode uses self data with therapist fallback when assigned; therapist mode resolves patient-viewing context and shows the patient banner; admin mode behaves like patient mode when therapist-linked or falls back to patient-viewing context. Missing patient context must block normal exercise and history bootstrap.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] /api/users is the first call during authenticated bootstrap, before /api/programs or /api/logs
- [ ] User is matched by auth id or email from the /api/users response
- [ ] currentUserRole, currentUserProfileId, viewingPatientId, and therapistId are all set before data loading continues
- [ ] currentEmailNotifyEnabled is synced from the /api/users response before continuing
- [ ] Patient banner is shown for therapist mode with correct "Viewing exercises for:" text
- [ ] Admin mode behaves like patient mode when therapist-linked, else uses patient-viewing fallback
- [ ] If no valid patient context can be resolved, normal exercise and history bootstrap is blocked

The spec requires three distinct auth surfaces: sign-in (titleless auth card with email input, password input, inline error area, submit button, forgot-password link), forgot-password (explanatory sentence, inline success or error area), and new-password (new-password and confirm-password inputs, inline error handling). Labels "Sign In", "Forgot password?", "Reset Password", "Send Reset Link", "Back to sign in", "Set New Password", "Update Password" must all be present. Pending states "Sending..." and "Updating..." and success toast "Password updated successfully!" must be preserved exactly.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code

- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

- [ ] All labels present: "Sign In", "Forgot password?", "Reset Password", "Send Reset Link", "Back to sign in", "Set New Password", "Update Password"
- [ ] Forgot-password submit shows "Sending..." pending state; new-password submit shows "Updating..." pending state
- [ ] Inline copy "Check your email for the reset link." appears on reset submit success
- [ ] Inline copy "Passwords do not match." appears when passwords are mismatched
- [ ] Success toast "Password updated successfully!" appears on completed password update

- [ ] Header remains sticky and persistent when user switches between picker, logger, and history views

title: Add shared input normalization modes for reusable fields
description:
Source Reference: follow-up design request from user on 2026-03-17
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,forms,input,normalization,refactor,ui
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/lib/
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Design and implement a shared input-normalization mechanism so reusable form fields can explicitly choose how free-text input should be normalized.

Context:
This came up while defining the shared select-plus-`Other...` component/module. Some fields may allow free-text entry and need a consistent normalization rule, such as lowercase, title case, sentence case, or no normalization. That rule should not be reimplemented ad hoc inside each page or field.

Required Direction:
- Add a shared mechanism that lets a field declare its normalization mode.
- Expected modes include at least:
  - `none`
  - `lower`
  - `title`
  - `sentence`
- This should be designed as a separate concern from dropdown interaction behavior, but it must be easy to use together with shared select/`Other...` components.
- Prefer a small shared helper/module plus a clear component API over page-local string manipulation.

Examples:
- A select-with-`Other...` field may allow `Other...` and normalize entered text to lowercase.
- Another field may allow free text but preserve title case.
- A plain text field may opt into the same normalization mechanism without using a dropdown at all.

Constraints/Caveats:
Do not silently force one normalization rule on all fields. The call site must explicitly choose the mode. Preserve the ability for some fields to opt out entirely.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Shared normalization mechanism exists
- [ ] Field/API surface allows explicit per-field normalization choice
- [ ] Works cleanly with shared select/`Other...` field behavior
- [ ] Fields can opt out with `none`

Dependencies:
- related: pt-ant

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Next.js SessionLoggerModal shows a 'Both' option in the side selector, but DB confirms 'both' is never stored — only 'left', 'right', or null. Static app hides side selector for bilateral exercises (pattern !== 'side') and logs side=null. Next.js modal must match: hide selector for bilateral, omit side from payload.

Context:
Discovered during DN-058 side checklist review (2026-03-03). Confirmed via backup JSON (823 sets): distinct side values are 'right', 'left', null — 'both' never appears. Static index.html: isSided = exercise.pattern === 'side'; bilateral exercises get currentSide=null and side selector hidden. Next.js SessionLoggerModal always shows left/right/both when hasSide is true — incorrect for bilateral pattern. Fix: when exercise.pattern !== 'side', hide side selector and omit side from set payload (or send null).

title: [DN-056] In-session last-used form_data parity is still missing for panel-seeded/create flows
description: Static getLastUsedParamValue(exerciseId,paramName,side) checks currentSession before persisted history, and showLogSetModal/showNextSetModal call renderFormParameterFields each time the modal opens. Current Next.js computes default_form_data from logs only when the exercise is selected and then reuses that snapshot for handleTimerApplySet and handleTimerOpenManual. SessionLoggerModal historicalFormParams also comes from collectGlobalParameterValues(logs), which excludes current in-session sets until reload. Result: after a user changes a form parameter earlier in the same session, later create/next-set/panel-seeded logs can keep stale defaults/options instead of the latest in-session value.
notes:
2026-03-13 stop-point: not implemented yet in this thread. Evidence from code + review: Next.js still seeds default_form_data once at exercise select and derives historical options from persisted logs, so same-session changes can remain stale until reload. Expected implementation surface next session: pages/index.js handoff/state + lib/session-form-params.js helpers, with possible SessionLoggerModal wiring if current-set values need to refresh options live. Do not assign this as a separate implementation thread from ptrebuild-1qq.2 because both touch the same files.
2026-03-21 implementation in progress: session-form-params helper layer now prefers current in-session accepted sets before persisted history, and tracker/manual wiring now requests fresh form-parameter defaults/options from live tracker state instead of reusing the exercise-select snapshot. Runtime verification still pending.
2026-03-21 preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app using the live patient account confirms in-session last-used parity. On Ankle Dorsiflexion (Theraband), Log Set reopened with Resistance Band prefilled to blue immediately after saving a draft right-side set in the same session, without requiring a page reload or server save.
close_reason: Implemented and preview-verified on 2026-03-21

title: [DN-056] Side-aware form-parameter defaults/history parity is still missing in Next.js logging flow
description: Static index form-parameter helpers accept and apply side for sided exercises: showLogSetModal/renderFormParameterFields pass the current side, getHistoricalParamValues(exerciseId,paramName,side) filters by side, and getLastUsedParamValue(exerciseId,paramName,side) filters currentSession/history by side. Current Next.js form-parameter helpers are side-agnostic: collectGlobalParameterValues(logs) is global-only, getLastUsedParameterDisplay(logs,exerciseId,paramName) has no side input, buildDefaultFormDataForExercise(exercise,logs) has no side input, pages/index.js seeds default_form_data once per selected exercise, and SessionLoggerModal renders historicalFormParams[paramName] without recomputing when set.side changes. Result: left/right sided logging can prefill or offer the wrong form_data values.
notes:
2026-03-13 stop-point: not implemented yet in this thread. Evidence from static review: public/index.html passes side into form-parameter history/default helpers, while current Next.js helpers remain side-agnostic. Expected implementation surface next session: lib/session-form-params.js for side-aware history/default lookup, pages/index.js for side-aware seeding in panel/manual/next-set flows, and possibly SessionLoggerModal if per-set side changes must refresh options/defaults. Do not split this into a separate implementation thread from ptrebuild-1qq.1 because both converge in the same helper/state path.
2026-03-21 implementation in progress: session-form-params helper layer now supports side-aware history/default lookup, SessionLoggerModal now reads side-scoped option lists for sided exercises, and manual/next-set tracker flows rebuild form-parameter context when side changes. Runtime verification still pending.

In the same draft session for Ankle Dorsiflexion (Theraband), Right reopened with Resistance Band = blue, switching the Side field to Left immediately changed the default to black, and reopening again preserved the distinct left/right values.

title: Next.js /pt-view removes static Undo Send flow and replaces it with a non-restoring archive undo banner
description:
Source Reference: QA review discovered this session.

Static parity:
- sent messages can expose an Undo Send action that deletes the message within the allowed window
- archive/hide is a separate action

Current Next.js bug:
- components/MessagesModal.js only exposes Archive in the message actions
- after archive, the modal shows an Undo banner, but the code comment confirms this Undo only dismisses the banner and does not restore the message
- hooks/useMessages.js still exposes deleteMessage/remove, but the modal never wires a delete/undo-send action to it

Evidence:
- public/pt_view.html:2005-2006
- public/pt_view.html:2341-2342
- public/pt_view.html:2480-2492
- components/MessagesModal.js:88-98
- components/MessagesModal.js:165-182
- hooks/useMessages.js:83-87

Impact:
The migrated modal lost a static message-management path and its replacement Undo affordance is misleading because it cannot actually reverse the archive.
notes: Evidence review 2026-04-04: components/MessagesModal.js:125-132 implements Undo Send as a confirmation-backed delete action, and 242-247 renders the Undo Send button. app/(protected)/TrackerPage.js:460-478 wires onRemove={msgs.remove}, and hooks/useMessages.js:124-127 maps remove() to deleteMessage(token, messageId). Closing as completed.
close_reason: Closed after truth-check: pt-view now exposes a real Undo Send action wired to the message delete path.

title: Agent Ops Epic: tracker/tooling friction log
description: Purpose: shared operational issue sink for Codex/Claude when tooling/process friction reduces delivery speed or reliability (Beads runtime issues, merge/handoff pain, tracker drift, branch hygiene friction, CI friction). Rules: (1) create discovered-from child issues for concrete incidents, (2) include root cause + mitigation, (3) close child after fix, (4) keep epic open as longitudinal signal for whether Beads remains best-fit. Scope: agent operations only, not product features.
notes:
2026-03-13 Beads process feedback from active multi-agent migration work:
- Likes: parent/child issue structure worked well for carving off parity slices without bloating the main tasks; closure-block notes made recovery easy; Beads history was useful for reconstructing prior verification context.
- Gripes: auto-push metadata churn can leave the local Dolt working set dirty even when the actual issue content is already synced, which then blocks bd dolt pull with a 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes' message until metadata is committed. Concurrent writers also make non-fast-forward auto-push failures common.
- Operational lesson: implementation should stay single-threaded on shared files while review/verification can run in parallel threads; Beads updates are safest when one thread owns the active issue subtree.
- Decision input for later: revisit whether current Beads + Dolt auto-push behavior is worth keeping as-is, or whether the workflow should standardize a clearer sync/commit step when multiple agents are active.
2026-03-13 follow-up after bd 0.60.0 update: the doc/tooling refresh work is resolved via ptrebuild-3di. Local beads mirror was refreshed, repo workflow docs were updated, stale bd sync references were removed from the local workflow, and a derived BEADS_QUICKREF.md generator was added so future command drift is less likely. Active bd now resolves to 0.60.0 from C:\Users\cindi\go\bin. Remaining friction not yet resolved: Windows can still leave the old AppData bd.exe locked on disk even after PATH cleanup, and multi-writer Dolt metadata churn still needs observation in real usage.

description:
Source DN: DN-050
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Agent: codex

Issue:
Phase 4f: Add exercise sort mode selector + drag-and-drop manual ordering to ExercisePicker. Sort modes: custom/manual, body area, recent activity, alphabetical, PT/program order fallback. Persist preference to localStorage (user-scoped key). Drag-and-drop: pointer events, onPointerUp, 44px grip handle, drag ghost while moving.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js, pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.module.css

Context:
Part of Phase 4 index.html migration (DN-045). Resolves DN-012. Sort preference key: pt_sort_mode_${userId}. Custom order key: pt_exercise_order_${userId}. No onclick/click handlers — iOS-safe only. Claude reviews and fixes if needed, then verifies on iOS preview.

Constraints/Caveats:
No onClick or click events. onPointerUp only. touch-action: manipulation. Drag ghost must be visible during move. localStorage keys must include userId to be user-scoped.
notes:
2026-03-21 implementation in progress: added a dedicated tracker sort-state hook plus pure exercise-order helpers so user-scoped sort mode and manual order persist outside pages/index.js. ExercisePicker now owns the visible manual-order UI with a six-dot grip handle, drag ghost, and pointer-driven reordering for manual mode. Local next build passed before the final grip-style polish; preview verification still pending.
2026-03-21 follow-up fix: initial preview test exposed an iOS-style drag lifecycle gap where the ghost could enter a stuck state and background content could still receive interaction. Updated ExercisePicker so manual reorder enters full drag mode immediately, locks background touch interaction during drag, routes move/release through the drag overlay plus window fallback, and cancels back to the original order when release happens outside the list bounds. Local next build passed after this fix; fresh preview retest is next.
2026-03-21 follow-up fix: manual reorder now requires a touch-and-hold on the six-dot grip before drag mode activates. Moving before the hold delay cancels the reorder attempt so normal scrolling does not accidentally pick items up. Preview retest pending after deploy.
2026-03-21 persistence follow-up: manual reorder was not surviving reopen because the stored order could be overwritten before the user-scoped sort state finished hydrating. Scoped fix is in useExerciseSortState so localStorage writes wait until the current user's stored values have been read first. Preview retest pending after deploy.
2026-03-21 reopen persistence root cause confirmed in live preview: the manual-order key was restoring correctly, but the hook rewrote the default order over it during reload before restored state had fully settled. Updated useExerciseSortState so write-back stays disabled until the live sort mode and normalized manual order match the restored user-scoped values.
2026-03-21 second reopen fix: live retest showed hydration was still arming too early while exercises were temporarily empty, which let the default order overwrite the stored manual order before data bootstrap completed. Updated useExerciseSortState so hydrate/write-back stay blocked until the exercise list is populated for the current user.
2026-03-21 restore bug root cause fixed: useExerciseSortState was normalizing manualOrderIds against an empty exercise list on reload, which collapsed the restored saved order to [] before real data arrived. The normalization step now stays off until exercises are loaded, so restored ids survive long enough to be applied.
2026-03-21 final restore race fix in progress: live React inspection showed localStorage still held the custom order, but useExerciseSortState was feeding ExercisePicker a default-order manualOrderIds array because the normalization effect ran during the same mount cycle as the restore effect and won the race. Added a hydration gate so normalization cannot run until the current user's restored state is settled.
2026-03-21 preview verification after ad5bb6f: confirmed restored manual order now aligns across user-scoped localStorage, useExerciseSortState manualOrderIds, and rendered ExercisePicker cards after reload. Live preview came back in the saved custom order (Figure-four stretch, Seated Thoracic Rotation, Abdominal Hollow with Marching, Long Arc Quad, Ball Squeeze, ...), proving the restore path is no longer snapping back to default order during hydration. A synthetic desktop drag attempt in Playwright did not change the order further, so remaining validation is real-device iOS touch reorder + reopen persistence.
close_reason: 2026-03-21 implemented, pushed, and verified. Preview verification confirmed restored manual order aligns across localStorage, hook state, and rendered list after reload. User confirmed on iOS that touch-and-hold reorder now persists across close/reopen. Any future reorder polish can reopen as a new bead.

description:
Source DN: DN-063

Agent: claude

Next.js SessionLoggerModal shows a 'Both' option in the side selector, but DB confirms 'both' is never stored — only 'left', 'right', or null. pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js

Discovered during DN-058 side checklist review (2026-03-03). Confirmed via backup JSON (823 sets): distinct side values are 'right', 'left', null — 'both' never appears. Static index.html: isSided = exercise.pattern === 'side'; bilateral exercises get currentSide=null and side selector hidden. title: [DN-067] Offline resilience parity gap: app-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced entries, and offline cold-open can fail with "Offline and no cached data available" when cache hydration has not previously occurred.
description:
Source DN: DN-067
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Agent: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, sync, reliability, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js

Issue:
App-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced offline queue entries. The 'online' event listener only fires when the device comes back online mid-session, not on initial mount.

Context:
The offline write queue (pt_offline_queue_${userId}) correctly accumulates sessions when offline and syncs when the 'online' event fires. But if the app is opened fresh while already online with queued items, sync never triggers — entries stay stranded until the user manually refreshes or goes offline/online.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve idempotency — sync already uses client_mutation_id and treats 409 as success. Do not re-introduce /api/sync. Queue is user-scoped; must check userId before syncing.

Specific Fix:
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
In the useEffect that registers the 'online' event listener, add a mount-time sync check:
  - After registering the listener, check if navigator.onLine === true AND queueRef.current.length > 0
  - If so, call sync() immediately
  - This runs once on mount (after queue loads from localStorage), catching stranded entries
Note: queue loads in a separate useEffect dependent on userId — mount-time sync must fire after that useEffect runs. Best approach: add a separate useEffect dependent on [queue, sync] that checks navigator.onLine on the first render once queue is populated.

Validation Checklist:
- Queue items present + app opened online → sync fires on mount, items cleared
- Queue empty + app opened online → no unnecessary sync call
- App opened offline → no sync attempted (navigator.onLine false)
- Online event mid-session still works as before

Dependencies: child of ptrebuild-nyn

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.3) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of mount-time sync/reconnect behavior; pt-780.3 remains the final acceptance surface for reconnect parity.
close_reason: Closed
