title: Code review: hooks/useTimerPanelOutput.js + lib/timer-panel.js
description: Review finding for Claude SRP split: timer panel output hook and lib additions.
notes:
## Review findings for hooks/useTimerPanelOutput.js and lib/timer-panel.js

### hooks/useTimerPanelOutput.js — Clean

**Responsibility:** Display text and apply-payload shaping for TimerPanel (`repInfoText`, `targetDoseText`, `buildSetPatch`, `canApply`).

No findings. All four values are derived from inputs (executionState, timer outputs, exercise/mode/targetReps/targetSeconds). No state owned, no effects, no side effects. Correct layer.

The hook receives `executionState` (which includes `_resetToken` and `exerciseId` from the execution state hook) and uses it in the `canApply` spread — this is the same `_resetToken` bleed-through noted in the useTimerExecutionState review. That finding lives on pt-u5mn.3.12.

### lib/timer-panel.js

**What changed:**
1. `getDurationCompletionSpeech` function added (lines 70-86) — pure, no imports needed, correctly placed.
2. Cosmetic reformatting: 4-space indent changed to 2-space throughout the entire file.

**Finding: INFO — Cosmetic reformat adds diff noise**

The reformatting of the existing functions (getExerciseMode, getTargetReps, getTargetSeconds, etc.) generates a large diff that is unrelated to the functional change. This makes the staged diff harder to audit — a reviewer must scan through reformatting to identify the one real addition (`getDurationCompletionSpeech`). No behavioral change, but it violates the principle of minimal-scope changes and makes future `git blame` less useful for the reformatted lines.

If the indent change was driven by a repo linter running on commit, this is acceptable. If it was done manually, the reformatting should have been a separate commit or omitted.

### useTimerSpeech.js — Clean (integration hook)

The integration hook is now 82 lines and genuinely thin. It:
- Derives shared inputs (mode, isSided, exerciseId, targetReps, targetSeconds, audio)
- Composes four sub-hooks: useTimerExecutionState, useExerciseTimer, useTimerAudio, useTimerPanelOutput
- Returns a flat public API

The rename in the return (`buildCurrentSetPatch: buildSetPatch`) correctly preserves the original public API name while using the cleaner internal name from useTimerPanelOutput. No callers need to change.

One structural note: `useTimerSpeech` still derives mode/isSided/exerciseId/targetReps/targetSeconds inline and passes them to both useTimerExecutionState and useTimerPanelOutput. This is correct — useTimerSpeech owns the shared-input computation layer and both sub-hooks are downstream consumers. No concern.

### Docs

README.md entries for useTimerExecutionState and useTimerPanelOutput are accurate. lib/timer-panel.js entry updated to mention getDurationCompletionSpeech. IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS updated to document ownership hierarchy. All correct.

### Approval verdict
**useTimerPanelOutput.js: Ready for approval.**
**lib/timer-panel.js: Ready for approval with the cosmetic-reformat noise noted.**
**useTimerSpeech.js: Ready for approval — it is genuinely thin and correctly composed.**
close_reason: _resetToken spread into getCanApply fixed (explicit fields passed). lib/timer-panel.js getDurationCompletionSpeech addition clean. Committed 45d92d3.

title: Add temporary offline debug logs to useUserContext and useIndexData
description: Add console.log statements to trace the offline bootstrap sequence: session resolution, users store clear, getCachedUsers result, and patientId availability. Marked for easy removal after verification.
notes:
Logs are wrapped with:
  // --- OFFLINE DEBUG: remove after verifying pt-lqql ---
  // --- END OFFLINE DEBUG ---
Remove with: grep -r 'OFFLINE DEBUG' hooks/ to find all blocks.

Locations:
1. hooks/useUserContext.js — effect entry: logs session (bool) and hadSession (bool) each render
2. hooks/useUserContext.js — null-session branch: logs 'CLEARING users store (sign-out path)' — should NOT appear on initial offline load after fix
3. hooks/useUserContext.js — after getCachedUsers: logs count of users returned from IDB and navigator.onLine
4. hooks/useIndexData.js — effect entry: logs token (bool), patientId (bool), hadAuth (bool) each run
5. hooks/useIndexData.js — bail path (!token || !patientId): logs 'BAILING' message
6. hooks/useIndexData.js — hadAuth clear path: logs 'CLEARING IDB caches' — should NOT appear on initial offline load
7. hooks/useIndexData.js — reload() bootstrap read: logs getCachedTrackerBootstrap hit/miss and patientId match

Expected healthy offline sequence:
  [useUserContext] effect, session: false, hadSession: false   (initial render)
  [useUserContext] effect, session: true, hadSession: false    (auth resolved)
  [useUserContext] getCachedUsers: N, online: false/true       (N > 0 confirms fix)
  [useIndexData] effect, token: true, patientId: true          (both deps present)
  [useIndexData] reload: getCachedTrackerBootstrap: hit        (data loads from IDB)
close_reason: Added console.log blocks marked with OFFLINE DEBUG / END OFFLINE DEBUG comments referencing pt-lqql for easy grep-and-remove.

title: Cross-check Vercel function count via CLI and build output
description:
Validate the dashboard-reported function count using the CLI inspect path and local build-output inspection. Compare what Vercel deploys with what .vercel/output/functions contains for this Next.js app.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attempt CLI deployment inspection and report whether it exposes function inventory clearly.
- Run local Vercel build if feasible and inspect .vercel/output/functions.
- Compare results to the dashboard function list.
- No repo code changes.
notes: Tried both alternate verification paths. Option 2 (ercel inspect https://nextjs.pttracker.app) failed locally because the Vercel CLI token in this shell is invalid: 'The specified token is not valid. Use vercel login to generate a new token.' Option 3 (ercel build) also failed locally before build output generation because project settings are not pulled in this shell: 'No Project Settings found locally. Run vercel pull --yes to retrieve them.' Cross-check via the Vercel MCP/connector also returned auth required on get_project. Conclusion: the dashboard Resources tab remains the only working source of truth available in this environment right now. If we want CLI/build-output inspection to be repeatable later, we need valid Vercel auth plus pulled project settings in the shell environment first.
close_reason: Attempted CLI/build-output cross-check; both blocked by Vercel auth/setup in this environment

title: Verify Vercel function-count implications of App Router API routes
description:
Check whether the current Next.js app/api route handler count in pttracker meaningfully consumes Vercel function slots or whether bundling means the practical function count is lower. Use official Vercel/Next documentation plus current repo route shape to answer the operator question in plain language.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Verify current guidance from official docs or primary-source discussion.
- Relate the answer to this repo's current app/api route shape.
- Record whether adding another route such as profile editor is likely safe.
- No code changes.
notes:
Official-source check complete. Vercel docs (Functions -> Runtimes, updated 2026-02-18) explicitly say: when using Next.js or SvelteKit on Vercel, dynamic code (APIs, server-rendered pages, or dynamic fetch requests) is bundled into the fewest number of Vercel Functions possible, so it is unlikely to hit the Hobby limit of 12 bundled functions per deployment. Docs also say the 12 limit applies directly when using Vercel Functions without a framework, where each api file maps 1:1 to a function. Repo relevance: current app/api surface contains 11 route.js handlers under app/api, but that should NOT be read as 11 guaranteed Vercel Function slots consumed. Claude's practical conclusion is directionally right: route count alone is not the limiting metric for this Next.js app, and adding another route such as a profile editor is unlikely to be blocked by a 12-function limit. Important caution: official docs do not promise an exact bundle count for this repo, so avoid overclaiming '1-3 actual functions' unless we inspect real Vercel build output or deployment internals.
Vercel connector cross-check via codex_apps succeeded. Current production deployment dpl_CuSToFdXUweFx2t9BB46PvGLCqA2 (commit c440628...) reports meta.lambdaRuntimeStats = {\ nodejs\:2}. Previous production deployments showed larger counts (for example 10 on 18edff6-era builds). Important nuance: the dashboard Resources tab can list many route/function endpoints, but deployment metadata may still show a much smaller number of underlying Node lambda bundles. For this app, latest deployed truth from the Vercel connector is 2 Node lambdas on production after the app/api cleanup/folding work. This supports Claude's broad thesis more strongly than the earlier dashboard-only reading, while still leaving the UI/function-list semantics a bit confusing.
Cross-check refinement after working Vercel connector path: dashboard Resources view and deployment metadata answer different questions. Resources can list many route-level function entries, while deployment meta.lambdaRuntimeStats reflects the underlying bundled Node lambda count. For operator guidance, prefer lambdaRuntimeStats when the question is Hobby function-limit headroom; use Resources when the question is route-level surface/invocation identity.
close_reason: Verified official Vercel guidance and recorded repo-specific conclusion

title: Research: Vercel Hobby 12-function limit and Next.js bundling behavior
description: Investigate whether our current 11 app/api route files actually count as 11 Vercel Functions on Hobby plan, or whether Next.js App Router bundling collapses them. Research alternative strategies (catch-all routes, edge middleware, etc.) to understand our real headroom.
notes:
RESEARCH COMPLETE 2026-03-30. Sources: vercel.com/docs/limits, vercel.com/docs/functions/runtimes, GitHub Discussion #5458 (Vercel maintainer), Vercel Community forum threads.

KEY FINDING — THE 12-LIMIT ALMOST CERTAINLY DOES NOT APPLY TO US:
Vercel docs say the limit is 'Framework-dependent*' for Next.js (not a hard 12). The asterisk links to the Runtimes page which states: 'When using Next.js on Vercel, dynamic code will be bundled into the fewest number of Vercel Functions possible. Because of this, it is unlikely that you will hit the limit of 12 bundled Vercel Functions per deployment.' A Vercel maintainer confirmed in GitHub Discussion #5458: all Next.js API routes equate to 'a single Serverless Function' in most cases. Bundle splits only happen if a route's dependencies push it over 50MB.

WHAT ACTUALLY COUNTS:
- Next.js App Router route handlers: bundled together, NOT 1 function per file
- Raw /api/ files WITHOUT a framework: 1 function per file (this is what the 12 limit was for)
- Edge Middleware (middleware.js): does NOT count against the 12 limit — separate fluid compute billing
- Edge runtime option on route handlers: same count as node runtime, just different execution environment

OUR SITUATION: We have 11 app/api/*/route.js files in a Next.js App Router project. They almost certainly compile to 1-3 Vercel Functions total (bundled), not 11. We were likely not in danger of hitting the limit even at 12.

ALTERNATIVE STRATEGIES RESEARCHED (for reference):
1. Catch-all [...slug]/route.js — single file dispatches on slug[0] and request.method. Useful for non-framework or explicit bundling control. Not necessary for us.
2. Next.js Middleware — can short-circuit requests before serverless functions, useful for auth gating. Does not count against 12. Already using via proxy.js.
3. Edge runtime — does not provide a separate limit quota, just a different runtime environment.

RECOMMENDATION: No changes needed to our API structure. The 11-route consolidation we did was good for code clarity and maintainability, but was not actually required to stay under Vercel's limit. The profile editor endpoint (our 12th slot) is also safe to add when needed.
close_reason: Research complete. Finding: Next.js App Router bundles all route handlers into the fewest possible Vercel Functions (typically 1). The 12-function limit does not apply per-file to framework projects — it's for raw /api/ files without a framework. Our 11 route files are almost certainly 1-3 actual Vercel Functions. No structural changes needed.

title: Pre-create subagent execution beads for delegated work
description: Agent-ops improvement: when spawning subagents for bounded investigations or verification passes, create/assign a dedicated bead up front so the worker can write notes directly instead of spending time searching existing beads or creating a new one mid-task. Record the recommended pattern in notes and route future delegated work through that bead-first step.
notes:
Recommended delegated-work pattern: before spawning a subagent for bounded work, pre-create or select the target bead, assign/claim it as appropriate, and include the bead ID in the delegated prompt. Tell the subagent explicitly: this is your bead; write all findings and updates to this bead; do not search for other beads; do not create another bead unless explicitly instructed. Acceptance criteria: (1) workflow guidance is recorded in durable memory, (2) bead note documents the exact prompt pattern, (3) future subagent prompts can reuse the wording directly.
Recommended delegated-work pattern: pre-create or select the target bead before spawning a subagent, then include the bead ID in the delegated prompt and tell the worker explicitly: this is your bead; write all findings and updates to this bead; do not search for other beads; do not create another bead unless explicitly instructed.

title: Explore custom issue types and browser_verify enforcement
description: User wants to explore: 1) What custom types make sense for this project (verification, agent-message, etc). 2) Whether types.custom can be used to enforce browser_verify close criteria. 3) Whether bd mail / on_close hooks could be used for cross-agent notification when a bead closes. Review docs/messaging.md in PT_Backup beads clone — bd mail is a first-class feature using BEADS_ACTOR identity. Hook system (on_create, on_update, on_close) receives JSON on stdin — could be used to enforce status rules at close time. Explore with user before implementing anything.
notes:
Docs to read next session:
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/CONFIG.md — types.custom section (custom issue types)
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md — bd types command, custom types examples
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/messaging.md — bd mail setup (already read, notes below)
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/PLUGIN.md — on_close hook details for enforcement

bd mail setup questions to investigate:
- messaging.md says bd mail delegates to an external provider via BEADS_MAIL_DELEGATE env var (e.g. 'gt mail' in an orchestrator context)
- 'gt mail' appears to be Gas Town / orchestrator-specific — may not apply here
- Need to check: is there a standalone bd mail setup that works without an orchestrator?
- Or can we use on_close hook directly for cross-agent notification instead of bd mail?
- BEADS_ACTOR env var sets sender identity (replaces deprecated BD_ACTOR)
- Check if bd mail works standalone: bd mail inbox / bd mail send — try with just BEADS_ACTOR set
bd mail requires Gas Town orchestrator — too much overhead, not applicable here. Cross-agent notification path: on_close hook writes bd note to other agent's AMC bead directly. Zero external dependencies. Pursue this instead of bd mail.
claude-handoff = no. Claude Code desktop only, not CLI, and doesn't help Codex anyway. beads-compound = no, Codex not supported. BeadHub = worth investigating for inter-agent messaging — agent agnostic bd wrapper, has async mail + sync chat. Check version compatibility with bd 0.62.0 first. Primary path remains on_close hook (fires for both agents). Secondary: BeadHub if version-compatible.
Community tools investigation complete.

WORTH PURSUING:
- BeadHub (bdh) — explicit Claude Code + Codex support. File locking prevents agent collisions on shared files. Async mail works for both. Codex limitation: cannot self-notify, human must prompt. Docker or hosted at beadhub.ai (free for OSS). Check 0.62.0 compat.
- Foolery — dispatches + monitors both agents from one UI. Supports Claude Code + Codex CLI explicitly. Routes task types per agent. Local server, Node.js only. v0.4.0 (2026-03-11), actively maintained.
- beads-web — human Kanban visibility layer. Windows x64 binary. Dolt-aware. beads-ui is lighter alternative (npm install -g).

RULED OUT: claude-handoff (Claude only), claude-protocol (hooks Claude only), beads-sdk (dev lib), lavra/beads-compound (no Codex).

CUSTOM COMMAND IDEAS from reviewing these tools:
1. /session-start — read + close previous AMC bead, create new one, run bd ready filtered by assignee. Replaces manual session startup ritual.
2. /session-end — bd list in_progress sweep, close done beads, final bd note on AMC bead, git push, bd dolt push. Replaces manual landing-the-plane.
3. /handoff — write structured context note to other agent's AMC bead via bd note. Lightweight version of claude-handoff without the file complexity.
4. /verify-queue — list all browser_verify beads assigned to claude, open preview URL. One command to start a verification pass.
5. on_close hook — check if closing bead was browser_verify, require verification evidence in close reason (enforcement not just convention).
6. file-lock pattern from BeadHub — before touching a shared file, check if other agent has it claimed. Could be a pre-coding step in AGENTS.md even without BeadHub installed.
BeadHub clarification: does NOT replace beads. bdh is a transparent wrapper over bd — calls bd commands under the hood. Existing beads database, Dolt sync, all beads untouched. Additive only. User runs Docker so server requirement is not a blocker.

FINAL INVESTIGATION CRITERIA before adopting BeadHub or Foolery:
1. Resource usage — how much CPU/RAM does the server consume at idle and under load? (Serena was rejected for this reason — too heavy)
2. MCP or not? — if it adds an MCP server, that means +++ tokens per tool call. Need to know if bdh/Foolery use MCP or pure CLI.
3. Token impact — does it add tokens to every message (like MCP system prompts do), or is it CLI-only (zero token overhead when not actively called)? Net token cost vs. any savings from better coordination.

Serena precedent: rejected because resource/token overhead wasn't worth the benefit. Apply same bar here.
CRITICAL resource bar: Serena caused a full machine lockup — not just slow, machine became unusable. User's laptop is older. This is a hard requirement, not a preference. BeadHub/Foolery must be confirmed lightweight before any recommendation to install.

title: Consolidate icon.svg to _document.js (remove per-page Head references)
description:
icon.svg is referenced in pages/index.js, pages/program.js, pages/pt-view.js, and pages/rehab.js via individual <Head> tags. In Next.js, the correct pattern is one global <link rel='icon' href='/icons/icon.svg'> in _document.js.

Action:
1. Add <link rel='icon' href='/icons/icon.svg'> to _document.js (create _document.js if it doesn't exist)
2. Remove the per-page icon.svg <link> from index.js, program.js, pt-view.js, rehab.js

References in public/manifest-tracker.json, public/manifest.json, and public/sw.js are correct browser-served asset references — leave those alone.

This is low risk and makes icon handling consistent for any new pages added during migration.

File scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/_document.js (create or edit)
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/rehab.js
notes: 2026-03-22 implemented in commit 283974f on nextjs: added pages/_document.js to own the global favicon/apple-touch-icon links and removed the per-page icon.svg Head references from index.js, pt-view.js, and rehab.js. This consolidates icon ownership for the Next.js surface.

title: Claude pass: revise assignment rules for discovered work
description: Source Reference: child of pt-am6 created from tracker hygiene discussion on 2026-03-17. Produce an independent Claude proposal for how Beads assignments should work when agents discover new issues. Problem: the discovering agent often tags the issue to itself, which means the other agent may never even see it while looking for work. Review the current workflow and propose concrete rules for: when discovered issues should stay assigned to the finder, when they should be left unassigned, when they should move into a shared pool, and whether a third shared/unassigned category should be used for work either Codex or Claude can do. Focus on practical rules that improve visibility without creating chaos.
notes: Policy decided: (1) Discovered issues should NOT be auto-assigned to the finder. (2) If work clearly fits one agent → assign + label agent:codex or agent:claude. (3) If either agent can do it → leave unassigned + label agent:shared. This surfaces in bd ready for both agents. (4) Claiming is explicit: bd update <id> --claim when an agent starts the work. (5) The agent:shared label already exists in BEADS_TEMPLATE.md — no new infrastructure needed.
close_reason: Policy defined: unassigned+agent:shared for either-agent work; explicit claim before starting.

title: SW update: bump cache version + skipWaiting so Refresh picks up new app code
description: When a new deploy lands, users on the PWA continue to get old app code from the service worker cache until they close all tabs. Fix: bump the cache version string in sw.js to invalidate old cache, add skipWaiting() so new SW activates immediately, and listen for controllerchange on the client to call window.location.reload(). This makes the Refresh button (or any next page load) pick up new code automatically. Separate from pt-4id (which just restored the Refresh button to the hamburger).
close_reason: Done: CACHE_NAME bumped to v14, STATIC_ASSETS cleaned of all deleted legacy paths, fetch handler guarded to GET-only before cache.put, getNavigationFallback simplified to Next.js routes only. Commit 23d1a3b on nextjs branch.

title: Write pt-rebuild/README.md — honest codebase reference
description:
Create pt-rebuild/README.md as the canonical landing doc for the project. It must honestly document both structures that coexist in this folder: the legacy vanilla JS app and the in-progress Next.js migration.

## Sections to include

### What This Is
One paragraph: PT tracker app for physical therapy session logging. Two users (admin/therapist). Deployed on Vercel + Supabase.

### Two Structures in This Folder
Honest explanation: legacy vanilla JS pages live in public/ and api/; Next.js migration lives in pages/, components/, hooks/. Both are active. The migration is incremental (strangler fig) — old HTML pages are retired one at a time as Next.js versions are verified. Branch: nextjs. See docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md for migration status.

### Running Locally
npm run dev → http://localhost:3000

### Folder Structure
Annotated tree of pt-rebuild/ — what each top-level folder and key file is for. Cover: pages/, components/, hooks/, lib/, api/, public/, styles/, supabase/, docs/. Do NOT invent structure — read the actual filesystem.

### Shared Components (components/)
Registry pulled from the Shared Architecture section of docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md. For each component: file name, what it does, when to use it, basic usage. Components to document: NavMenu.js, AuthForm.js, NativeSelect.js, ExercisePicker.js, SessionLoggerModal.js (and any others found in components/).

### Shared Utilities (lib/)
Same treatment. Lib files to document: supabase.js, text-format.js, rehab-coverage.js, and any others found in lib/ that are Next.js-layer files. Do NOT document lib/auth.js or lib/db.js here — those are legacy API-layer files, note them separately as 'Legacy API layer (do not use in Next.js pages)'.

### Shared Hooks (hooks/)
Same treatment. useAuth.js, and any others in hooks/.

### Key Docs
Bullet list pointing to: docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md (migration status + decisions), docs/NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md (authoring rules for new files), docs/AGENTS.md (workflow rules for agents), docs/BEADS_TEMPLATE.md (issue tracker template).

### Deployment
Vercel project: pt-rehab. Production: https://pttracker.app. Preview (nextjs branch): https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Supabase project: zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz.

## After writing README.md
- In docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md: replace the Shared Architecture section body with 'See pt-rebuild/README.md — Shared Components and Shared Utilities sections.' Keep the section heading so the doc structure is intact.
- Commit with a Beads ID in parentheses: (pt-t4l)

## Rules
- Document what EXISTS, not what is planned
- Do not copy planned/future sections from migration docs
- Read the actual filesystem before writing the folder structure section
- Keep it factual and navigable — this is a reference, not a tutorial
notes:
-
Closed after adding pt-rebuild/README.md, replacing the duplicated Shared Architecture body in docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md with a README pointer, and pushing commit f2418d7 to origin/nextjs. Timer/audio wiring ownership and current legacy-to-Next.js page mappings are now documented in the README.

title: Allow multiple clinicians to access one patient record
description:
## Summary
Consider allowing more than one clinician to log in and access the same patient record.

## Why
The app currently assumes a single therapist relationship through users.therapist_id, but the user would like the option for multiple clinicians such as a PT assistant and chiropractor to view the same patient data.

## Scope
- Evaluate a many-to-many clinician-to-patient access model such as patient_care_team or user_patient_access
- Identify required auth and RLS changes for shared clinician access
- Identify helper and UI assumptions that currently depend on one therapist per patient
- Keep this separate from the current patient-scoped status change unless scope is explicitly expanded

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A future refactor path is captured in Beads under the features epic
- [ ] The bead notes that current users.therapist_id assumptions would need structural changes
- [ ] The bead remains clearly separate from the current patient-program status work

## Notes
Likely touch points later include users schema, route auth/RLS, user-context helpers, and any UI that assumes one therapist owns one patient.

title: Allow multiple clinicians to access one patient record
description: Why: the app currently assumes a single therapist relationship through users.therapist_id, but the user wants to consider allowing multiple clinicians (for example a PT assistant and chiropractor) to log in and view the same patient data. What: evaluate and, if approved later, design a many-to-many access model such as a patient_care_team or user_patient_access join table; update auth/RLS and user-context helpers so more than one clinician can access the same patient. Keep this separate from the current patient-scoped status cleanup unless explicitly expanded.

title: Code review: lib/program-page-data.js + hooks/useProgramPageData.js + pt-x31ya + pt-3l0r5
description: Review findings for Claude clean files and bead decisions.
notes:
## Review findings: lib/program-page-data.js, hooks/useProgramPageData.js, pt-x31ya, pt-3l0r5

---

### lib/program-page-data.js + hooks/useProgramPageData.js — Clean

**What changed:**
- `buildAccessErrorState(currentUser)` added to lib/program-page-data.js (8 lines, pure function).
- `buildCachedProgramSnapshot` in lib now calls `buildAccessErrorState` instead of the inline object literal it previously owned.
- `hooks/useProgramPageData.js` now imports and calls `buildAccessErrorState` instead of its own inline object literal.

**Result:** The access-error state shape and the error message string (`'Therapist or admin access required.'`) are now defined once in a single pure function in the correct lib layer. Both the cached-bootstrap path and the live-refresh path now use the same builder.

No findings. The extraction is minimal, correctly scoped, and correctly placed. The review bead (pt-n4hj4) identified exactly this extraction and nothing more. The implementation matches.

**Approval verdict: Ready for approval.**

---

### pt-x31ya (hooks/useMessages.js) — No code change, bead closed

**Outcome:** Bead closed as "no extraction justified." No code was changed.

**Finding: INFO — Close reason contradicts the bead's own revised notes**

The bead notes contain two rounds of findings. The final revised note explicitly says: "This file does not fully adhere to SRP/SOC" and recommends extracting sync lifecycle from unread-state policy. The close reason says the opposite: "No extraction justified. refresh updates messages+unreadCount atomically — splitting would cause a badge flash."

The close reasoning (atomicity concern causing badge flash) is a valid technical argument that the revised bead notes do not address. It may be correct. But the contradiction between the close reason and the bead's own final revised note is not reconciled in writing anywhere. A future agent picking up this file will see conflicting signals in the bead history.

**Per scope rules this review cannot read the unchanged useMessages.js file**, so the correctness of the badge-flash argument cannot be independently verified here. The close decision is defensible but the reconciliation gap in the bead record is a documentation risk.

---

### pt-3l0r5 (hooks/useManualLog.js) — No code change, bead closed

**Outcome:** Bead closed as "subagent review was correct, summary bead revision was overly aggressive." No code was changed.

The bead notes also contain conflicting rounds — first review says keep as-is (high confidence), revised note says extract submit-side tracker handoff (high confidence). The close reason resolves in favor of the first review and gives a clear rationale: all pure state shaping already lives in lib/manual-log-state.js; the hook owns exactly one concern.

**This reconciliation is documented clearly.** No finding beyond noting this is a judgment call that should be verified against the actual file if contested. Per scope rules that verification is outside this review.

---

### Open questions
- useMessages.js: the badge-flash atomicity concern should be verified by reading the file before the bead is treated as permanently resolved. The current close state is plausible but unverified.
- useManualLog.js: accepted on the strength of the close reason. Would benefit from a one-line note in the file header documenting the submit-handoff boundary as intentionally co-located.

title: Code review: clean files — queue split and picker split
description: Review finding summary for all clean Codex files: useProgramOfflineQueue, useProgramQueueSync, useSessionLogging, lib/session-log-draft, ExercisePicker, useExercisePickerModel, useExercisePickerManualReorder, useExercisePickerActiveDrag, useExercisePickerDragDomEffects.
notes:
## Review findings: clean files

All files below passed review with no significant findings.

---

### hooks/useProgramOfflineQueue.js
Responsibility: queue state, persistence, hydration, and public API wiring.
Result: Clean. 105 lines. Correctly delegates sync lifecycle to useProgramQueueSync and enqueue actions to useProgramMutationQueueActions. Public API is stable.

### hooks/useProgramQueueSync.js
Responsibility: replay timing, reconnect listeners, sync locking.
Result: Clean. 120 lines. navigator.onLine at line 88 is inherited from original — not a new violation. Both effects (auto-replay on queue length change and online listener) are correctly scoped. claimSyncLock/releaseSyncLock correctly exposed for the action hook to use.

### hooks/useSessionLogging.js
Responsibility: session log modal draft orchestration (open/close, create/edit state).
Result: Clean. 131 lines. Correctly delegates to lib/session-log-draft for pure mutations and to useSessionLogSubmission/useSeedSetLogging for transport. The submit closure at line 127 correctly captures current draft state.

### lib/session-log-draft.js
Responsibility: pure draft-state shaping for session log modal.
Result: Clean. 80 lines. Pure functions, no React, no side effects. Correct layer. Imports from lib/session-logging for createDefaultSet/normalizeSet, which is appropriate.

### components/ExercisePicker.js
Responsibility: render-only picker UI for search controls, exercise list, and drag ghost.
Result: Clean. 195 lines. Now accepts pickerModel prop and is truly render-only. ExercisePickerCard is co-located as a memo'd sub-component — the bead review explicitly noted this extraction as optional, so no finding.

### hooks/useExercisePickerModel.js
Responsibility: picker query state, visible-list derivation, display shaping, drag composition.
Result: Clean. 139 lines. The getAdherence formatter is local — the bead review noted this is acceptable since it is picker-specific badge copy. Model correctly delegates drag to useExercisePickerManualReorder.

### hooks/useExercisePickerManualReorder.js
Responsibility: hold-to-activate reorder orchestration.
Result: Clean. 120 lines. One clear responsibility. Correctly separates pending hold state from active drag state.

### hooks/useExercisePickerActiveDrag.js
Responsibility: active drag motion, preview ordering, pointer capture.
Result: Clean. 150 lines. releasePointerCapture(dragTargetRef.current, null) at activateDrag line 57 is intentional (pointerId=null is a no-op in the helper) — this drops any prior capture before setting a new one.

### hooks/useExercisePickerDragDomEffects.js
Responsibility: active-drag DOM listeners, touch-lock, and cleanup.
Result: Clean. 40 lines. Single focused effect. Correct cleanup on dragState null.

---

### Docs updates
README.md: Updated with all new file entries. Ownership descriptions are accurate and match the code.
docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md: Updated with correct use/avoid patterns for all three split areas.
docs/README.md: Minor description update. Accurate.

### Approval verdict
All files in this group are ready for approval.
Final outcome: the clean-review file set shipped in commit 01afd06 on branch nextjs, and the leftover docs index note for docs/README.md shipped in follow-up commit dd66747 under bead pt-bykxt.
close_reason: Clean review group documented, implemented, committed, and pushed

title: Migrate remaining legacy tracker localStorage state to shared offline storage patterns
description:
Source Reference: implementation-patterns violation sweep 2026-03-20
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: high
Scope: legacy
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: legacy,tracker,offline,storage,pattern
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html
- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js

Issue:
Legacy static tracker surfaces still keep important app state in page-local localStorage paths instead of the approved shared offline-storage helpers. This includes the tracker offline queue/sync flow in public/index.html, message/UI state in public/pt_view.html, and editor auth bootstrap storage in public/js/pt_editor.js.

Context:
Discovered during the implementation-patterns violation sweep after IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md was added. The tracker queue persistence is especially important because the current implementation-patterns binder says app persistence should use the shared IndexedDB-backed/offline-cache path rather than new page-local localStorage ownership.

Required scope:
- Replace or retire remaining page-local tracker queue persistence and sync ownership in public/index.html
- Move remaining pt_view UI/message state storage onto shared offline-cache helpers where appropriate
- Remove editor auth bootstrap localStorage usage in favor of the shared storage path
- Coordinate with existing offline architecture work where these findings overlap broader migration tasks
- Keep IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md accurate if shared storage guidance changes while resolving this work

Constraints/Caveats:
- This issue overlaps existing offline-architecture work; do not duplicate implementation unnecessarily if another active bead already owns a deeper Next.js-side migration slice
- Preserve legacy static functionality during migration
- Avoid introducing any new localStorage persistence

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] No scoped app-state persistence in the legacy tracker/editor surfaces relies on page-local localStorage ownership where a shared storage helper exists
- [ ] Tracker queue ownership is aligned with the approved shared offline-storage direction
- [ ] pt_view UI/message state persistence is aligned with shared helpers where applicable
- [ ] IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md remains accurate if storage guidance changes during the fix

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-pbs
- relates-to: pt-780.10

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if later migration work leaves additional legacy static storage paths outside the shared offline helpers.
notes:
Code references from 2026-03-20 sweep:\n- pt-rebuild/public/index.html:4692 (loadOfflineQueue() reads pt_offline_queue from localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/index.html:4784 (saveOfflineQueue() writes queue back to localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/index.html:4792 (syncOfflineQueue() remains page-owned sync path)\n- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:1109-1113 (lastReadMessageTime, dismissedNotes, 
otesCollapsed, lastSentMessageTime,  iltersExpanded all bootstrap from localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:1521 (
otesCollapsed persisted to localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:2226,2231 (message timestamps persisted to localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:60,74 (editor auth bootstrap reads/removes pt_editor_auth from localStorage)\nOverlap note: broader offline-architecture migration is tracked in pt-780.10; this issue is the implementation-pattern/storage-drift slice.
Quick fix map:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html:4692 - loadOfflineQueue reads localStorage
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html:4784 - saveOfflineQueue writes localStorage
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html:4792 - syncOfflineQueue stays page-owned
- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:1109-1113 - UI/message state bootstraps from localStorage
- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:1521 - notesCollapsed persisted to localStorage
- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:2226,2231 - message timestamps persisted to localStorage
- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:60,74 - editor auth bootstrap localStorage usage
Overlap:
- Coordinate with pt-780.10 for broader offline architecture migration
2026-03-20 scope review update:
- IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md now scopes shared storage guidance primarily to maintained current codepaths.
- Legacy static localStorage usage should be treated as migration/modernization work coordinated with broader offline architecture beads, not as a blanket same-day defect label by itself.
- Keep this issue focused on migration cleanup and overlap management with pt-780.10.
2026-03-20 scope decision:
- Legacy static tracker/editor storage cleanup is out of scope for this conversation.
- public/index.html is frozen and should not be edited here.
- Keep this as backlog/reference only unless the user explicitly reopens legacy migration cleanup.
close_reason: Closed under current policy: static legacy surface is frozen unless work is user-approved, security-related, or explicit migration/parity work.

title: Fix legacy PT editor implementation-pattern violations
description:
Source Reference: implementation-patterns violation sweep 2026-03-20
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: legacy
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: legacy,editor,pattern,vocab,auth
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/pt_editor.html
- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/vocab-options.js
- pt-rebuild/components/NativeSelect.js

Issue:
The legacy PT editor still violates several approved implementation patterns: it hardcodes a vocabulary-backed contribution dropdown, hand-rolls repeated select-plus-Other behavior instead of using the shared NativeSelect pattern, bypasses lib/vocab-options.js for vocab label normalization, and still persists editor auth bootstrap data in localStorage.

Context:
Discovered during the implementation-patterns violation sweep after the new binder was added. These findings all belong to the same legacy editor surface and should be fixed together so vocab/select/auth behavior can be evaluated coherently.

Required scope:
- Replace inline hardcoded contribution options with shared vocab-backed data
- Consolidate select-plus-Other behavior onto the approved shared pattern where practical
- Route vocab option label building through lib/vocab-options.js instead of repeated manual mapping
- Remove remaining page-local editor auth persistence that should use the shared storage path
- Keep IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md accurate if the approved legacy-compatible pattern needs clarification

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve legacy editor behavior while aligning it with shared helpers/patterns
- Validate migration risk before replacing deeply embedded legacy markup flows
- Avoid introducing new localStorage persistence

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Contribution options come from shared vocab-backed data rather than inline hardcoded values
- [ ] Repeated select-plus-Other flows are consolidated onto the approved pattern where feasible
- [ ] Vocab-backed option labels use lib/vocab-options.js normalization
- [ ] Editor auth bootstrap no longer relies on localStorage
- [ ] IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md remains accurate if guidance changes during the fix

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-pbs

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if later legacy-editor work reintroduces inline vocab/select/auth patterns that bypass the shared helpers.
notes:
Code references from 2026-03-20 sweep:\n- pt-rebuild/public/pt_editor.html:793 (
ewRoleContribution hardcodes high / medium / low)\n- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:496,509,521,533,545 (manual vocab { code, definition } mapping instead of lib/vocab-options.js)\n- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:587,607,879,909,939,969,999 (repeated select-plus-__other__ branching instead of shared NativeSelect pattern)\n- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:1206,1952 (role dropdown builders bypass lib/vocab-options.js and render raw codes)\n- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:60,74 (editor auth bootstrap still reads/removes pt_editor_auth from localStorage)
Quick fix map:
- pt-rebuild/public/pt_editor.html:793 - hardcoded contribution select values
- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:496,509,521,533,545 - manual vocab label mapping
- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:587,607,879,909,939,969,999 - repeated select-plus-Other branching
- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:1206,1952 - role dropdown builders bypass shared vocab label helper
- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:60,74 - editor auth bootstrap localStorage usage
2026-03-20 scope review update:
- IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md now states that current shared patterns are the default for maintained React/Next.js codepaths.
- Legacy static pages may preserve existing patterns unless the work is already migrating that surface or extracting a shared helper intentionally.
- Treat the NativeSelect and direct lib/vocab-options.js parts of this issue as modernization opportunities for the legacy editor, not automatic defects by themselves.
- The stronger remaining items here are hardcoded extendable domain options and legacy auth storage handling.
2026-03-20 scope decision:
- Legacy static surfaces are frozen for this conversation and should not be edited.
- Treat this issue as backlog/reference only unless the user explicitly reopens legacy-editor modernization.
- Do not start implementation from this bead under the current scope.
close_reason: Closed under current policy: static legacy surface is frozen unless work is user-approved, security-related, or explicit migration/parity work.

title: Duration timer completion cue parity
description:
Source Reference: static logger trace from pt-rebuild/public/index.html on 2026-03-17
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,logging,audio,speech,duration-timer
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js

Issue:
Match static duration-timer completion behavior exactly. Duration timers use countdown warnings, then at timer end play the triple completion beep and speak Set complete only. They do not use rep-left milestone phrases.

Static Source Anchors:
- initTimerMode(): public/index.html around lines 2708-2718
- startTimer(): public/index.html around lines 2804-2812
- playCompletionSound(): public/index.html around lines 2894-2903

Static Rules:
- Duration mode uses currentExercise.seconds_per_set or fallback currentExercise.seconds_per_rep
- Duration timer reaches zero, plays completion triple beep, speaks Set complete, and keeps elapsedMs at target time
- Duration mode does not speak 5 reps left, 3 reps left, or Last rep

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve single-run duration semantics from ptrebuild-spg.3
- Do not close until linked parity beads ptrebuild-spg.3 and ptrebuild-spg.10 are verified or closed

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Duration timers play 3/2/1 countdown warnings
- [ ] Duration timer completion plays triple completion beep
- [ ] Duration timer completion speaks Set complete only
- [ ] No hold-style rep-left announcements occur in duration mode
notes: 2026-03-22 tracker cleanup: this item stays open as a deferred enhancement, not as a parity blocker. The user-approved direction is richer duration completion phrasing (Set X of Y complete and side-aware wording), which intentionally diverges from the original static duration speech. It should not keep pt-9v1 or the timer/audio parity slice open.
close_reason: Closed per operator confirmation: duration timer completion cue item is done.

value: Codex caused severe Windows network damage 2026-04-20 by recommending netsh winsock reset on Dell Inspiron 3593 with Qualcomm QCA9377 WiFi. Result: WiFi completely dead (ms_nativewifi unbound from NDIS), Ethernet TCP stack broken (socket family error on ipconfig /renew), machine completely offline. Recovery required: physically buying ethernet cable, plugging into router, downloading Dell OEM Qualcomm driver YX23Y v12.0.0.1118 A47, reinstall as admin, reboot. WiFi restored but system has had lingering instability since: Dolt wsasend TCP aborts resurfacing (was stable for weeks before), Zoom dropping 7x in 1hr session. DISM CheckHealth found component store repairable. RestoreHealth in progress 2026-04-24. Dolt instability is NOT a version issue - same version was stable before winsock reset. NativeWiFi filter now shows correctly bound. Do NOT run any netsh/netcfg/winsock commands on this machine without extensive research first.

Before retrying a failed commit, run npm run commit:preflight -- --message "Your title (pt-xxxx)" --trailer "Co-Authored-By: Agent Name <recognized-agent-email>" so README, Biome, structure, gitleaks, and commit-message blockers surface together. Use a commit title with at least one Beads ID in parentheses. Recognized agent emails for the trailer are codex@openai.com and noreply@anthropic.com; if no agent attribution should be recorded for that commit, use the one-time PT_AI_TRAILER_OK override instead.
Commit ownership rule: stage and commit only files you changed in this session unless the user explicitly approves taking ownership of pre-existing dirty files.
Never prepend cd to bash commands in pttracker. Working directory is already set. cd doesn't persist between tool calls — it's pure token waste.

PT Tracker Beads essentials: claim before work; create discovered-from beads immediately for new findings; close done beads before commit; close verification beads when they pass; end sessions with bead state updated plus git push and bd dolt push.

Before proposing any fix that adds, changes, or defaults data, run run_pipeline to understand what the system already does — what the schema actually allows, what the existing pattern is, how analogous paths behave. Never assume what the DB requires or what a payload needs. The question to ask is: how does the system handle this today, and what does it actually permit? Proposing a fix based on assumptions about constraints costs 2-3 correction cycles. One vexp call first costs nothing.
PT Tracker repo exception: read the actor-specific local memory file plus C:\\Users\\cindi\\OneDrive\\Documents\\PT_Backup\\agent_memory\\MEMORY.md at session start, then run  d prime.
MUST: skip beads for tiny housekeeping-only actions; use beads for real tracked work. MUST: mark a bead in_progress before starting and close it before committing when the scoped work is done.

VEXP patching: use impact_graph/flow at public hook/component/route/shared-helper boundaries for blast radius and explicit paths; if private helpers or callback chains return 0, corroborate with get_context_capsule/get_skeleton before concluding no connection. For exact patch anchors, capsule/skeleton are usually better than expand_vexp_ref.
